title: Text-Only Virtual Currency LLM for Consumer Electronics: Social Sentiment Anomaly Detection and Trading Guidance
authors: Xiaotong Jiang, Zhiyu Zhang, Jun Wu
abstract: Consumer participation in the virtual currency market is increasingly mediated through personal electronics. Social media sentiment plays an important role in this market, where collective sentiment can trigger significant price volatility. Conventional financial models often struggle to capture these dynamics, exposing consumers to potential trading risks. To address this issue, we propose VirInsight. The framework integrates fine-grained sentiment classification, transaction anomaly detection, and trading recommendation. The key novelty lies in the design of an anomaly detection mechanism that is aware of sentiment dynamics and links social media sentiment to consumer trading guidance. For the Vir-Llama model, we develop a five-class sentiment classification scheme. We also construct a new dataset, VirSent, which contains over 12,000 annotated tweets related to major virtual currencies, including Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Binance Coin. Experimental results show that the proposed sentiment-aware approach improves the accuracy of anomaly detection and enables the generation of practical trading recommendations. This research contributes a robust method for modeling sentiment in the virtual currency market and offers a new perspective on consumer behavior. It ultimately paves the way for intelligent financial applications designed to empower and protect the everyday consumer.

title: Self-Enabled Write Assist Cells for High-Density SRAM in Resistance-Dominated Technology Node
authors: Minjune Yeo, Seungjae Yei, Keonhee Cho, Giseok Kim, Mingu Kang, Seong-Ook Jung
abstract: As technology scaling increases interconnect resistance, writeability degradation in static random access memory (SRAM) becomes critical. This article presents a self-enabled write assist cell (SEWAC) that mitigates writeability degradation caused by increased bitline resistance (R<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\mathrm {BL}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) without requiring timing control. The SEWAC has a cell-compatible layout with the standard 6T bitcell and is directly inserted between bitcell rows without white space. SEWAC detects BL voltage transitions and self-activates to provide additional write paths, reducing the BL saturation voltage. Simulation results using a 4:1 bit-interleaved <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$256\times 128$</tex-math> </inline-formula> SRAM macro show that SEWAC achieves the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$6\sigma $</tex-math> </inline-formula> writeability yield target for R<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">BL</sub> per cell (R<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\mathrm {BL\_cell}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$120~\Omega $</tex-math> </inline-formula>. Compared to the write assist cell (W-AC) scheme, SEWAC reduces area overhead by 50% in the bit-interleaved structure while maintaining comparable writeability performance. A 28-nm test chip measurement shows that SEWAC maintains 100% writeability yield up to R<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\mathrm {BL\_cell}} = 240~\Omega $</tex-math> </inline-formula> and reduces the required word-line pulsewidth (WLPW) by 33%. In addition, SEWAC demonstrates stable operation across a wide supply voltage range from 0.67 to 1.2 V, outperforming the conventional structure. The proposed SEWAC scheme also shows robustness to threshold voltage variation of the assist cells, thus making it a suitable solution for high-density, high-reliability SRAM macros in advanced technology nodes.

title: A Pre-Load Compression Using Test Vector Segmentation for High-Speed Memory Testing
authors: Duyeon Won, Juyong Lee, Gyeonggyu Park, Youngki Moon, Sungho Kang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: The Algorithmic Pattern Generator (ALPG) is widely adopted to address massive test vector volumes in memory testing. However, the test rate is limited by the complex arithmetic operations required for real-time vector generation, making it difficult to match the high operating speeds of modern memory. While this test rate bottleneck is mitigated by adopting code-based compression schemes with simple decoding hardware, insufficient compression ratios are obtained for memory test vectors. Therefore, a new Pre-load Compression (PLC) is proposed to achieve high compression ratios and high-speed memory testing simultaneously. High compression efficiency is achieved by segmenting the test vector based on DRAM properties and pre-loading valid care bit fields into the don’t care (X) bit fields generated during mandatory stabilization cycles. Furthermore, high-speed memory testing is enabled by the PLC decoder through a simple mechanism that shifts pre-loaded care-bit data out or holds it to align with the original vector timing. In experimental results, test rates at least 62.58% higher than conventional ALPGs are achieved by the proposed PLC. Moreover, an average compression ratio 25.56% higher than conventional compression methods is achieved for test vectors generated by diverse test patterns and memory specifications.

title: New Constructions of Non-GRS MDS Codes, Recovery and Determination Algorithms for GRS Codes
authors: Guodong Wang, Hongwei Liu, Jinquan Luo
abstract: In this paper, we propose a new method for constructing a class of non-GRS MDS codes. The lengths of these codes can reach up to q+3/2 (for finite fields of odd characteristic) and q+4/2 (for even characteristic), respectively. Owing to their special structure, we can use the Cauchy matrix method to obtain the necessary and sufficient conditions for these codes to be MDS codes and non-GRS MDS codes. Additionally, the inequivalence between these codes and twisted GRS codes is analyzed. Furthermore, we analyze the relationships among several existing classes of codes used for constructing non-GRS MDS codes, propose explicit constructions, and discuss the lengths of non-GRS MDS codes based on these constructions. Finally, we design two efficient algorithms to address two main problems in GRS code research, i.e., determining whether an unknown code C is a GRS code from its generator matrix G, and recovering the key vectors α and v such that C=GRSn,k(α,v) if C is indeed a GRS code. A computational complexity comparison of the proposed algorithms (O(nk+n)) with that of the Sidelnikov-Shestakov attack (exceeding O(qk2n+qk3)) shows that our methods offer superior computational efficiency.

title: Lagrange–Chebyshev Interpolated CNN–LSTM Framework for ECG Arrhythmia Classification
authors: Wandi Yusuf Kurniawan, Putu Harry Gunawan, Norma Binti Alias, Narita Aquarini, Muhammad Ilyas
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Accurate electrocardiogram (ECG) interpretation is crucial for early arrhythmia detection, but it remains hindered by noise and morphological distortion. This study proposes a Lagrange–Chebyshev–based Convolutional Long Short-Term Memory (CNN–LSTM) framework that unifies numerical interpolation with deep temporal learning for robust ECG reconstruction and classification. Signals from the MIT–BIH Arrhythmia Database were denoised, segmented, and reconstructed using Lagrange–Chebyshev interpolation to suppress endpoint oscillations and preserve diagnostic morphology before classification into the AAMI five-class taxonomy. The integrated CNN–LSTM model achieved strong overall performance (accuracy 0.9691, macro F1 0.9315, macro AUC 0.9929), demonstrating reliable discrimination across arrhythmia types with low computational cost. Unlike prior deep or attention-based methods, this framework enhances ECG interpretability by coupling mathematically grounded waveform reconstruction with data-driven temporal modeling, improving both morphological fidelity and model generalization. The approach is compact enough for near-real-time inference, highlighting its potential for embedded or wearable diagnostic applications. This framework thus establishes a mathematically interpretable and scalable foundation for future real-time arrhythmia detection systems.

title: A Systematic Literature Review of Semi-Supervised Learning on Time-Series Data
authors: Fahris Mumtaza Ahsan, Igi Ardiyanto, Sunu Wibirama
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Time-series data are widely utilized in fields such as healthcare, industry, finance, and energy, yet their effective analysis remains constrained by the scarcity of labeled data. Semi-supervised learning (SSL) has emerged as a promising paradigm that integrates limited labeled data with abundant un-labeled samples to enhance model generalization and robustness. This study presents a comprehensive systematic literature review (SLR) of 93 peer-reviewed studies published between 2021 and 2024, focusing on SSL methodologies, application domains, and evaluation strategies in time-series analysis. The review identifies pseudo-labeling as the most prevalent approach due to its simplicity and flexibility, while recent research trends highlight the growing adoption of advanced methods such as contrastive learning, graph-based models, generative adversarial networks (GANs), and ensemble frameworks including MixMatch, Fix-Match, and SelfMatch. These approaches demonstrate improved capability in handling temporal dependencies, missing data, and noisy observations. SSL has been successfully applied across diverse domains, including medical diagnosis, industrial fault detection, energy forecasting, and remote sensing. Accuracy remains the dominant evaluation metric, but complementary measures such as F1-score, recall, AUC, and regression-based errors are increasingly employed for fairer assessments. Overall, SSL exhibits strong potential to enhance time-series modeling under limited supervision, with current trends moving toward hybrid, domain-adapted, and interpretable frameworks that bridge methodological rigor and real-world applicability.

title: Predicting Live Commerce Conversion in Indonesia with Machine Learning
authors: Sabar Aritonang Rajagukguk, Timothy Dillan, Ady Ekayana Putra
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Live commerce, or shoppertainment, is rapidly transforming digital retail in Indonesia, yet conversion outcomes remain difficult to predict. This study develops a machine learning framework that integrates behavioral analytics to model conversion drivers in Indonesian live commerce sessions. Using a large-scale dataset of 1.2 million interactions, we extract behavioral features such as comment velocity, flash sale frequency, and host responsiveness. Among tested models, XGBoost achieved the strongest performance (AUC = 0.924, accuracy = 89.3%), significantly outperforming baselines. Feature interpretability analysis with SHAP values highlights the central role of interaction intensity and promotional strategies. Real-world validation with 23 SME partners confirmed the model’s practical utility, yielding an average 34.7% increase in conversion rates and a 1,254% ROI. The findings demonstrate the value of behavioral-level prediction for optimizing live commerce strategies and provide a foundation for future research integrating multimodal and cross-cultural perspectives.

title: Boundary-Aware Collaborative Mixture-of-Experts Architecture for Fetal Brain Anatomical Segmentation
authors: Jintang Li, Zhan Gao, Guanyuan Chen, Qika Lin, Chunlian Wang, Kenli Li
abstract: Accurate segmentation of fetal brain anatomical structures is crucial for prenatal disease screening and diagnosis. However, ultrasound images often suffer from noise, artifacts, and blurred boundaries, making manual delineation labor-intensive and prone to variability. Lightweight automated methods struggle with these challenges, while more complex algorithms are difficult to deploy on resource-constrained portable ultrasound devices and consumer-grade medical electronics. To address these issues, we propose the Boundary-aware Collaborative Mixture-of-Experts (BAC-MoE) framework. This approach dynamically adjusts the number of experts to optimize performance for resource-limited environments. It integrates frequency-domain filtering to enhance boundaries and textures, and spatial inverse attention to focus on small-scale, blurred structures. A collaborative learning-driven hybrid feature fusion strategy is designed to efficiently integrate complementary information from different expert branches, ensuring robustness while maintaining computational efficiency. Experiments on a fetal brain ultrasound dataset show that BAC-MoE significantly outperforms existing methods in segmenting tiny structures, achieving an optimal balance between accuracy and efficiency. These results demonstrate the potential of BAC-MoE for both clinical applications and deployment on consumer-grade ultrasound devices. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/201610006/BAC-MoE.

title: RelightFlow: An Inversion-Free Video Relighting Model via Dual-Trajectory Diffusion Editing_supp1-3683103.mp4
authors: Longguang Wang
abstract: Video relighting is a fundamental task with wide-ranging applications in contemporary visual computing, including film production, immersive virtual reality, augmented reality, and interactive digital worlds. Its objective is to generate temporally stable lighting effects while preserving the structural integrity, visual appearance, and intrinsic physical properties of objects in the source video. Recent studies combined image relighting models with video diffusion models, achieving notable progress in training-free video relighting. However, these methods rely on mapping noisy latents back to the pixel space during the relighting process, leading to degraded fidelity, consistency, and stability. In this work, we propose RelightFlow, a training-free video relighting framework built upon a flow-matching-based video DiT model, which requires neither inversion nor additional training, obtaining more feasible and robust relighting effects. Specifically, we first design a detail-preserving relighting module coupled with an interpolation trajectory, which injects stable lighting cues in the early generation stages while maintaining fine spatial details. Next, we develop a temporally consistent relighting module to form a flow-editing trajectory, leveraging the velocity fields predicted by the video DiT model to enhance temporal coherence. Finally, we introduce a dynamic fusion strategy that adaptively integrates these two trajectories to balance relighting intensity and temporal stability. Extensive experiments demonstrate that RelightFlow achieves high-quality video relighting with stable relighting intensity, superior fidelity, and temporal consistency. The code are provided in https://github.com/Yukun66/RelightFlow.

title: Learning-Based Quadratic Programming for Safe Trajectory Tracking of Quadrotor UAVs in Dynamic Environments
authors: Mingxin Wei, Tao Han, Lanxiang Zheng, Ruidong Mei, Ming-Feng Ge
abstract: In dynamic and cluttered environments, quadrotors face significant challenges in simultaneously achieving precise trajectory tracking while maintaining robust safety guarantees due to time-varying disturbances and complex interactions with obstacles. Traditional control methods often fail to address these dual objectives simultaneously, with model-based approaches struggling to quantify uncertainty, and learning-based methods lacking formal safety assurances. To bridge this gap, this study proposes a learning-based safety-preserving quadratic control (LB-SPQC) framework that integrates adaptive learning with rigorous stability and safety constraints. A Gaussian process (GP)-based disturbance observer is developed, incorporating online hyperparameter optimization to enable real-time uncertainty quantification with probabilistic error bounds. The observer dynamically adapts to varying aerodynamic effects and environmental disturbances, ensuring accurate modeling across diverse flight conditions. In addition, a unified quadratic programming (QP) architecture is proposed, combining robust control Lyapunov functions (CLFs) for exponential stability and high-order control barrier functions (CBFs) for collision avoidance, while accounting for actuator saturation limits. This guarantees both tracking performance and safety. A computationally efficient sparse approximation scheme is implemented to reduce GP complexity through optimized inducing point selection, allowing real-time execution on embedded hardware. The framework is validated through extensive simulations and real-world flight experiments. Results demonstrate superior performance under challenging conditions, including wind disturbances and complex obstacle environments, showcasing LB-SPQC’s ability to provide a reliable and computationally efficient solution for quadrotor operations in dynamic scenarios.

title: SimVidT: Video Transformer Acceleration via Exploiting Spatio-temporal Similarity
authors: Qinyu Wang, Xiaofeng Zou, Ziqian Zeng, Huiping Zhuang, Mingkui Tan, Cen Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Video transformers (VidTs) have demonstrated powerful spatio-temporal modelling capabilities in various video understanding tasks. However, their practical deployment remains constrained by high computational and memory overheads, primarily caused by the quadratic complexity of spatio-temporal self-attention and the intensive demands of processing high-frame-rate video streams. While recent efforts have attempted to leverage spatio-temporal similarity to eliminate redundant computation, they either rely on expensive similarity recognition or support only limited reuse, resulting in limited performance gains. In this work, we present a comprehensive analysis of the potential opportunities and challenges in exploiting spatiotemporal similarity within VidTs. Building upon these insights, we propose SimVidT, a novel VidT accelerator that leverages spatio-temporal similarity to reduce computational redundancy. SimVidT is supported by an algorithm and architecture codesign. At the algorithm level, we introduce a locality-aware similarity identification method and a redundancy-eliminated attention mechanism to efficiently identify and eliminate redundant computations at multiple granularities. At the architecture level, we develop a dedicated accelerator featuring a customized hierarchical memory management unit and specialized compute units, which adapts to the complex execution flow of VidTs and translates redundancy reduction into performance gains. We implement a cycle-accurate simulator for performance evaluation. Experimental results demonstrate that SimVidT achieves 1.7×speedup and 2.5× better energy efficiency on average compared to the state-of-the-art VidT accelerator CMC.

title: T-wave Peak-to-End Changes, Quantified by Time-Warping, Surrogates Ventricular Repolarization Dispersion for Ventricular Fibrillation Prediction in a Porcine Myocardial Infarction Model
authors: Neurys Gómez, Alena S. Abramochkina, Julia Ramírez, Alexey O. Ovechkin, Olesya G. Bernikova, Pyotr G. Platonov, Jan E. Azarov, Pablo Laguna, Juan Pablo Martínez
abstract: Background: Ventricular fibrillation (VF) during acute ischemia is linked to increased dispersion of ventricular repolarization (DOR), which is considered a robust marker of arrhythmic susceptibility. In previous works, we showed that a time-warping-based index quantifying morphological changes from the T-wave peak to the T-wave end over a PCA transform lead, $d^{{\kern0.28436pt}\rm{PCA}}_{w,{\scriptscriptstyle \mathrm{T}_{pe}}}$, outperformed the T-peak-to-T-end interval ($\mathrm{T}_{pe}$) in arrhythmic risk prediction in a closed-chest porcine infarction model. In this study, we compare $d^{{\kern0.28436pt}\rm{PCA}}_{w,{\scriptscriptstyle \mathrm{T}_{pe}}}$ with the DOR measured in an open-chest porcine infarction model, to test the hypothesis that $d^{{\kern0.28436pt}\rm{PCA}}_{w,{\scriptscriptstyle \mathrm{T}_{pe}}}$ is a noninvasive surrogate for DOR, investigating its predictive value for VF occurrence. Methods: Myocardial ischemia was induced by coronary ligation in 55 domestic pigs. Surface ECG and intramyocardial electrograms were recorded simultaneously. DOR was computed as the range of the end-of-repolarization times across intramyocardial electrograms, while $d^{{\kern0.28436pt}\rm{PCA}}_{w,{\scriptscriptstyle \mathrm{T}_{pe}}}$ was extracted from $\mathrm{T}_{pe}$ morphology, measured in a spatially transformed PCA lead and using a warping-based approach. Additionally, the classical $\mathrm{T}_{pe}$ interval was computed as a reference. Indices were compared between pigs developing delayed VF (Delayed-VF group) and those who did not (Non-VF group). Results: $d^{{\kern0.28436pt}\rm{PCA}}_{w,{\scriptscriptstyle \mathrm{T}_{pe}}}$, DOR and $\mathrm{T}_{pe}$ remained stable at baseline but showed progressive changes during ischemia, with larger changes in animals that developed VF. At 5, 10, and 15 minutes after occlusion, ROC analysis showed significant discriminatory capacity, with AUC values consistently $\geq$0.79 for all indexes and an early predictive value with AUC of 0.870 for $d^{{\kern0.28436pt}\rm{PCA}}_{w,{\scriptscriptstyle \mathrm{T}_{pe}}}$ at 5 minutes, accompanied by balanced sensitivity and specificity values. Cox regression for VF risk prediction resulted in hazard ratios ranging from 5.86 to 11.95 across time points. Kaplan-Meier survival curves stratified by ROC-derived thresholds showed significant separation between groups at all three time points. Linear regression analyses between $d^{{\kern0.28436pt}\rm{PCA}}_{w,{\scriptscriptstyle \mathrm{T}_{pe}}}$ and DOR showed consistent correlation in VF-susceptible animals (median ${R^{2}=0.33}$). This association was stronger than that observed between $\mathrm{T}_{pe}$ and DOR (median ${R^{2}=0.16}$). Conclusions: The time-warping-based index, $d^{{\kern0.28436pt}\rm{PCA}}_{w,{\scriptscriptstyle \mathrm{T}_{pe}}}$, is a reliable non-invasive surrogate of DOR, with comparable predictive value for delayed VF risk under ischemic conditions, providing earlier and more robust prediction than the $\mathrm{T}_{pe}$ index. These findings highlight their translational potential and require validation in human cohort studies.

title: Unraveling Public Discourse on Sustainable Fashion: Mapping Critical Themes and Issues Using BERT-based Multi-Label Classification
authors: Syarafina Putri Adzhani Jogaswara, Andry Alamsyah, Dian Puteri Ramadhani
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: The fashion industry is a major contributor to environmental pollution, textile waste, and carbon emissions, yet consumers remain confused by inconsistent sustainability claims, often exacerbated by greenwashing practices. Despite growing awareness of the industry’s environmental, social, and economic impacts, these issues are often fragmented in public discourse, with a dominant focus on environmental concerns while social and economic aspects are frequently overlooked. This gap in understanding hinders informed consumer decision-making, and the rise of fast fashion further exacerbates the environmental and social costs associated with the industry. This study uses a BERT-based multi-label classification model to analyze 20,162 discourse samples from digital platform. The text is categorized into five dimensions of sustainable fashion—social, environmental, economic, cultural, and aesthetic—and five emotional traits—hopeful, frustration, skepticism, concern, and anger. The results reveal that economic sustainability is the most frequently discussed dimension, followed by environmental sustainability, with emotional responses such as anger and concern strongly linked to social and environmental issues. Hopeful emotions were more closely associated with economic and aesthetic dimensions. The BERT model achieved an F1-score of 0.81 for sustainability dimensions and 0.77 for emotional traits. These findings emphasize the need for better transparency in brand communication and more effective strategies for engaging consumers. Future research could expand this analysis with BERT Topic for real-time sentiment tracking to track the evolution of public sentiment on sustainable fashion, offering deeper insights into emerging trends and consumer behavior.

title: A Detail Injection-Based Fusion Framework for Hyperspectral, Multispectral, and Panchromatic Remote Sensing Images
authors: Jian Fang, He Sun, Xu Sun, Li Ni, Lianru Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The primary goal of fusing hyperspectral (HSI), multispectral (MSI), and panchromatic (PAN) images is to transfer spatial details from MSI and PAN into HSI to generate high-resolution hyperspectral images. Nevertheless, most existing methods rely on black-box deep learning architectures and rarely leverage this fundamental principle, resulting in limited physical interpretability. To address this challenge, we introduce a novel Detail Injection-Based Fusion Framework for Hyperspectral, Multispectral, and Panchromatic Imagery (DIM-HMPF). The framework is engineered to enrich the spatial detail of HSI through fusion with MSI and PAN modalities, all while maintaining high-fidelity spectral information. Therefore, we utilize the physical model of detail injection for unfolding, using deep learning to learn this process. We further employ an optimization problem-solving method based on the proximal gradient descent (PGD) algorithm, achieved through a series of iterative steps. By unfolding these iterative steps into several network modules, DIM-HMPF is ultimately obtained. On the representative remote sensing hyperspectral datasets of Chikusei, Houston, and Xiongan, both quantitative evaluation metrics and visual comparison results consistently demonstrate that DIM-HMPF significantly outperforms existing mainstream fusion algorithms in terms of reconstruction accuracy, especially as evidenced by its superior performance in structural preservation and spectral fidelity. These results comprehensively validate the distinct superiority, robustness, and effectiveness of DIM-HMPF within the HMPF framework, as well as its strong generalization capability and practical potential in complex scenarios. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/Freelancefangjian/DIM-HMPF.

title: Phenomenological Modeling and Tracking Control for Photothermal-Driven Liquid Crystal Elastomer Actuator Without Temperature Measurement
authors: Jundong Wu, Xinyu Nie, Yawu Wang, Qingxin Meng, Chun-Yi Su, Daiki Sato
abstract: The photothermal-driven liquid crystal elastomer (PtD-LCE) has extensive potential in soft robot actuators. Existing studies on the PtD-LCE actuator are often conducted in a multistage manner, examining the relationship between input voltage and temperature, and the relationship between temperature and output displacement, simultaneously. This article proposes a single-stage direct control method for the PtD-LCE actuator without temperature measurement by directly investigating the relationship between its input voltage and output displacement. The proposed control method avoids the uncertainties induced by additional temperature measurement and reduces the complexity in control of the PtD-LCE actuator. In the modeling research, a dynamic model is established based on a phenomenological approach, which characterizes the rate-dependent hysteresis of the actuator by combining a hysteresis module and a third-order linear system in series. As for the trajectory tracking control, a feedforward and feedback combined method is proposed, in which the feedforward controller is designed based on the established model to compensate for the actuator’s nonlinear hysteresis and a fuzzy PI feedback controller is employed to eliminate disturbances and uncertainties. Experimental results confirm that the control method presented in this article is effective in tracking various target trajectories for the PtD-LCE actuator without temperature measurement.

title: Modeling AI-Driven Business Intelligence and Firm Performance: A Mediated-Moderated Approach
authors: Caesari Tyas Wimasada, Rajiv Dharma Mangruwa, Han-Foon Neo
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: This study investigates the influence of Artificial Intelligence (AI) adoption on business performance by integrating managerial attitude as a mediating variable and organizational agility as a moderating factor. Data were obtained from 410 employees representing diverse organizations in Indonesia, and the proposed relationships were analyzed using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM). The findings reveal that AI adoption significantly improves business performance both directly and indirectly through managerial attitude, emphasizing the importance of managerial trust, openness, and positive perceptions toward AI in achieving superior outcomes. Conversely, organizational agility does not exhibit a direct positive effect on performance but demonstrates a negative moderating influence, implying that excessive or poorly aligned agility may reduce the effectiveness of AI initiatives due to strategic or coordination complexities. Further analysis using effect size (f<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) and predictive relevance (Q<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) metrics supports the robustness, explanatory capability, and predictive validity of the structural model. The findings advance theoretical understanding by elucidating the psychological and organizational mechanisms linking AI adoption to firm performance. Practically, the study emphasizes the importance of managerial commitment, strategic alignment, and balanced agility to realize AI-driven transformation benefits. Ethical standards, including informed consent and data confidentiality, were strictly maintained. Future studies are encouraged to adopt longitudinal and cross-industry designs to explore the boundary conditions influencing AI-driven business transformation.

title: Data-Driven Koopman Model Predictive Control of Free-Floating Space Robots Under Time Delays and Unknown Dynamics
authors: Saurabh Chaudhary, Niladri S. Tripathy, Suril V. Shah
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Space robots are remotely operated, and the considerable distance from the control station necessitates transmitting control signals over communication networks, which inevitably introduces time delays in both actuator-to-controller and sensor-to-controller channels. Furthermore, accurate modeling of Free-Floating Space Robots (FFSR) is challenging due to unmodeled dynamics and uncertainties, which can degrade control performance. To address these challenges, this work proposes a data-driven Koopman Model Predictive Control framework for FFSR trajectory tracking under communication-induced delays and unknown system dynamics. The proposed approach compensates for delays in both channels without relying on analytical modeling. Comprehensive numerical simulations on a planar FFSR under various time-delay scenarios demonstrate the framework's effectiveness in mitigating time delays and maintaining robust control performance.

title: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning with Attributed Patch Semantic Graph for Automated Patch Correctness Assessment
authors: Zhenyu Yang, Jingwen Wu, Zhen Yang, Zhongxing Yu
abstract: Automated program repair (APR) aims to automatically repair program errors without human intervention, and recent years have witnessed a growing interest on this research topic. While much progress has been made and techniques originating from different disciplines have been proposed, APR techniques generally suffer from the patch overfitting issue, i.e., the generated patches are not genuinely correct despite they pass the employed tests. To alleviate this issue, many research efforts have been devoted for automated patch correctness assessment (APCA). In particular, with the emergence of large language model (LLM) technology, researchers have employed LLM to assess the patch correctness and have obtained the state-of-the-art performance. The literature on APCA has demonstrated the importance of capturing patch semantic and explicitly considering certain code attributes in predicting patch correctness. However, existing LLM-based methods typically treat code as token sequences and ignore the inherent formal structure for code, making it difficult to capture the deep patch semantics. Moreover, these LLM-based methods also do not explicitly account for enough code attributes. To overcome these drawbacks, we in this paper design a novel patch graph representation named attributed patch semantic graph (APSG), which adequately captures the patch semantic and explicitly reflects important patch attributes. To effectively use graph information in APSG, we accordingly propose a new parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) method of LLMs named Graph-LoRA. Our method focuses on the typical real-world scenarios where ground-truth patches are inaccessible, and does not rely on ground-truth patches to work. Extensive evaluations have been conducted to evaluate our method, and the results show that compared to the state-of-the-art methods, our method improves the accuracy and F1 score by 3.1% to 7.5% and 3.0% to 7.1% respectively.

title: Robust Beamforming Optimization for STAR-RIS Empowered Multi-User RSMA Under Hardware Imperfections and Channel Uncertainty
authors: Muhammad Asif, Asim Ihsan, Zhu Shoujin, Ali Ranjha, Xingwang Li, Khaled M. Rabie, Symeon Chatzinotas
abstract: This study investigates the synergy between ratesplitting multiple access (RSMA) and simultaneous transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surface (STAR-RIS) as a unified framework to realize ubiquitous, intelligent, and resilient connectivity in future sixth-generation networks, while enhancing both spectral and energy efficiency. Specifically, in the STAR-RIS-assisted multi-user RSMA network under consideration, we develop an intelligent optimization strategy that jointly designs the active beamforming at the transmitter, the allocated transmission rate for the common stream, and the passive beamforming vectors for both transmission and reflection regions of the STAR-RIS, while accounting for transceiver hardware impairments and imperfect channel state information (CSI). In addition, system robustness is ensured by incorporating a bounded channel estimation error model that rigorously reflects CSI imperfections and ensures resilience against worst-case estimation errors. To tackle the highly non-convex problem, we propose an intelligent optimization algorithm that decouples the original problem into two sub-problems, which are then solved iteratively. Firstly, the active beamforming vectors for both the common and private signals are obtained by reformulating the original non-convex problem into a tractable convex semi-definite programming (SDP) framework, leveraging successive convex approximation (SCA) and semi-definite relaxation (SDR) for enhanced computational efficiency. Secondly, the passive beamforming vectors for the transmission and reflection regions of the STAR-RIS are optimized through a convex SDP reformulation by exploiting SCA and SDR techniques. Additionally, when the resulting active or passive beamforming solutions are of higher rank, Gaussian randomization is employed to construct rank-one solutions. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed optimization strategy is demonstrated through numerical simulations, which reveal significant performance gains over benchmark schemes and confirm rapid convergence.

title: Auto Lumina: Intelligent Adaptive Headlamp and Turn Signal Automation for Driver Assistance
authors: Vignesh R, Dev Adhithya M, Navneeth Krishnan, Vijendra Babu D
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: This article deals with a smart headlight control system that improves road safety by dynamically changing beam intensity and direction based on ambient light, oncoming vehicle glare and lane detection in real time. It uses Light Dependent Resistors to measure ambient light and sense oncoming car glare and automatically switch between high and low beam. A pantilt servo system aligns headlight direction based on steering angle input for better cornering visibility. A YOLOv3 based lane analysis module takes live camera input, processes it into blobs, detects vehicles and estimates distance to determine if a lane change is safe. The decision logic is programmed in an ESP32 and relays the result to an Arduino Nano that controls the headlight. The system consumes low power by only engaging beam control when glare or low light condition is present. The modular design is cost effective compared to expensive adaptive lighting system and can be integrated with other ADAS features in the future. By combining deep learning, embedded hardware and real time vehicle monitoring, this system greatly improves night time driving safety and comfort and is a good candidate for retrofitting in non-ADAS vehicles. The prototype results in an automated mode with acceptable performance by using low-cost sensors and embedded computer technology with 3W power consumption and 80-82

title: Dysarthria Detection Based on Voice Recordings using Multiscale CNN Architecture with Temporal Pyramid Pooling
authors: Nico Arya Divano, Yuita Arum Sari, Sigit Adinugroho
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Dysarthria is a neurological speech disorder commonly observed in patients with multiple sclerosis, Parkinson’s disease, and stroke. Traditional diagnostic procedures rely on sebjective and time-consuming clinical evaluations, which motivates the need for automated and reliable assessment methods. however, conventional Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) face a fundamental limitation when processing speech recordings of varying durations, often requiring truncation or padding that leads to loss of temporal information and reduced classification performance. This study proposes a robust dysarthria detection framework based on mel-spectrogram features for binary classification, Dysarthria and Control, using the TORGO dataset. The audio signals are converted into mel-spectrogram representations, then fed into a Multiscale CNN architecture. The Multiscale CNN employs parallel convolutional branches with different receptive fields to enrich multiresolution feature extraction, while a Temporal Pyramid Pooling (TPP) layer transforms variable-length temporal features into fixed-dimensional representations, enabling the model to effectively handle recordings of diverse durations. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed Multiscale CNN-TPP model effectively overcomes the variable-length speech problem and significantly outperforms a standard CNN baseline. The model achieves an accuracy of 97.56% and an F1-score of 97.54%, indicating enhanced spatio-temporal feature extraction and improved generalization. These findings confirm the effectiveness of integrating multiscale and temporal pooling mechanisms and provide a promising foundation for developing reliable automated tools to support early dysarthria detection.

title: SIMMS: Securing the IoT Through a Multi-Protocol Multi-Gateway System
authors: Özlem Şeker, Umut Can Çabuk, Gökhan Dalklç
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computing
abstract: Securing the Internet of Things (IoT) is a big challenge since the emergence of the IoT. Lack of a global security standard is mainly caused by the availability of multiple modes of connectivity and heavily customized network architectures. This research introduces the Secure Multi-protocol Multi-gateway Scheme (SIMMS), a robust framework designed to enhance security in IoT setups by integrating multi-factor authentication, role-based access authorization, and data encryption across Wi-Fi, Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE), and ZigBee. The scheme proposes a two-layered gateway lineup: one (or more) relay gateway(s) handling communication over various protocols in the lower layer, and a main gateway organizing the dataflow on the higher level. Utilizing MQTT, SIMMS supports dynamic token changes and incorporates QR code-based secret transmissions alongside HMAC-based one-time passwords (HOTP) to ensure privacy and access authorization. Our prototype, with ESP32 modules, Raspberry Pi, and a PC-based identity server, demonstrates the framework's practicality and effectiveness. Performance evaluations confirm that SIMMS maintains message reliability and network performance, as throughputs remain higher than 100 kbps for BLE and 1 kbps for ZigBee. Overall, SIMMS offers a flexible, scalable security solution that greatly enhances the security capabilities of IoT systems.

title: Mitigating Predicted Misfit in Optimal Transport-Based Iterative Marchenko Multiple Elimination
authors: Haoxin Zhu, Zhangqing Sun, Bin Hu, Jianwei Nie, Fei Jiang, Fuxing Han, Yang Zhang, Mingchen Liu, Zhenghui Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate reconstruction of subsurface structures represents a critical challenge in wave-equation inversion and resource exploration. The Born approximation, which assumes primary dominance, serves as the theoretical foundation for imaging and inversion methods. However, strong multiple reflections violate this assumption, leading to misinterpretation of mid-deep structures and distortion in reservoir assessment. Although the Marchenko multiple elimination (MME) method possesses theoretical advantages, its practical application is constrained by input data requirements such as ultra-high signal-to-noise ratio and ideally source wavelet deconvolution. These limitations stem from MME's purely numerical iteration process, which propagates errors and generates non-causal artifacts when the aforementioned requirements are not perfectly met. Conventional approaches merely adapt to MME's requirements through data preprocessing without addressing the fundamental limitation of its iterative algorithm: the lack of a regional constraint. This study proposes an optimal transport-based Marchenko iterative framework that utilizes the Wasserstein distance as an objective, transforming the originally pointwise numerical correlation-based iteration into a regionally-constrained distribution system. By minimizing the distributional discrepancy between predicted and observed wavefields, our method simultaneously resolves phase and amplitude mismatches, fundamentally suppressing error propagation. Validation using both synthetic and field data demonstrates that the proposed approach significantly enhances the stability, accuracy, and computational efficiency of multiple suppression, providing reliable support for mid-deep imaging and complex reservoir characterization.

title: Performance Improvement of Single-Input-Multi-Output Free-Space Optical Communication Links Using Hybrid Spatial-Modal Diversity
authors: Chengze Ming, Xinyi Zheng, Tianyan Li, Yining Xu, Tongling He, Shiming Gao
abstract: A receiving improvement method is proposed and experimentally demonstrated based on hybrid spatial-modal diversity for single-input-multi-output (SIMO) free-space optical (FSO) communication links. A moderate turbulence with a Rytov variance of 0.4665 is simulated using a rotatable phase plate. For 10 Gb/s quadrature phase shift keying (QPSK) signals, the hybrid spatial-modal diversity is realized by employing a two-aperture array with three-mode photonic lanterns. At the bit error rate (BER) of 1×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-4</sup>, the transmitted power is reduced by about 2.8 dB due to the spatial diversity compared to a single aperture. Meanwhile, a 2.2-dB improvement is achieved using linear polarization modal diversity. The overall improvement reaches 3.9 dB through the hybrid spatial-modal diversity.

title: Physically Plausible Human-Object Rendering from Sparse Views via 3D Gaussian Splatting
authors: Weiquan Wang, Jun Xiao, Yi Yang, Yueting Zhuang, Long Chen
abstract: Rendering realistic human-object interactions (HOIs) from sparse-view inputs is a challenging yet crucial task for various real-world applications. Existing methods often struggle to simultaneously achieve high rendering quality, physical plausibility, and computational efficiency. To address these limitations, we propose HOGS (Human-Object Rendering via 3D Gaussian Splatting), a novel framework for efficient HOI rendering with physically plausible geometric constraints from sparse views. HOGS represents both humans and objects as dynamic 3D Gaussians. Central to HOGS is a novel optimization process that operates directly on these Gaussians to enforce geometric consistency (i.e., preventing inter-penetration or floating contacts) to achieve physical plausibility. To support this core optimization under sparse-view ambiguity, our framework incorporates two pre-trained modules: an optimization-guided Human Pose Refiner for robust estimation under sparse-view occlusions, and a Human-Object Contact Predictor that efficiently identifies interaction regions to guide our novel contact and separation losses. Extensive experiments on both human-object and hand-object interaction datasets demonstrate that HOGS achieves state-of-the-art rendering quality and maintains high computational efficiency.

title:
A 3nm Fin-FET 19.87-Mbit/mm
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    2RW Pseudo Dual-Port 6T SRAM With Metal Wire-R Tracking and Sequential Access-Aware Dynamic Power Reduction
authors: Tomotaka Tanaka, Koji Nii, Yuichiro Ishii, Makoto Yabuuchi, Yumito Aoyagi, Masaya Hamada, Kazuto Mizutani, Hidehiro Fujiwara, Isabel Wang, Hong-Chen Cheng, Hung-Jen Liao, Tsung-Yung Jonathan Chang
abstract: This article presents a 2-read/write (2RW) pseudo dual-port (PDP) static random access memory (SRAM) macro implemented in advanced 3nm Fin-FET technology, achieving a competitive bit density of 19.87Mbit/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> for advanced nodes. To address challenges in process scaling, reliability under process voltage temperature variations, and dynamic power consumption, two design techniques are introduced: 1)metal wire resistance tracking for adaptive between margin optimization, and 2)sequential access-aware dynamic power reduction to achieve power savings under specific conditions. Post-layout simulations and silicon measurements validate the proposed techniques, demonstrating stable operation across a wide voltage–frequency range and reducing macro-level dynamic power by up to 12%. These innovations significantly enhance performance, power, and area for high-density, low-power applications.

title: Impact of Unit Carbon Quota Mechanism on Clean Energy Investment Strategies: A Biform Game Analysis
authors: Wei Chen, Ruonan Zhu, Jing Chen
abstract: This study investigates clean energy investment decisions in an electricity supply chain, focusing on the unit carbon quota mechanism (UCM). Using a biform game framework that combines noncooperative pricing with cooperative investment, we compare equilibrium outcomes with and without the UCM. We find that: (1) the UCM does not universally stimulate clean energy investment. When unit carbon emissions are low, the UCM promotes investment and expands electricity demand; when unit carbon emissions are high, the no-UCM scenario instead yields higher investment and demand. (2) Under low unit carbon emissions, upstream producer profits, downstream retailer profits, and social welfare are higher with the UCM; under high unit carbon emissions, these outcomes are higher without the UCM. (3) When unit carbon emissions are low, a higher carbon price reduces both wholesale and retail electricity prices while increasing clean energy investment and demand; when unit carbon emissions are high, a higher carbon price increases wholesale and retail prices but suppresses investment and demand.

title: The Asymptotic Number of Equivalence Classes of Linear Codes with Given Dimension
authors: Andrea Di Giusto, Alberto Ravagnani
abstract: We investigate the asymptotic number of equivalence classes of linear codes with prescribed length and dimension. While the total number of inequivalent codes of a given length has been studied previously, the case where the dimension varies as a function of the length has not yet been considered. We derive explicit asymptotic formulas for the number of equivalence classes under three standard notions of equivalence, for a fixed alphabet size and increasing length. Our approach also yields an exact asymptotic expression for the sum of all q-binomial coefficients, which is of independent interest and answers an open question in this context. Finally, we establish a natural connection between these asymptotic quantities and certain discrete Gaussian distributions arising from Brownian motion, providing a probabilistic interpretation of our results.

title: A Geometric Analysis-Based Method for Offline Conflict Detection and Resolution of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles
authors: Jian Yang, Weiqiang Zeng, Kerou Liang, Lidong Zhang, Guoli Yang
abstract: With the wide applications of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), ensuring their flight safety has become a critical concern. This paper addresses offline conflict detection and resolution for large-scale UAV operations in urban airspace. By modeling flight segments as function of time, we derive analytical safe separation constraints for various types of conflicts and propose a multi-UAV conflict detection mechanism. Since two linearly mutually exclusive constraints can be derived from pairwise flight segment conflicts, we model the multi-UAV conflict resolution problem as a Mixed Integer Linear Programming (MILP) model, with the objective to find the optimal takeoff time adjustment solutions. Compared with existing methods, our algorithm resolves conflicts more effectively while requiring only minimal takeoff time adjustments, ensuring both safety and operational efficiency.

title: Executive Information System for Morbidity Reporting Based on ICD-10 at Benteng Public Health Center in Indragiri Hilir
authors: Muhammad Rakha, Muhammad Ridha, Saskia Putri Ananda, I Wayan Palton Anuwiksa
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: The Public Health Center (Puskesmas) is a technical implementation unit (UPT) of the city or district Health Office, responsible for providing health services within a specific work area. Puskesmas regularly submit morbidity reports to the Health Office, which are then recapitulated as part of the Health Office's health service. Several obstacles often hinder obtaining morbidity reports because compiling and sending them to a higher level is still done manually by recapping in a ledger at the Sub-health Center. At the same time, the Benteng Health Center uses Microsoft Excel to send the reports to the Indragiri Hilir Health Office. The Microsoft Excel file of the morbidity report received by the Indragiri Hilir Health Office cannot be used for detailed and specific information because it is difficult to read and understand. Therefore, the management of morbidity reports intended to produce information has not been well-organized. The requirement for detailed reports and information has not been fulfilled for consideration in decision-making. Hence, the executive information system is needed by implementing a computerized system with the waterfall development method. This system can accurately state and summarize morbidity report information by displaying it in graphics and tables. Moreover, it can be drilled down into more specific and detailed reports. Based on the results of tests carried out using the Black Box and User Experience Questionnaire, the system runs well. It obtains the results of the attractiveness test of 2.408, perspicuity 2.300, efficiency 2.488, dependability 2.363, stimulation 2.200, and novelty 1.925, all categorized as excellent.

title: A Lightweight Convolutional Neural Network Architecture for Intelligent Classification of Patient Food Leftovers’ Levels
authors: Sekar Parameswara Meilia Soegiharto, Yuita Arum Sari, Sigit Adinugroho
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Accurate assessment of hospital food leftovers is critical in monitoring patient nutrition and minimizing inefficiency; however, the current method of visual estimation by observer is subjective, carried out without impartiality and time-consuming. Hence, this article has proposed a lightweight convolutional neural network (CNN) architecture, based on MobileNetV2, to classify hospital food leftovers images according to the Comstock scale. As a highly configurable model designed for low-resource devices such as mobile tablets, the proposed Lightweight CNN enables real-time and impartial assessment of food leftovers. The experimental results reveal that the proposed Siamese MobileNetV2 architecture achieves an overall accuracy of 73.58%, MAE equals 0.547, and Cohen’s Kappa equals to 0.641. Even with a small dataset and class imbalance, our model yields objective predictions at low computational cost, therefore proposing promising applications in hospital. This method shows potential for AI-based nutrition care and reducing food waste in hospitals – can be embedded into hospital information system to assist dietitians in interpreting patients’ consumption patterns and developing personalized menu plans. The results also confirm that Lightweight CNNs can process real-world clinical image data and facilitate the translation of scientific research into clinical practice. Our future study will concentrate on expanding the dataset, multimodal analysis and deploying real-time to mobile devices.

title: ZACHO: Zero-Shot Agile Circuit Hardening Optimizer via Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Junfeng Zhu, Yueming Hu, Liang Wang, Zhe Li, Yan Li, Xiaoyang Zeng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: As integrated circuits (ICs) continue to scale, soft errors pose a growing threat to system reliability—particularly in safety-critical applications. Among various circuit components, flip-flops (FFs) are especially susceptible to soft errors due to their state-holding nature, making them prime targets for selective hardening. However, such hardening introduces area and power overheads, necessitating careful tradeoffs. Existing methods typically rely on multiobjective optimization (MOO) algorithms to identify Pareto-optimal hardening strategies, yet their inherent randomness limits controllability, and they must be rerun entirely for each new circuit instance, resulting in poor adaptability. In this work, we propose a deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based hardening optimizer that supports zero-shot generalization to unseen circuits. Built upon the advantage actor–critic (A2C) framework, our model is trained on synthetic circuits and learns to make sequential hardening decisions under strict resource constraints. A recurrent neural network (RNN) captures the historical context of prior hardening steps, while an attention mechanism dynamically focuses on FFs with high soft-error vulnerability at each decision step. Experimental evaluations on benchmark circuits demonstrate that our proposed framework achieves an average hypervolume (HV) improvement of 19.58%. Furthermore, the framework provides at least a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$9.9\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> runtime speedup for individual circuits, offering an agile and generalizable solution for reliability-centric circuit design. The source code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/Magic00JuJu/ZACHO/tree/main</uri>

title: Nonsmooth Sensorless Control for Distributed Dual In-Wheel PM Motors Coordination Drive System
authors: Xiaolei Cai, Xiaoyong Zhu, Tangyou Huang, Li Zhang, Xiaonan Yang, Jiaqi Jing
abstract: Due to the strict synchronization performance requirements of the dual in-wheel motors distributed sensorless coordination control system, higher requirements for rotor position estimation and tracking accuracy of the corresponding in-wheel motor sensorless control are put forward. Yet, the estimation accuracy of the in-wheel motor sensorless control will be reduced by the uncertain interference under complex operating conditions, which will seriously affect synchronization performance. Most previous studies regarding coordination control may neglect the inherent limitations of sensorless systems, leading to the instability of system. In this article, a nonsmooth sensorless control strategy for the distributed dual in-wheel motors coordination drive system at zero/low speed is innovatively proposed, which is based on high-frequency voltage injection. First, a nonsmooth extended state observer is designed to improve the estimation accuracy and anti-interference performance of the sensorless control system. Additionally, a nonsmooth speed controller with disturbance observer feedback compensation is designed to improve the convergence performance of the sensorless coordination control. Therefore, the proposed algorithms for the dual in-wheel motor drive system have good estimation performance of the sensorless control, strong anti-disturbance capability, rapid transient response, as well as good synchronization performance under different operating conditions. The experimental results verify the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed dual in-wheel motors sensorless coordination drive control strategy.

title: A fiber ring resonator for measuring modulation parameters of electro-optic modulators
authors: Sicheng Jiang, Yanchen Fang, Zhongyuan Wu, Fengchun Qiu, Zeren Zhao, Jiali Liu, Yuanbo Du
abstract: This paper demonstrates a novel application of the fiber ring resonator in the modulation parameter measurement of electro-optic phase modulators (EOMs). A piezoelectric (PZT)-based fiber tension device controls the resonant frequency by altering the optical path length of the fiber through stretching. This enables the fiber ring resonator to detect the spectra, thereby allowing the measurement of the EOMs' modulation parameters. Experimental results show that it can achieve a half-wave voltage resolution of better than <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula>0.01 V at 2.1 GHz and a frequency resolution of 50 MHz using the fiber ring resonator. The measurement results are also validated using independent methods. This provides a simple and cost-effective solution for calibrating the performance of EOMs. Following further integration with the signal source and the acquisition system, this method holds promise for developing portable devices with broader applications.

title: Hybrid ARIMA-LSTM Ensemble for Cryptocurrency Price Forecasting: A Comparative Study Across Bitcoin, Ethereum, Binance Coin, and Cardano
authors: Nurul Mukhlisah Abdal, Asmaul Husnah Nasrullah, Dewi Fatmarani Surianto, Wirawan Setialaksana
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: This study compares classical, deep learning, and hybrid approaches for cryptocurrency price forecasting across Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), Binance Coin (BNB), and Cardano (ADA) using daily data from 15 September 2022 to 15 September 2025. We implement an ARIMA baseline, a stacked LSTM network, and an inverse-error-weighted ARIMA- LSTM ensemble. Feature engineering includes trend, momentum, volatility, and volume indicators; models are evaluated with expanding walk-forward validation and multiple metrics (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, RMSE, MAE, MAPE, sMAPE, MASE). Statistical significance is assessed via Diebold-Mariano tests. Results indicate that ARIMA consistently outperforms LSTM across all assets, with average performance of R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.924 ± 0.051, MAPE = 2.19 ± 0.81%, and MASE = 0.95 ± 0.30, compared with LSTM’s R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.527 ± 0.709, MAPE = 4.36 ± 0.26%, and MASE = 1.91 ± 1.25. The ensemble attains R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.902 ± 0.084 and MAPE = 2.36 ± 0.58%, retaining ~97% of ARIMA’s explanatory power while reducing volatility relative to LSTM. Asset-specific analyses show strong ARIMA performance for ETH (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.972 ± 0.038) and BNB (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.964 ± 0.045), and LSTM failure on BTC (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = -0.534 ± 0.214). Cross-asset dispersion in R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> is markedly lower for ARIMA than for LSTM, indicating superior generalization. DM tests confirm ARIMA’s advantage over LSTM (p < 0.001) across assets, with ARIMA versus ensemble differences nonsignificant for BNB. Under rigorous out-of-sample evaluation, the parsimonious ARIMA model provides the most accurate and stable forecasts, while the ensemble offers a robust alternative when cross-asset stability is prioritized.

title: A Content-Based Recommender System for Acne-Prone Skin via Active Ingredient Analysis and Naive Bayes Approach
authors: Sherly Angelina, Z. K. A. Baizal
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Information technology and online platforms have significantly changed consumer behavior when purchasing skincare products. However, most current recommendation platforms still focus on reviews and popularity, neglecting active ingredient composition, especially for those with acne-prone skin, due to its direct impact on skin condition and overall appearance. To address this issue, this study proposes a content-based skincare product recommendation system, which is skilled at determining the suitability of active ingredients based on a user's skin type. To achieve this, the proposed system combines Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF), Multinomial Naïve Bayes, and Cosine Similarity as techniques to generate recommendation output. The classification accuracy of the most suitable skin type based on specific ingredients using the TF-IDF and Multinomial Naïve Bayes classification algorithms reached 80%. Furthermore, the developed recommendation system achieved a Precision@5 score of 0.93, a Recall@5 score of 0.66, a MAP@5 score of 0.66, and a NDCG@5 score of 0.95. This demonstrates that TF-IDF + Naïve Bayes + cosine similarity can be effectively combined to provide more relevant and secure online skincare product recommendations. This study has practical applications, demonstrating the capabilities and applicability of more relevant and personalized e-commerce and digital dermatology platforms.

title: Energy-Fairness-Aware Multi-UAV Trajectory Planning Algorithm for AoI-Constrained Data Collection
authors: Sihua Gao, Yujie Qiao, Kaibiao Fu, Kanghua Hui, Rui Huang
abstract: Multi-UAV assisted data collection has been widely applied to enhance data freshness in wireless sensor networks. Most existing studies focus on minimizing the maximum or average Age of Information (AoI), overlooking the heterogeneous AoI requirements of the sensor nodes. These varying constraints increase the difficulty of scheduling UAVs' flight paths and corresponding speeds, resulting in higher total energy consumption and imbalanced energy usage. To address this issue, we optimize the cooperative trajectories of UAVs to meet diverse AoI constraints while considering their limited on-board energy. The cooperative trajectory planning is essential not only to reduce overall energy consumption but also to ensure energy fairness, which is critical for balancing workloads and avoiding excessive burden on individual UAVs. In this paper, the trajectory of each UAV is represented by the data collection positions and flight speeds, where each position is determined by a target sensor node (SN) and an associated hovering offset. We propose a deep reinforcement learning-based algorithm, Cooperation Hybrid Independent Proximal Policy Optimization (CHI-PPO), which jointly schedules UAVs' access sequences to target nodes, hovering offsets, and flight speeds. Since target nodes are modeled as discrete actions, while offsets and speeds are continuous actions, handling such hybrid decision spaces poses a significant challenge. Our algorithm draws inspiration from the distributed framework of Independent Proximal Policy Optimization (I-PPO) and establishes a Cooperation Hybrid Proximal Policy Optimization (CH-PPO) network to select hybrid actions for scheduling the trajectory of each UAV. Simulation results demonstrate that CHI-PPO achieves stable convergence and significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in both energy efficiency and fairness.

title: Energy Minimization in Backscatter Assisted Short Packet Communication Using HARQ-IR
authors: Yiyi Xie, Liqin Shi, Yinghui Ye, Xi Song, Gan Zheng
abstract: In this work, we investigate the average energy minimization for a backscatter assisted short packet communication (SPC) network employing hybrid automatic repeat request with incremental redundancy (HARQ-IR). In the considered network, an Internet of Things (IoT) node modulates its short packets on the energy signal emitted by an energy source (ES) and then backscatters the modulated signal to the receiver with retransmissions. Considering a nonlinear energy harvesting (EH) model at the IoT node, we formulate a problem to minimize the average energy consumption at the ES by jointly optimizing the power reflection coefficient (PRC) and the short-packet blocklength of the IoT node as well as the number of transmissions for SPC. The problem is formulated as a two-dimensional nonlinear integer programming problem. To address it, we first derive the closed-form expression for the IoT node's PRC using convex optimization theory. Then we relax the short-packet blocklength as a continuous variable and prove that the transformed problem with the fixed number of transmissions is convex. Further, an iterative algorithm is proposed to obtain both the optimal short-packet blocklength and the optimal number of transmissions. Simulation results show that the proposed scheme outperforms the scheme without retransmissions in terms of the average energy consumption of the ES.

title: Experimental Investigation into the Challenges of High-Viscosity Oil Spray Cooling Systems
authors: P. Shams Ghahfarokhi, A. Podgornovs, Antonio. J. Marques Cardoso, P. Rasilo
abstract: Oil spray cooling is increasingly adopted for direct thermal management of hairpin end windings in electric traction motors, yet high-viscosity oils introduce atomization and coverage limitations that are not well quantified using practical, off-the-shelf hydraulic nozzles. This paper experimentally investigates how lubricant viscosity (via temperature), nozzle type, and inlet pressure jointly influence (i) nozzle inlet parameters (flow rate and pressure evolution), (ii) spray formation quality, and (iii) the trade-off between heat removal and temperature uniformity on a representative hairpin conductor. Flat-fan, full-cone, and hollow-cone nozzles are tested under identical system constraints (1–4 bar, flow limited by the pump) and assessed using spatially distributed thermocouples. Results show that increased viscosity at low temperature reduces flow rate and can distort spray patterns, with the full-cone nozzle most sensitive to viscosity while the flat-fan nozzle remains comparatively robust. At 4 bar, the hollow-cone nozzle L40 yields the most uniform surface temperature but the lowest heat-transfer coefficient, whereas the flat-fan NF0390 and full-cone MPL0.3M provide the best balance between uniformity and heat removal. A simplified nondimensional interpretation is provided to link observed performance to internal flow regime and impact dynamics, enabling practical nozzle-selection guidance for early-stage design.

title: Concept-cognitive Neural Network for Fairness-aware Maximal Cliques Identification
authors: Fei Hao, Min Tao, Tao Zhang, Li Li, Jianrui Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computing
abstract: To alleviate the scalability bottleneck faced by Formal Concept Analysis (FCA) in mining fairness-aware maximal cliques in attributed social networks, this paper proposes a neuro-symbolic model termed Concept-Cognitive Neural Network (CCNN). Based on the equivalence between fair equiconcept and fairness-aware maximal clique in FCA, CCNN employs deep learning techniques to emulate the concept-generation process of FCA. Specifically, CCNN comprises three core modules: a node embedding encoder, a fair equiconcept upper-bound predictor, and a fair equiconcept generator. Extensive experiments were conducted on multiple random networks and four real-world datasets. The experimental results show that CCNN reduces the runtime on batch data to only 5%–8% of that required by symbolic methods such as RFCRefine, RFCAlter, and FCAMI. The study demonstrates that CCNN offers significant advantages in structural identification and network analysis, thus providing new insights for responsible computational social systems.

title: Enhancing Anime Recommender System Through Semantic Understanding with Sentence-BERT
authors: Fikri Fauzi, Z. K. A. Baizal
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: The rapid rise in anime popularity, resulting in large number of available anime storylines, genres, and titles. This condition makes it difficult for anime lovers to find anime that suit their preferences, making anime recommender system an effective solution to find anime that align with their preferences. However, current anime recommendation systems are mostly still based on popularity and shallow text representations, thus failing to capture deeper semantic meaning. Therefore, we propose an anime recommender system using Sentence-BERT (SBERT) to generate semantically rich embeddings from anime synopses and genres. The system designed to generated personalized anime recommendations by calculating semantic relationship between anime, then generate recommendation lists based on its similarity value. Experimental results show that the SBERT-based model outperforms baseline methods such as BERT and Weighted Word2Vec, achieving the best performance with precision@5 = 0.316, recall@10 = 0.632, F1@5 = 0.346, HR@10 = 0.948, and nDCG@5 = 0.704. These results indicate that SBERT effectively generates relevant anime recommendations. Furthermore, the findings highlight the advantage of using sentence embeddings can enhance the system’s capability to interpreting user preferences from descriptive text. The proposed approach can be applied to other recommender system domains that mostly rely on textual information. This study demonstrating the general applicability of SBERT for semantic-based recommender task.

title: Mapping Spreader Roles in Web3 Discourse: Network Analysis and Topic Modeling of NFT and DeFi Communities
authors: Wynona Jocelin Juono, Dian Puteri Ramadhani, Andry Alamsyah
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Web3 technologies, notably Non-Fungible Tokens (NFTs) and Decentralized Finance (DeFi), have generated extensive social media discourse. This study integrates Social Network Analysis (SNA) and BERTopic to examine spreader roles in shaping Web3 conversations on platform X. We collected 12,925 NFT and 7,087 DeFi posts from August 23 to September 23, 2025, using domain-specific keywords. Data preprocessing removed duplicates, spam, and irrelevant content through URL stripping, hashtag filtering, and manual verification of top spreaders to exclude automated accounts. All collection adhered to X's Terms of Service using publicly available English-language posts without personal identifying information. In-degree centrality analysis identified top spreaders. @GiveRep in NFT achieved a time reached of 288 hours with an average propagation speed of two hours, while @BioProtocol in DeFi demonstrated a uniform persistence of 192 hours uniform persistence across top actors. BERTopic analysis revealed thematic differences. NFT discussions centered on community engagement and speculation, whereas DeFi discourse concentrated on protocol infrastructure and yield mechanisms. NFT spreaders exhibited varied influence duration aligned with thematic diversity, while DeFi spreaders showed uniform persistence constrained by content overlap. This integrated framework advances computational social research methodologies and offers practical insights for Web3 stakeholders to identify key influencers and optimize community engagement strategies. Limitations include single-platform focus and one-month observation period.

title: Kite-Inspired Tethered Lifting-Wing Multicopter Tracking Ground Platform without Relative Position
authors: Haoyu Wei, Quan Quan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: The tethered multicopter is a typical aerial robot that interacts with a ground platform. However, tracking the mobile ground platform without relative position information remains a limitation. The development of physical human-robotics interaction brings a solution. However, the instability of the aerial robot's structural design limited this method to indoor and low-speed environments. In this paper, a kite-inspired structure is designed, and a control method is proposed for tethered lifting-wing multicopters to track mobile ground platforms without requiring relative position information. There is no other interaction between the lifting-wing multicopter and the ground platform other than a physical cable connection. First, the criticality of the kite-inspired structure is analyzed theoretically, which enhances the longitudinal static stability and brings horizontal self-stabilization in tracking tasks. Secondly, a horizontal controller is designed for the tethered lifting-wing multicopter, enabling it to exhibit an admittance mechanical characteristic. The passivity, robustness, and asymptotic stability of the horizontal controller are proved through the theory of passive systems. Simulations and real-world experiments demonstrate the feasibility and reliability of the proposed method.
