title: Data Governance and Accountability in Healthcare Systems
authors: Chitiz Tayal
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Data and information are considered crucial elements of today’s industry, helping drive global economic growth. One of the broadest sectors that handle huge volumes of data globally is the healthcare sector. One of the important domains of the healthcare sector is data governance and accountability. Healthcare involves sensitive data such as diagnosis reports, medical histories, and treatment plans. To ensure data transparency while in storage, governance and accountability are used, as medical errors are often seen. Data governance ensures that health-related information is properly extracted and stored securely, and that it is always accessible when treating a patient or referring to their medical history. However, combining data governance with healthcare is not easy. Some technical structures like DAMA (Discharge against Medical Advice) or COBIT (Control Objectives for Information and Related Technology) are to be applied along with programming languages like Python to perform data analysis. After conducting the data analysis, a few visuals were generated. From the visualization, it was observed that data stewardship was 100%, but the rates of log access and authorized updates were 91.6%. These results proved that the healthcare system needs maintenance and real-time monitoring.

title: Evaluating HQ-SAM for Rural Building and Paddy Field Segmentation in Indonesia
authors: Muhammad Ihsan, Anisa Nabila Rizki Ramadhani, Nabila Sabrina Amelia, Angghi Rohimat, Nanin Trianawati Sugito
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Automating rural basemap production remains challenging due to heterogeneous settlement forms and fragmented agricultural parcels. This study evaluates the zero-shot performance of the High-Quality Segment Anything Model (HQ-SAM) for segmenting residential buildings and paddy fields from 10 cm orthophotos across 11 rural sites in Indonesia. We compare automated outputs (with light post-processing merging) against expert manual digitization using class-specific Intersection over Union (IoU) and end-to-end time efficiency. HQ-SAM attains mean IoU of 0.886 for buildings and 0.758 for paddy fields, with coverage averaging 90.0% and 75.2%, respectively. The workflow reduces annotation time by ≈80% for buildings and ≈77% for paddy fields relative to manual digitization, demonstrating practical viability for large-area mapping. Performance varies with landscape complexity: regular roof geometries and large, well-bounded fields yield higher accuracy, whereas smallholder mosaics and complex topography reduce boundary fidelity. A lightweight merge step improves agreement by 5–9% without fine-tuning or prompting. Results suggest a hybrid operational paradigm in which HQ-SAM provides fast pre-annotation for routine updates and rapid assessments, while complex zones receive targeted quality control.

title: Performance Analysis and Energy-Efficient Optimization for Multiple Active RIS-Assisted MIMO Communication Systems
authors: Hanxiao Yu, Ningzhe Shi, Yiqing Zhou, Zhong Zheng, Jinglin Shi, Zesong Fei
abstract: This paper investigates an uplink multi-user multiple-input multiple-output (MU-MIMO) system assisted by multiple active reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (ARISs). To analytically characterize the performance of multi-ARIS-assisted MIMO system under practical channel conditions, the operator-valued free probability theory is employed to derive closed-form expressions for the ergodic sum rate in spatially transceiver-correlated MIMO channels. Building on these expressions, an alternating optimization framework is developed to jointly design the transmit covariance matrices of the users and the phase-shift matrices of the ARISs, with the objective of maximizing the system energy efficiency (EE). The inherent non-convexity of the formulated problem is addressed by decomposing it into tractable subproblems, which are solved via the Dinkelbach algorithm and a gradient descent method. Simulation results verify the accuracy of the theoretical analysis and demonstrate that the proposed design achieves substantial EE gains and rapid convergence across a wide range of system configurations.

title: Joint Location and Velocity Estimation and Fundamental CRLB Analysis for Cell-Free MIMO-ISAC
authors: Guoqing Xia, Pei Xiao, Qu Luo, Bing Ji, Yue Zhang, Huiyu Zhou
abstract: This paper presents a fundamental performance analysis of joint location and velocity estimation in a cell-free (CF) MIMO integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) system. Unlike prior studies that primarily rely on continuous-time signal models, we consider a more practical and challenging scenario in the discrete-time digital domain. Based on the discrete received signal, we first formulate a logarithmic likelihood function (LLF) for both single- and multiple-target sensing under deterministic, unknown, and spatially varying radar cross-section (RCS) models. Building upon the proposed LLF framework, closed-form Cramér-Rao lower bounds (CRLBs) for joint location and velocity estimation are derived. These CRLBs can serve as a fundamental performance metric to guide CF MIMO-ISAC system design. To enhance tractability, we also develop a class of simplified closed-form CRLBs, referred to as approximate CRLBs, along with a rigorous analysis of the conditions under which they remain accurate. Furthermore, we investigate how the sampling rate, squared effective bandwidth, and time width influence CRLB performance. For a multiple-target scenario, the concepts of safety distance and safety velocity are introduced to characterize the conditions under which the CRLBs converge to their single-target counterparts. Extensive simulations using orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) and orthogonal chirp division multiplexing (OCDM) validate the theoretical findings and provide practical insights for CF MIMO-ISAC system design.

title: Sea Level Anomaly Prediction Using BiTCN with Comparative Deep Learning Evaluation: A Case Study in Semarang
authors: Bagas Eko Tjahyono Putro, Didit Adytia
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Tidal flooding is a major threat to the coastal areas of Semarang, driven by a combination of tidal dynamics, meteorological influences, and long-term sea level rise. One important indicator for understanding and predicting this phenomenon is the Sea Level Anomaly (SLA), which represents the deviation of sea level from normal tide conditions. This study proposes a Bidirectional Temporal Convolutional Network (BiTCN) model for SLA prediction, leveraging meteorological data as predictive features. We analyzed the impact of key weather parameters, including rainfall, wave height, and wind speed, to identify an optimal set of inputs for the model. The results showed that the combination of SLA and wind speed provided the most optimal performance, so these two variables were used as the primary inputs in all model experiments. The performance of BiTCN is also compared with three other deep learning architectures, namely Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM), Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN), and Transformer. The test results showed that BiTCN provided the best performance with an MAE value of 0.0213, an RMSE of 0.0273, and a correlation coefficient (CC) of 0.9665. This higher accuracy and correlation confirm that BiTCN is an effective approach for SLA prediction and has the potential to support early warning systems and tidal flood risk mitigation in the coastal areas of Semarang.

title: The Integration of Machine Learning and IoT for Predictive Analysis on Air Monitoring System: A Review (2015-2025)
authors: Ahmad Fauzi, Endah Setyowati, Hafiyyan Putra Pratama, Valentino Darmawan, Ratu Risky Makhrojah, Raka Yudistira, Saska Amanda Shaliha, Farhan Taqi Ghani
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: This study aims to analyze the evolution of research on predictive analysis, machine learning (ML), the Internet of Things (IoT), and monitoring from 2015 to 2025. It focuses on identifying thematic trends, influential publications, and emerging research directions within the domain. A bibliometric analysis was conducted using data retrieved from Google Scholar through the Publish or Perish (PoP) application, yielding 1,000 publications, of which 997 were included in the analysis. VOSviewer was employed to generate visualizations of keyword co-occurrences, citation networks, and thematic clusters. The findings show a steady increase in publications, with a peak of 200 papers in 2020. Highly cited studies highlight the prominence of deep learning, digital twins, and explainable AI in enhancing IoT-based predictive systems. Visualization mapping identified four major clusters: (i) learning and detection, (ii) IoT and real-time intelligence, (iii) healthcare and applied domains, and (iv) big data and predictive analytics. Research trends indicate a transition from foundational model development to domain-specific and interdisciplinary applications. This study provides a structured mapping of the intellectual landscape and thematic progression related to the integration of IoT and ML. It offers insights into the evolution of predictive analysis and monitoring technologies, guiding researchers in identifying emerging opportunities and addressing real-world challenges.

title: Comparative Analysis of Ornstein-Uhlenbeck (OU) and Multivariate Ornstein-Uhlenbeck Models (MOU) for Weather Forecasting in Indonesia
authors: Indwiarti, Bayu Erfianto, Irfan Darmawan
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: This study aims to analyze and model mean reversion in multivariate weather time series data using a stochastic approach. Daily historical data for five weather variables —minimum temperature, maximum temperature, humidity, Rainfall, and wind speed, totaling 1684 observations, were collected from the BMKG website. The application of the Ornstein-Uhlenbeck (OU) and Multivariate Ornstein-Uhlenbeck (MOU) models to weather data represents a new development, as it introduces methods typically used in finance to the environmental science domain, which has distinct data characteristics. The OU/MOU model in weather data enables the quantification of weather risk and volatility, similar to market risk assessment. The Speed of Reversion matrix (A) in MOU provides insight into the interactions between weather variables, not only showing how quickly temperatures return to normal, but also how today's humidity conditions affect the speed of temperature return to normal the following day. The OU model was estimated to model Rain Drop independently. Meanwhile, the MOU was estimated using a Vector Autoregressive (VAR(1)) model to capture cross-dependence and joint mean reversion among the five variables. The MOU Autoregressive Coefficient Matrix showed high persistence (diagonal coefficients close to 1) and significant interactions between variables. The performance of both models was evaluated using the RMSE metric. Quantitative results indicate that the MOU model (RMSE: 0.9430 and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>: 0.9144) provides superior accuracy compared to the OU model (RMSE: 0.9842 and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>: 0. 9067). Based on the performance test results of the MOU and OU models, there was no significant difference between the OU and MOU models in predicting rainfall. When applied to weather data, the MOU model is more appropriate because weather data consists of several interacting variables or multivariate factors.

title:
Temperature-Aware SoH Estimation for LiFePO
                    <sub>4</sub>
                    Batteries Using IQR and Kalman Filtering
authors: Sucipto Hening, Eka Firmansyah, Oyas Wahyunggoro, Rizky Toyibah, Maylani Tiarna Riasmin, Wiwin Ayu Kusumawati
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Although the state of health (SoH) in battery management systems (BMS) represents how nominal capacity the provided battery energy has, it becomes an essential industrial application parameter in estimating SoH of the electric forklift lithium-ion traction battery. From a practical standpoint, however, real-world operating SoH measurements prove challenging due to unpredictable currents and incomplete charge/discharge intervals. This study developed a SoH estimation method. It relied on charge capacity data smoothing, in which outlier noise reduction used the interquartile range (IQR) method followed by a Kalman filter to increase the reliability and continuity of estimation results during highly dynamic operating conditions. SoH estimations on real-world data, spanning from 66 batteries JK BMS telemetry and 60-360 days of operation, were evaluated for accuracy against a calibrated multimeter (Hioki LR8450) to within 2 % error on temperature, voltage, and current. After 293 days, a maximum SoH loss of 0.7 % and an average of 0.2 % deterioration were noted. The most concerning data were the charging temperatures 47.2 °C (well below the safety limit). The minimal impact of SoH degradation from varying charge temperature changes indicates the proposed smoothing and filtering methods effective at long-term estimation for fleet SoH degradation monitoring, supporting the development of temperature-aware correction parameters in industrial battery health prediction.

title: Magnetic-Field Tuning of Acoustic Propagation in Multiferroic Thin-Film Heterostructures
authors: Mingye Du, Yuxi Wang, Yuetong Jin, Jiawei Li, Daozheng Luo, Xuankai Xu, Lu Sun, Fengyu Liu, Tao Wu
abstract: Magnetoelectric heterostructures integrating piezoelectric and magnetostrictive phases have emerged as a compelling material platform for investigating high-frequency magnetization dynamics and magnetoelastic coupling via acoustic wave excitation. This work presents magnetically tunable 1.015 GHz acoustic delay lines (ADLs) based on AlN/FeGaB heterostructure films, demonstrating both in-band phase and amplitude modulation capabilities. By investigating three characteristic frequency peaks (997.2 MHz, 1015.7 MHz, and 1027.5 MHz) within the device passband, it is observed that tunability tends to be enhanced near the modal peak frequencies where insertion loss is low. Under a magnetic field of 160 Oe with the acoustic wave direction perpendicular to the magnetic field (θ = 90°), the device achieves a maximum differential phase shift of 4.07° and an amplitude modulation of approximately 0.25 dB at 1015.7 MHz. Furthermore, linear phase and amplitude modulations of 3.23°/120 Oe and 0.19 dB/170 Oe are obtained at θ = 90°. These results experimentally demonstrate the feasibility of magnetic-field-engineered control in Lamb-wave acoustic delay lines, highlighting their strong potential for reconfigurable RF front-end applications.

title: Using Machine Learning Models to Predict the Onset of Type 2 Diabetes from Electronic Health Records (EHRs)
authors: Chitiz Tayal
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: The increase in the number of cases of patients suffering from chronic diseases have increased in the past few decades. Studies show that lack of diagnosis and awareness of the individuals about their health to be affected from such diseases has been the major cause. New interventions in the field of healthcare have been introduced where the utilization of technological advance claims to help early detection of chronic diseases. In this study, a technical evaluation has been performed using machine learning models where prediction of Onset of Type 2 diabetes from electronic health records have been processed. The utilization of a random healthcare dataset including records of patients in terms of their probability to be affected by Type 2 diabetes have been performed referring to the Logistic Regression Model and the Gradient Boosting Model. Evaluation of these models have also been performed referring to the performance evaluation metrics of accuracy, precision, recall and F1 Score. The conclusions reveals that the gradient boosting method performs better when compared to that of the logistic regression method by generating an accuracy rate of 90%. This provides indication on the implementation of gradient boosting to identify the probability of patients to develop Type 2 diabetes with time.

title: Sentiment Analysis and Clustering of Vocational Education Selection with Machine Learning
authors: Muhammad Ridha, Yosep Setiawan, Kadek Bintang Januarta, Wildan Yazid Ziddan, Deni Handika Shaputra
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Vocational education plays a vital role in preparing a skilled workforce aligned with Indonesia’s industrial demands, yet it continues to face a "prestige gap," being perceived as less favorable than academic education. This study analyzes public sentiment toward vocational education and explores the underlying factors influencing such perceptions using Machine Learning (ML) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques. The dataset, consisting of 483 valid texts collected from social media, online forums, and field questionnaires, was preprocessed and transformed using TF–IDF and semantic embedding representations. Sentiment classification was conducted using Naïve Bayes, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN) algorithms under different experimental setups with 90:10 and 80:20 train– test splits. To address data imbalance, oversampling techniques including Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) and Random Oversampling (ROS) were applied. Among all experiments, the Naïve Bayes model with TF–IDF and 90:10 configuration achieved the highest performance (accuracy = 81.63%, F1-score = 0.80). Sentiment distribution revealed 58% negative and 42% positive opinions, reflecting ongoing skepticism toward vocational pathways. To identify key discussion themes, semantic-based K-Means clustering was implemented, yielding a Silhouette Score of 0.1303 and forming five major clusters representing social stigma, limited outreach, industrial alignment, curriculum competence, and economic motivation. The integration of sentiment analysis and semantic clustering provides a comprehensive, data-driven understanding of how public perceptions toward vocational education are formed, offering actionable insights for institutions and policymakers to enhance communication strategies, curriculum relevance, and vocational image.

title: Governance-Aligned Agentic AI for Procure-to-Pay: An Object-Centric Policy-as-Code Framework
authors: Rafli Ahmad Nugroho, Andry Alamsyah, Dodie Tricahyono
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Procure-to-Pay (P2P) automation is essential for enhancing overall enterprise efficiency. Yet, existing systems face several challenges such as inconsistent policy enforcement, audit readiness, and operational inefficiencies, especially in multi-stakeholder environments. These issues and complexities hinder smooth integration and effective process optimization. Current research lacks a comprehensive framework to address these issues in a fully scalable, adaptable, and operationally sound manner. This study designs and validates a novel governance-aligned AI framework that integrates policy-as-code, object-centric analysis, and continuous monitoring to ensure consistent policy enforcement, compliance, and audit readiness. The methodology combines structured stakeholder interviews, an integrative literature review, and systematic model development, followed by a rigorous and thorough proof-of-concept evaluation using synthetic P2P scenarios. Performance results from 30 independent trials show two key findings: the average decision latency is 128.4 milliseconds (σ = 7.6ms), indicating stable performance even under high load, and the framework ensures consistent policy enforcement while providing a tamper-proof audit trail, effectively preventing errors such as double-counting and inconsistent exception handling. These results validate the framework's operational effectiveness and provide a robust, reliable foundation for implementing governance-aligned P2P automation, indicating readiness for real-world deployment across diverse organizational contexts and offering practical insights for future scalability and enhanced policy design.

title: Sensing-Aided Channel Estimation for Near-Field MIMO ISAC Systems Via Cross-Attention Transformer
authors: Peihao Dong, Renbin Li, Shen Gao, Shuangshuang Li, Fuhui Zhou, Wei Xu, Qihui Wu
abstract: Near-field integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) can deliver the high spatial resolution and transmission capability with the shared spectrum and hardware. Due to the partial overlap between communication scatterers and radar targets, the sensing information can provide valuable priors to enhance the channel estimation while fusing the two heterogeneous modalities remain challenging. To address this problem, a Cross-Attention Transformer based Channel Estimation Neural Network (CAT-CENet) is developed, which includes a communication pilot branch generating the the Key and Value features and a sensing information branch generating the Query feature. By elaborating the three-module structure, CAT-CENet can focus on features of overlapped targets automatically without need of identifying them in advance. The modality contribution is theoretically analyzed based on the Shapley value to verify the cross-attention gain achieved by CAT-CENet. Simulation results show that CAT-CENet outperforms the state-of-the-art schemes, especially with the higher overlapping proportion, and is robust to the model pruning.

title: Study of the Performance of Plasma Interference Switch in 10-MW-Class S-Band Microwave Compressor
authors: Liu Zijian, Shi Kouzhong, Wei Xiuli, Li Jianfei, Lyu Xingbao, Chu Zijia, Vladislav S. Igumnov, Yao Jingfeng, Yuan Chengxun, Zhou Zhongxiang
abstract: This article studies the performance of a microwave plasma interference switch (MPIS) in a 10-MW-class S-band microwave pulse compressor (MPC). It analyzes the impacts of pin and plate electrodes and distance on the output gain, cavity gain, and self-breakdown characteristics of the MPC. Frequency-domain simulations and experimental validation are conducted using a vector network analyzer (VNA) and high-power microwave tests, in order to determine the optimal structural parameters for the resonant cavity. The obtained results show that large-distance pin electrodes provide the highest output gain (up to 17.7 dB) and are less prone to self-breakdown at repetition frequencies below 25 Hz. On the contrary, small-distance plate electrodes have higher performance at higher repetition frequencies compared with small-distance pin electrodes due to their reduced self-breakdown. The 2-D fluid simulation results of the plasma switch demonstrate its evolution process and allow us to understand the experimental results.

title: COMMA: Coordinate-aware Modulated Mamba Network for 3D Dispersed Vessel Segmentation
authors: Gen Shi, Hui Zhang, Jie Tian
abstract: Accurate segmentation of 3D vascular structures is essential for various medical imaging applications. The dispersed nature of vascular structures leads to inherent spatial uncertainty and necessitates location awareness, yet most current 3D medical segmentation models rely on the patch-wise training strategy that usually loses this spatial context. In this study, we introduce the Coordinate-aware Modulated Mamba Network (COMMA) and contribute a manually labeled dataset of 570 cases, the largest publicly available 3D cerebrovascular dataset to date. COMMA leverages both entire and cropped patch data through global and local branches, ensuring robust and efficient spatial location awareness. Specifically, COMMA employs a channel-compressed Mamba (ccMamba) block to efficiently encode full-resolution image data, capturing long-range dependencies while optimizing computational costs. Additionally, we propose a coordinateaware modulated (CaM) block to enhance interactions between the global and local branches, allowing the local branch to better perceive spatial information. We evaluate COMMA on six datasets, covering two imaging modalities and five types of vascular tissues. The results demonstrate COMMA’s superior performance compared to state-of-the-art methods with computational efficiency, especially in segmenting small vessels. Ablation studies further highlight the importance of our proposed modules and spatial information. The code will be available at COMMA.

title: Accelerating Frontier MoE Training with 3D Integrated Optics
authors: Mikhail Bernadskiy, Peter Carson, Thomas Graham, Taylor Groves, Ho John Lee
abstract:
As traditional semiconductor scaling slows, highspeed interconnects have emerged as the new scaling engine, enabling the creation of larger logical GPUs by linking many GPUs into a single, low-latency, high-bandwidth compute domain. While initial scale-up fabrics leveraged copper interconnects for their power and cost advantages, the maximum reach of passive electrical interconnects (approximately 1 meter) effectively limits the scale-up domain to within a single rack. The advent of 3Dstacked optics and logic offers a transformative, power-efficient scale-up solution for connecting hundreds of GPU packages (thousands of GPUs) across multiple data center racks.

This work explores the design tradeoffs of scale-up technologies and demonstrates how frontier LLMs necessitate novel photonic solutions to achieve aggressive power and performance targets. We model the benefits of 3D integrated optics (Passage) enabled GPUs and switches within the scale-up domain when training Frontier Mixture of Experts (MoE) models exceeding one trillion parameters. Our results show a 2.7X reduction in time-to-train, unlocking unprecedented model scaling.

title: A Multi-Sensor LiDAR–Photogrammetry Workflow for 3D Documentation of Sumbawa Besar Heritage Sites Toward H-BIM Development
authors: Tri Satriawansyah, Lalu Mulyadi, Awan Uji Krismanto, Luh Kadek Dera Erlinda, Ketut Tomy Suhari
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: High-fidelity 3D documentation of historic wooden architecture is essential for cultural heritage preservation and the development of Heritage Building Information Modeling (H-BIM). This study presents a multisensor Li-DAR–photogrammetry workflow applied to three major heritage sites in Sumbawa Besar: Bala Datu Ranga, Istana Dalam Loka, and Istana Bala Puti. Each monument was captured using tailored sensor configurations adapted to architectural scale, interior complexity, and accessibility, with accuracy validated through device-generated quality assessments. For Bala Datu Ranga, a hybrid GNSS–LiDAR–Visual SLAM system was used in PPK mode supported by terrestrial ground control points. The resulting point cloud achieved 100% compliance with the 5 cm precision threshold. More than 80% of the points fall within the 1–2 cm error range, and residuals on ground control points were on the order of 3–6 mm, demonstrating high geometric stability across both indoor and outdoor areas. For Istana Dalam Loka, a combination of static terrestrial laser scanning and handheld SLAM-based scanning was employed. Registration produced a global alignment error of 0.003 m, with individual cloud-to-cloud link errors ranging from 0.001 m to 0.007 m and overlap levels between 23% and 83%. These results confirm strong consistency between scanning positions throughout the building’s expansive multi-level wooden structure. For Istana Bala Puti, rapid indoor–outdoor mapping using a mobile LiDAR–SLAM platform yielded 100% of points within the 5 cm threshold. Approximately 24.5% of the points lie within 0–1 cm, 47.8% within 1–2 cm, and 27.7% within 2– 5 cm, indicating accuracy sufficient for architectural modeling and heritage-scale H-BIM development. Trajectory stability and GNSS quality metrics further demonstrate reliable performance in GNSS-degraded conditions through fused visual–LiDAR SLAM. In summary, the multisensor fusion of static TLS, handheld SLAM, PPK georeferencing, and photogrammetric texturing produced dense and accurate point clouds suitable for semantic segmentation, structural interpretation, and H-BIM generation. The results demonstrate that integrating multiple sensor modalities significantly enhances completeness, minimizes occlusions, and accelerates data acquisition for complex wooden heritage architecture.

title: KRL Load Prediction Using ARIMA for Recommendations on TransJakarta Bus Stop Construction Points
authors: Muhammad Irfan Fathurrahman, Putu Harry Gunawan, Narita Aquarini
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Jakarta’s public transportation system faces persistent challenges of congestion and passenger overcrowding, particularly within commuter rail (KRL) networks during peak hours. These issues arise from the imbalance between passenger demand and available capacity, underscoring the need for data-driven forecasting to support operational planning. This study employs the AutoRegressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA) model to predict passenger traffic patterns and daily load dynamics at key KRL stations. Unlike previous studies that focus solely on short-term passenger trends or rely on deep learning models with high data requirements, this research shows that classical statistical forecasting remains robust and interpretable for urban mobility contexts with limited or irregular data availability. The ARIMA approach was selected for its simplicity, transparency, and proven reliability in time-series forecasting compared to alternatives such as LSTM or Prophet. The model achieved satisfactory performance with Mean Absolute Scaled Error (MASE) values of 0.4014 for boarding, 0.1460 for alighting, and 0.3139 for passenger load prediction. These results validate the model’s accuracy in capturing both temporal fluctuations and growth trends in passenger volumes. The findings suggest that accurate passenger load forecasts can inform optimal train frequency scheduling and the strategic placement of TransJakarta feeder bus stops, thereby improving intermodal connectivity and the efficiency of passenger distribution. Sensitivity and robustness analyzes further confirm the consistency of the model across time intervals, although future enhancements could include the integration of hybrid ARIMA-machine learning and expansion to multiple metro lines.

title: Book Recommendation System Based on Collaborative Filtering Using Matrix Factorization and Sentiment Analysis
authors: Zaky Al Fatih Nata Imam, Agung Toto Wibowo
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: The development of digital technology continues to progress from time to time. Which can facilitate their daily lives. However, digital development is also accompanied by new challenges, one of which is the difficulty of selecting items that match the user’s wishes due to the large amount of information available. One of the affected domains is how to choose a book that suits the user’s wishes. Therefore, we propose a hybrid recommendation system that combines collaborative filtering based on matrix factorization using Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) with sentiment analysis using RoBERTa. The dataset we use is Amazon Books Review, which consists of user identification, item identification, ratings, and text reviews. This research wants to show the importance of user sentiment in determining the desired item preference. Then the performance of the model is evaluated with MAE and RMSE. Our proposed model successfully outperforms the basic SVD model. With MAE 0.6470 to 0.5730 and RMSE 0.9113 to 0.7928. This shows an improvement of approximately 11.4% in MAE and 13.0% in RMSE. By combining collaborative filtering and sentiment analysis, the hybrid model can capture user subjectivity information. This research highlights the importance of recommendation systems that have information related to user sentiment in providing predictions. The result is that the recommendations received by users become more in line with their preferences.

title: Interference Analyses for Wireless Mobile Orthogonal Time-Frequency-Space (OTFS) Communication Systems
authors: Xiaoxue Rao, Xiao Yan, Hsiao-Chun Wu, Qian Wang
abstract: Orthogonal time-frequency-space (OTFS) modulation, which demonstrates the superior robustness against time-frequency-selective fading over the orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM) modulation, has been deemed to be a promising two-dimensional transmission technique for high-mobility wireless communications. Unfortunately, OTFS systems would suffer from inter-frame interference (IFI), inter-Doppler interference (IDoI), and inter-delay interference (IDeI). In this paper, a novel generalized channel model involving IFI, IDoI, and IDeI for wireless OTFS systems has been derived by considering practical factors such as Doppler effect, carrier-frequency drift of local oscillators, multi-path fading, and cyclic prefix coding. Meanwhile, the symbol-error-rate (SER) performance of the PSK-OTFS system is theoretically investigated accordingly. Monte Carlo simulation results demonstrate that our proposed new theoretical SER performance analysis can lead to more accurate results than the existing theoretical analysis involving IDoI only. Moreover, the detailed quantitative analysis of the dominant interference (IFI/IDoI/IDeI) under different conditions is also conducted in this work. Our proposed new interference analysis can be adopted easily to evaluate the reception quality of the future wireless OTFS transceivers.

title: A Hybrid Domain Algorithm for High-Speed High-Squint SAR Imaging with Curved Trajectory via Fifth-Order FNCS Processing
authors: Kun Deng, Yan Huang, Zhanye Chen, Lei Zhang, Yu Xiong, Bin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Because of the severe 2D range-azimuth coupling (RAC) and Doppler space-variance (DSV), high-speed high-squint (HSHS) synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imaging with curved trajectory is still extremely problematic. To address this issue, a hybrid domain algorithm via fifth-order frequency nonlinear chirp scaling (FNCS) processing is proposed in this paper. The proposed algorithm comprises three modules: range focusing, azimuth compression, and geometric correction (GC). To enhance model accuracy while simplifying the coefficient representation, a fifth-order slant range model based on equivalent velocity and parameter vectorization is initially established. After the pre-processing employed to remove bulk RAC, range focusing is accomplished using a range-variant Stolt interpolation. Then, to eliminate DSV for azimuth compression, a fifth-order FNCS processing is presented based on a minimalist derivation approach. This approach derives an explicit expression for the FNCS-processed signal directly from the determined model and NCS orders to aid in the solution of undetermined parameters. Subsequently, an instantaneous projection geometry is proposed for GC, with the capability to eliminate arbitrary geometric distortions. Finally, the results of processing simulation and real SAR data demonstrate the validity and superiority of the proposed algorithm.

title: Predefined-Time Reinforcement Learning for Multi-AAV System Formation Control under DoS Attack
authors: Zhenyu Gong, Feisheng Yang, Zhucheng Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This article studies the secure formation problem for multiple autonomous aerial vehicles (AAVs) subject to the denial-of-service (DoS) attack. An estimation-control hierarchical architecture is introduced to design the secure controllers. In the estimation layer, the resilient observers are designed to estimate the leader's states under DoS attack. In the control layer, by the time base generator (TBG) technique, the optimized predefined-time backstepping controllers are devised for the position and attitude loops. Obtaining the optimal control policies is related to solving the Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman equations. Utilizing the critic-only neural network structure, a TBG-based predefined-time reinforcement learning algorithm is proposed to learn the optimized control policies online. The corresponding predefined-time convergence and the closed-loop stability are analyzed. Finally, simulation examples are conducted to verify the effectiveness of the presented scheme.

title: Manganese (II) Coordination Polymer as a Nonlinear Saturable Absorber for Mode-Locking at 1-micron
authors: M. A. M. Lutfi, H. Ahmad, M. K. A. Zaini, M. Z. Samion, N. Yusoff, Mousa Hussein, K. Thambiratnam, Z. Mahmoodin, S. Sun
abstract: A passive mode-locked laser was demonstrated using an all-fibre ytterbium-doped fibre laser. Mode-locking was generated using a manganese (Mn (II)) material as a saturable absorber (SA) in a ring-cavity ytterbium-doped fibre laser. The Mn (II)-SA, fabricated via the hydrothermal method, using trans-1-(2-pyridyl)-2-(4-pyridyl)ethylene linker and manganese(II) chloride tetrahydrate (MnCl₂·4H₂O). The solution was then deposited onto a side-polished fibre, which exhibits a modulation depth of 18.1%, a saturation intensity of 0.74 MW/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, and a nonsaturable loss of 55.03%. The ring cavity outputs dissipative soliton mode-locked at 1030 nm, with a pulse duration of 9.1 ps and a repetition rate of 20.7 MHz.

title: Fast Algorithms for Modelling and Compensation of Non-Volatile Memory Crossbar Arrays Containing Interconnect Resistance
authors: Zifei Cai, Fan Yang, Boyang Yan, Fang Cheng, Pinfeng Jiang, Nan Li, Xiangshui Miao, Xingsheng Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: This paper firstly proposes a high-efficiency simulation approach for non-volatile memory crossbar arrays by exploiting the mathematical equivalence between random walk processes in resistive networks and Kirchhoff’s current equations, and on the basis of the approach, realizes the compensation of the crossbar array IR-Drop effect. By combining the ideas of alternating direction implicit (ADI) method, recursive algorithm with the circuit topology of the array, this simulation method achieves precise modeling of the equivalent conductance matrix (input-output relationship) for the arrays containing interconnect resistance with a computational complexity of O(mn) (for an m×n array). The method not only guarantees theoretical convergence, but also demonstrates orders-of-magnitude speed improvement over existing solutions and commercial tool, requiring only 3.2 seconds to simulate 1M cell scale arrays. Leveraging this simulation approach, we determined the precise deployment scheme for a CNN model for on 1M memristor arrays within ten minutes, reducing the relative error between the array’s equivalent conductance matrices and target CNN weight matrices from ~50% to less than 1%. This effectively compensates for IR-drop effects, restoring the hardware’s inference accuracy from 57.3% to 96.9% - nearly matching ideal software models.

title: High-Bandwidth and Efficient Silicon Microring Modulator Enabling Low-complexity DMT Transmission
authors: Zelu Wang, Geyang Wang, Kaihang Lu, Xuetong Zhou, Yeyu Tong, Lian Kuan Chen, Hon Ki Tsang
abstract: High-speed and energy-efficient optical interconnects are critical for next-generation data center and high-performance computing systems. Silicon microring modulators offer an attractive solution owing to their compact footprint and low power consumption; however, simultaneously achieving large electro-optic bandwidth and high modulation efficiency remains challenging, particularly for low-complexity intensity-modulation and direct-detection links. In this work, we experimentally demonstrate a high-performance silicon microring modulator with an electro-optic modulation bandwidth exceeding 110 GHz and modulation efficiency up to 50 pm/V, enabling high-speed transmission with simplified transmitter and digital signal processing. By employing discrete multi-tone modulation with one-tap frequency-domain equalization, direct-drive transmission from an arbitrary waveform generator is realized without RF amplifiers or bias tees at a data rate of 262.5 Gbit/s below the soft-decision forward error correction threshold using only 0.8 Vpp driving voltage. With electrical amplification, a back-to-back data rate of 300 Gbit/s is further achieved. A comprehensive comparison with single-carrier PAM-4 transmission shows that PAM-4 achieves 260 Gbit/s in a back-to-back configuration using linear equalization, but only 200 Gbit/s over 5 km of standard single-mode fiber with nonlinear equalization due to its sensitivity to chromatic dispersion. In contrast, the DMT-based system maintains robust operation over fiber links up to 5 km with low DSP complexity, achieving 225 Gbit/s. These results represent the highest reported data rate for microring modulator based DMT transmission using linear equalization and demonstrate a scalable, low-power solution for short-reach optical interconnects in future high-capacity silicon photonic systems.

title: In Situ Estimation of IBR Models for Analyzing Sub-Synchronous Oscillations
authors: Jianli Gao, Muhammad Sharjeel Javaid, Debraj Bhattacharjee, Youhong Chen, Balarko Chaudhuri
abstract: The opaqueness of vendor-specific black-box models of inverter-based resources (IBRs) is a barrier to the systematic analysis of IBR-induced sub-synchronous oscillations (SSO). Existing approaches via impedance scan require isolating each IBR from the power system and interfacing it with a voltage or current source. However, this procedure is not feasible when the IBR device is embedded within the rest of the system (RoS). In this paper, we propose an augmented eigensystem realization algorithm (ERA)-based method to estimate the IBR model in situ, i.e., while the IBR remains connected to RoS. The core idea is to disentangle the IBR's impedance spectra from its closed-loop voltage and current responses to a pulse probing. Notably, the augmented ERA enables automated model order selection, ensuring scalability for realistic IBR-rich zones of a power grid. The estimated linearized IBR models are then combined with the known RoS model, which yields a linearized state-space model of the overall power system. For validation, the case studies demonstrate the applicability and scalability of the proposed method. The frequency-domain and time-domain responses of the estimated IBR models closely match those of the actual models. Modal analysis accurately identifies the IBR with dominant contribution to poorly damped SSO, therefore enabling targeted and effective mitigation.

title: A Novel Structure-Aware Multipath Clustering and Tracking Algorithm for Dynamic Communication Channels
authors: Shuaiqi Gao, Mi Yang, Bo Ai, Jingjing Yu, Yi Gong, Ruisi He, Zhengyu Zhang, Junzhe Song
abstract: Extensive channel measurement campaigns have shown that multipath components (MPCs) generally exhibit clustered distributions, making cluster-based models a cornerstone of wireless channel modeling. Developing time-varying cluster-based channel models requires not only clustering MPCs in the delay, angle, and power domains, but also tracking their temporal evolution. However, existing multipath clustering algorithms typically rely on fixed hyperparameters and therefore are often poorly suited to dynamically evolving clusters, while tracking algorithms based solely on distance metrics are prone to ambiguous associations among spatially proximate clusters. Moreover, treating clustering and tracking as separate processes often prevents temporal evolution information from being fully exploited during clustering. This paper proposes a structure-aware unified framework that integrates Adaptive Neighborhood Robust Mean Shift (AN-RMS) clustering with BoxKF-Cluster tracking. Within the proposed framework, AN-RMS, built upon kernel density estimation, adaptively determines the effective number of nearest neighbors K by detecting abrupt changes in the second-order gradient of the neighborhood-distance sequence, thereby identifying structural cluster boundaries and providing locally adaptive guidance for density-mode iteration. BoxKF-Cluster introduces oriented bounding boxes derived from root-mean-square statistics to characterize the evolving morphology of clusters, and combines Kalman filtering with a successive-interference-cancellation (SIC)-like strategy, in which the influence of existing clusters is first removed to facilitate the detection of newly emerging ones. As a result, clustering and tracking are jointly accomplished within a unified framework. The proposed method is validated in complex scattering scenarios using both ray-tracing simulations and real vehicle-to-vehicle millimeter-wave measurement data, demonstrating its effectiveness for time-varying channel modeling.

title: Variable Regenerative Braking Torque and Improved Energy Recovery for Battery Electric Vehicle With Ultracapacitor
authors: Md. Irshad Ansari, Himanshu Misra
abstract: Battery Electric Vehicles (BEVs) have a smaller driving range than internal combustion (IC) engine vehicles due to their low specific energy capacity. This inherent problem can be addressed to some extent by the regenerative braking (RB) energy recovery. However, RB energy harvesting is limited by various constraints such as vehicle speed, driver's habits, road conditions, etc. Moreover, transient RB energy recovery in battery (BT) results in accelerated degradation of its state-of-health (SoH) and capacity. To address the above-mentioned challenges, this article proposes a novel configuration for the integration of BT and ultracapacitor (UC), where UC acts as the primary sink for RB energy. Furthermore, a unified approach of smooth and intensive RB with variable braking torque is introduced. This regulation of RB torque can assist the mechanical braking during various braking requirements of the vehicle. For smooth RB command, the duty ratio for the VSI switches is calculated by estimating the back emf from the motor speed. The intensive braking is achieved by controlling the braking torque through a proportional integral (PI) controller, generating the corresponding duty ratio. The experimental results show 8.03% higher RB energy is recovered, and the smoothing of the BT current results in a minimum standard deviation. Additionally, the proposed intensive braking technique achieved a maximum braking torque of -6 Nm with a minimum braking time of 3.6 s. The BT peak current and peak power are reduced with the increased participation from UC during transients and high load requirements, resulting in 29.15% improvement in BT's SoH.

title: Generalization Bounds for Quantum Learning via Rényi Divergences
authors: Naqueeb Ahmad Warsi, Ayanava Dasgupta, Masahito Hayashi
abstract: This work advances the theoretical understanding of quantum learning by establishing a new family of upper bounds on the expected generalization error of quantum learning algorithms, leveraging the framework introduced by Caro et al. (2024) and a new definition for the expected true loss. Our primary contribution is the derivation of these bounds in terms of quantum and classical Rényi divergences, utilizing a variational approach for evaluating quantum Rényi divergences, specifically the Petz and a newly introduced modified sandwich quantum Rényi divergence. Analytically and numerically, we demonstrate the superior performance of the bounds derived using the modified sandwich quantum Rényi divergence compared to those based on the Petz divergence. Furthermore, we provide probabilistic generalization error bounds using two distinct techniques: one based on the modified sandwich quantum Rényi divergence and classical Rényi divergence, and another employing smooth max Rényi divergence.

title: A Certificateless Aggregate Signature-Based Security Scheme for Smart Home Monitoring
authors: Naif Alzahrani
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: The increasing adoption of the Internet of Things (IoT) in the smart home environment has raised concerns regarding the security of exchanged information from the smart home environment to the gateway and onward to the user. Existing security mechanisms either fail to fulfill the security requirements of smart homes, are unable to preserve privacy, or have high computational and communicational overheads, rendering them unsuitable for resource-constrained IoT applications in a smart home environment. To address the issue of security and preserve user privacy, this paper proposes a certificateless aggregate signature-based framework for smart home surveillance. The proposed scheme integrates pairing cryptography and an aggregation method instead of a one-to-one approach, which could be flawed due to the maximum transmission delay. The pairing and aggregation boost all the involved entities for the authentic communication of sensitive information from IoT to the fog node, and onward to the user's mobile device for efficient and effective surveillance of the smart home. The proof of correctness and robustness analysis of the proposed security protocol is conducted through the Real-or-Random (RoR) model, while the performance evaluation section is accomplished through a testbed research method in measuring the cost of different cryptographic operations, bandwidth utilization for the communication cost, and computation cost, along with energy consumption. The results obtained from these analyses demonstrate that the proposed security protocol is feasible for the resource-constrained smart home environment.

title: Semantic-Driven Book Recommender: An Application of Word2Vec to Synopsis Embeddings
authors: Muhammad Hilmi Fauzi Sundayana, Z. K. A. Baizal
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: The growth of digital books made a significant challenge for users to find relevant titles, which effective recommender systems to provide personalized suggestions is needed. Traditional collaborative filtering approaches, including matrix factorization, have been widely used. However, these methods still face challenges such as the cold-start problem and suboptimal relevance. While content-based filtering using Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) is often fails to capture deep semantic relationships. To address this gap, we propose a content-based system using the Word2Vec model, which good at learning contextual meaning from text by representing book synopses as semantic vectors. We calculate similarity between synopses using cosine similarity and compiling a list of recommendations based on the degree of similarity. We evaluated the system using accuracy, precision, and recall metrics to measure the system’s performance in providing relevant recommendations. Our results show the Word2Vec-based approach achieved a precision of 15.20% and accuracy of 37.5% for Top-5 recommendations. This represents a significant improvement, achieving a 105% relative gain in precision over the traditional TF-IDF method (7.40%) and outperforming its 23% accuracy. These describe that semantic vector representation with Word2Vec provides more relevant and context-aware recommendations. This work’s main contribution is to demonstrate that synopsis embedding with Word2Vec is more effective approach for book recommendations than traditional frequency-based methods, offering a good way to improve content-based filtering on digital literacy platforms.

title: On the Robust Classification Forest with Expectile Link Loss for Diabetes Probability Prediction
authors: Rinda Nariswari, Wisnowan Hendy Saputra, Viska Noviantri, Mohd Zaki Awang Chek, Matthew Owen
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Accurate and early prediction of diabetes is critical for effective patient management. While standard Classification Forests (CF) are powerful, their symmetric loss functions can be suboptimal for clinical diagnostics. This study introduces the Robust Classification Forest with Expectile Link Loss (RCF-ELL), a novel framework that uses an asymmetric loss function to penalise false negatives more heavily, thus improving prediction reliability. We applied this framework to the Pima Indians Diabetes Dataset and compared it against a standard CF and a Decision Tree (DT). Model performance was rigorously evaluated using 10-fold stratified cross-validation. The empirical results demonstrate that the proposed RCF-ELL model achieves superior performance, yielding a mean F1-score of 0.813 and an Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 0.891, markedly outperforming the standard CF (F1-score of 0.794; AUC of 0.799). This significant improvement in the F1-score indicates a better balance between precision and recall, suggesting that RCF-ELL is a more robust and clinically relevant tool for diabetes risk prediction.

title: AI-IoT-Based Federated Edge and Digital Twin Framework for Real-Time Climate-Resilient Hazard Forecasting
authors: Thirupathi Regula, Warda Al-Hoqani, S Gouri Kiran Kumar, Sunitha Cherian
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Landslides and sandstorms pose significant risks to infrastructure and human safety across arid regions such as Oman and the wider GCC, where rapid environmental changes intensify hazard frequency and impact. This paper presents an Artificial Intelligence and Internet of Things (AI–IoT)–based Federated Edge and Digital Twin Framework designed for real-time landslide and sandstorm forecasting with climate-resilient early warning. The proposed architecture integrates Federated Edge Intelligence, TinyML-powered edge nodes, and Digital Twin modeling to enable decentralized, privacy-preserving, and low-latency hazard prediction. A network of heterogeneous sensors—including LiDAR, hyperspectral particulate sensors, MEMS accelerometers, and soil-moisture probes—collects continuous geotechnical and atmospheric data from high-risk zones. Edge nodes perform on-site anomaly detection and transmit aggregated insights to a cloud-based Digital Twin, which fuses satellite SAR imagery, geospatial models, and real-time sensor data to simulate terrain dynamics and environmental stress responses. The system employs LoRaWAN and 5G URLLC for reliable long-range and ultra-low-latency communication, while wearable haptic alert devices ensure localized and multilingual notifications even in connectivity-challenged areas. A reinforcement learning–driven alert prioritization engine minimizes false alarms and enhances situational accuracy. By combining AI-driven sensing, federated learning, immersive geospatial visualization, and adaptive early-warning mechanisms, the framework advances disaster readiness, safeguards human life and infrastructure, and aligns with sustainable climate adaptation goals for arid and semi-arid ecosystems. The framework was validated through simulation and field datasets demonstrating 94.8% accuracy and sub-3-second latency, with containerized scalability and federated privacy protection ensuring real-world deployability.

title: Developing an IoT-Enabled National Cadastral Digital Twin Framework for an Integrated Geoinformatics Reference System in Indonesia
authors: Ketut Tomy Suhari, Hery Purwanto, Dedy Kurnia Sunaryo, Fransiscus Xaverius Ariwibisono, Antonio Heltra Pradana, Rizky Aura Fifi Saputri
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: This paper presents the development of a Digital Twin framework for establishing a unified cadastral reference system in Indonesia. The research addresses persistent issues in the national cadastre, including data gaps, overlaps, and inconsistencies across institutions that arise from fragmented survey campaigns, heterogeneous mapping standards, and the absence of a unified, three-dimensional cadastral reference system capable of reconciling multi-agency datasets. At the international level, most existing Digital Twin implementations focus on infrastructure, mobility, or generic 3D city models rather than legally binding cadastre, leaving a gap in national-scale cadastral digital twins particularly in developing-country contexts. Integrating Building Information Modelling (BIM), Geographic Information Systems (GIS), and Internet of Things (IoT) technologies, the proposed system provides a three-dimensional (3D) digital representation of land parcels, ownership, and related assets. Data acquisition was carried out using oblique photogrammetry with a UAV Trinity Pro equipped with a D2M oblique camera system, achieving geo-metric accuracy with CE90 of 0.047 m and LE90 of 0.822 m. The prototype system, visualized through a WebGIS platform using CesiumJS, demonstrates how Digital Twin technology can support interoperability and multipurpose cadastral management. The IoT layer is implemented by linking cadastral objects in the 3D model to fixed CCTV camera sensors and their live metadata streams, allowing users to contextualize real-time observations within the cadastral Digital Twin. The study establishes a foundation for future GeoAI-based analytics in cadastral automation, spatial anomaly detection, and predictive land management toward a fully intelligent national cadastral system. In particular, the proposed National Cadastral Digital Twin (NCDT) prototype demonstrates how oblique multi-view 3D data, BIM–CityGML semantics, and 3D Tiles web streaming can be operationalized into a legally compliant and interoperable cadastral reference framework tailored to Indonesia, providing a concrete proof-of-concept for an IoT-enabled cadastral Digital Twin aligned with the One Map Policy.

title: Study on the Single-Event Burnout Mechanism of Ka-Band GaN MMIC Power Amplifiers Under Heavy Ion Irradiation
authors: Li Zhang, Xuefeng Zheng, Danmei Lin, Hao Zhang, Xiaohu Wang, Kang Li, Shunyu Wang, Fang Zhang, Yingzhe Wang, Peipei Hu, Jie Liu, Xiaohua Ma, Yue Hao
abstract: The single-event burnout (SEB) is one of the great threats to gallium nitride (GaN) based device in aerospace applications. This study investigates the SEB mechanism in a millimeter-wave (mmW) power amplifier (PA) monolithic microwave integrated circuit (MMIC) with the linear energy transfer (LET) of 83.85 MeV <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\cdot $</tex-math> </inline-formula> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/mg. Experimental results show that catastrophic burnout occurs when the drain bias reaches 45 V. Focused ion beam and scanning electron microscopy (FIB-SEM) revealed that both metal-insulator-metal (MIM) capacitors and GaN high-electron-mobility transistor (HEMT) experience catastrophic damage. The burnout of the MIM capacitor originates from persistent impact ionization within the dielectric layer. This process leads to the formation of a conductive path, triggering an abrupt jump in the leakage current, which causes dielectric rupture and damage to the metal electrodes. For the GaN HEMT, the heavy ions’ strike generates a high density of electron-hole pairs along their trajectory. Driven by the lateral and vertical electric fields, holes migrate toward and primarily accumulate beneath the gate field plate, forming a localized positive charge region. This accumulation generates an extremely high local electric field, which exceeds the critical value that the AlGaN barrier layer can withstand. This study provides critical theoretical and experimental insights for assessing the reliability of mmW GaN MMIC against SEB in irradiation environments.

title: MorphiNet: A Graph Subdivision Network for Adaptive Bi-ventricle Surface Reconstruction
authors: Yu Deng, Yiyang Xu, Linglong Qian, Charlène Mauger, Anastasia Nasopoulou, Steven Williams, Michelle Williams, Steven Niederer, David Newby, Andrew McCulloch, Jeff Omens, Kuberan Pushprajah, Alistair Young
abstract: Cardiac Magnetic Resonance (CMR) imaging is widely used to personalize heart models for cardiac digital twin analysis because of its ability to visualize soft tissues and capture dynamic functions. However, CMR images have an anisotropic nature, characterized by large inter-slice distances and misalignments from cardiac motion. These limitations result in data loss and measurement inaccuracies, hindering the capture of detailed anatomical structures. In this work, we introduce MorphiNet, a novel network that reproduces heart anatomy learned from high-resolution Computed Tomography (CT) images, unpaired with CMR images. MorphiNet encodes the anatomical structure as gradient fields, deforming template meshes into patient-specific geometries. A multilayer graph subdivision network refines these geometries while maintaining dense point correspondence, suitable for downstream computational analysis. MorphiNet achieved the strongest overall trade-off in bi-ventricular myocardium reconstruction on CMR patients with tetralogy of Fallot, with 0.3 higher Dice score and 2.6 lower Hausdorff distance compared to the best existing template-based methods, while achieving comparable geometric accuracy to neural implicit function methods on CT data at 50× faster inference. Cross-dataset validation on the Automated Cardiac Diagnosis Challenge confirmed robust generalization, achieving a 0.7 Dice score with 30% improvement over previous template-based approaches. We validate our anatomical learning approach through the successful restoration of missing cardiac structures and demonstrate significant improvement over standard Loop subdivision. Motion tracking experiments further confirm MorphiNet’s capability for cardiac function analysis, including ejection-fraction estimates that correctly identify myocardial dysfunction in tetralogy of Fallot patients. Code and checkpoints are available at https://github.com/MalikTeng/MorphiNetV2.

title: A Two-Level Stacked Blumlein Pulse Generator Using Bipolar-Charged Modules With Integrated High-Voltage Isolation
authors: Hao Zhou, Chuangzhou Du, Yuxin Hao, Song Qiu, Qingxiang Liu
abstract: With the ongoing development of high-power microwave (HPM) technology, lightweight and compact pulsed-power generators have increasingly become a key enabling component at the system level. In this article, a novel two-level stacked Blumlein pulse generator (TL-SBPG) has been proposed as a promising solution for generating high-efficiency, highly reliable, and compact pulsed-power outputs. At the first stacking level, a bipolar-charged Blumlein pulse generation module (BC-BPGM) is developed as the fundamental discharge unit. Each BC-BPGM is formed by stacking two BPFNs charged with opposite polarities. Through topological optimization, the BC-BPGM achieves a doubled output voltage without requiring any additional isolation components. At the second stacking level, multiple BC-BPGMs are stacked using isolation common-mode inductors (CMIs) to realize high-voltage output summation. Owing to their common-mode isolation and strong magnetic coupling characteristics, the proposed CMIs simultaneously provide high-voltage isolation and isolated driver power delivery. The operating principles and design criteria of the TL-SBPG and the isolation CMI are analyzed in detail and validated through circuit simulations. Finally, a five-stage TL-SBPG prototype is developed, which successfully generates nanosecond pulses with an output voltage of 66.5 kV, a peak current of 1438 A, a pulsewidth of 79 ns, and a rise time of 22.7 ns. The prototype occupies a volume of 5.7 L with a total mass of 4.7 kg.

title: Cloud-Based Real-Time Vehicle Asset Positioning System Using the Internet of Things (IoT)
authors: Adrian Kuman Firmansah, Alif Ibnu Fathian, M. Andrian Topaz Firdaus, Reza Rendian Septiawan, Rogers Dwiputra Setiady, Surya Michrandi Nasution
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: This study talks about a system that helps track and manage vehicles using the internet. It uses GPS to find the car's location, and it can send information to the cloud, which lets people see where their vehicles are in real-time. The system is tested by checking how accurate the GPS is, how quickly it responds, and how long it can run on battery. Based on the testing result, the GPS is usually about 4.78 meters off in open spaces, it can respond in about 1.63 to 3.34 seconds, and it works well even when the internet signal changes. The system uses a small amount of power, which means it can work for a long time. Our study shows that this vehicle tracking system is reliable and can be used in modern transportation. This is the first study to really test a cloud-based system for tracking vehicles with features like geofencing, where drivers can set safe zones.

title: Accuracy Assessment of Static and Mobile 3D Laser Scanning Systems: A Comparative Study
authors: Rayya Putri Nufaisa, Khairul Nastiar, Fasya Choirunnisa, Asri Ria Affriani, Sagita Nuraini, Fajar Andi Sugiyanto
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Three-dimensional laser scanning (3D laser scanner) technology has experienced rapid advancement and is increasingly utilized in various fields, including geospatial mapping, architecture, heritage conservation, and construction, due to its capability to capture highly accurate spatial data. This research aims to analyze the data accuracy that performance from static and mobile Terrestrial Laser Scanner (TLS) devices. The research focuses on evaluating point cloud accuracy obtained from indoor studio scanning. The resulting accuracy data serve as a reference for selecting suitable devices based on project requirements, distinguishing between applications requiring high accuracy, such as historical building restoration, high-resolution Building Information Modeling (BIM), and structural monitoring, and those tolerating moderate accuracy, such as basic as-built surveys or general building documentation.The data was collected from the Leica RTC360 for static and the Leica BLK2GO for the mobile method. The analysis was conducted using the Cloud-to-Cloud (C2C) method, which enables direct comparison between point clouds without the need for external reference targets. The results indicate that the Leica RTC360 achieved a Cloud-to-Cloud Target Error of 0.002 m, whereas the Leica BLK2GO recorded an error of 0.008 m. Although both devices fall within the commonly accepted accuracy threshold for the general tolerance limit less than or equal to 0.010 m, the RTC360 is recommended for projects demanding high-detail accuracy, while the BLK2GO remains suitable for applications with less stringent accuracy requirements.

title: Glitch-Aware Optimization of 4-2 Compressor-Based Approximate Multipliers
authors: Tong Li, Tongjing Wu, Ao Liu, Honglan Jiang, Jianfei Jiang, Qin Wang, Zhigang Mao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: Approximate multipliers have widely been used in error-resilient applications with hardware-efficient and relaxed precision requirements, such as multimedia signal processing and deep learning. Aiming to achieve improvements in power efficiency and performance, approximate multipliers are generally designed by simplifying the implementation circuits. However, the redundant switching activities (also known as glitches) due to unbalanced signal paths are seldom considered, leading to significant dynamic power. Moreover, prior approximate designs often overlook transistor sizing optimization, which limits their potential for power–delay efficiency. This article proposes a custom design and optimization framework for approximately 4-2 compressors at the structural and circuit levels, which effectively reduces glitches by balancing output delays. Based on the devised approximate 4-2 compressors, the constructed multipliers can then achieve significant reductions in the generation and propagation of spurious activities. In addition, to further reduce the dynamic power consumption, a delay-aware signal routing (DASR) strategy is introduced for interconnecting approximate compressors. The stability and efficiency of the proposed designs under varying conditions are verified by extensive simulations. The experimental results on HLMC 28-nm CMOS technology show that the approximate 4-2 compressors obtained by the proposed framework achieve 10.4%–52.1% power–delay product (PDP) reductions compared to existing approximate designs with the same accuracy, resulting in 5.20%–33.1% PDP improvements for multipliers. Moreover, the proposed optimization framework is generalizable to arbitrary approximate 4-2 compressor designs.

title: On Estimating the Trace of Quantum State Powers
authors: Yupan Liv, Qisheng Wang
abstract: We investigate the computational complexity of estimating the trace of quantum state powers tr(ρq) for an n qubit mixed quantum state ρ, given its state-preparation circuit of size poly (n). This quantity is closely related to and often interchangeable with the Tsallis entropy Sq(ρ) = 1—tr(ρq)/q—1, where q = 1 corresponds to the von Neumann entropy. For any non-integer q≥ 1 + Ω(1), we provide a quantum estimator for Sq(ρ) with time complexity poly (n), exponentially improving the prior best results of exp(n) due to Acharya, Issa, Shende, and Wagner (ISIT 2019), Wang, Guan, Liu, Zhang, and Ying (TIT 2024)| Wang, Zhang, and Li (TIT 2024), and Wang and Zhang (TIT 2025). Our speedup is achieved by introducing efficiently computable uniform approximations of positive power functions into quantum singular value transformation. Our quantum algorithm reveals a sharp phase transition between the case of q = 1 and constant q > 1 in the computational complexity of the Quantum q-Tsallis Entropy Difference Problem (TsallisQED q ), particularly deciding whether the difference Sq(ρ0)−Sq (ρ1) is at least 0.001 or at most −0.001: • For any 1 + Ω (1) ≤ q ≤ 2, TsallisQED q is BQP-complete, which implies that Purity Estimation is also BQP-complete. • For any 1≤q≤1+1/n — 1, TsallisQED q is QSZK-hard, leading to hardness of approximating the von Neumann entropy because Sq(ρ) ≤ S (ρ), as long as BQPQSZK. The hardness results are derived from reductions based on new inequalities for the quantum q-Jensen–(Shannon–)Tsallis divergence with 1 ≤ q ≤ 2, which are of independent interest.

title: Neural Network Optimization Reimagined: Decoupled Techniques for Scratch and Fine-Tuning
authors: Xin Ning, Qiankun Li, Xiaolong Huang, Qiupu Chen, Feng He, Weijun Li, Prayag Tiwari, Xinwang Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: With the accumulation of resources in the era of big data and the rise of pre-trained models in deep learning, optimizing neural networks for various tasks often involves different strategies for fine-tuning pre-trained models versus training from scratch. However, existing optimizers primarily focus on reducing the loss function by updating model parameters, without fully addressing the unique demands of these two major paradigms. In this paper, we propose DualOpt, a novel approach that decouples optimization techniques specifically tailored for these distinct training scenarios. For training from scratch, we introduce real-time layer-wise weight decay, designed to enhance both convergence and generalization by aligning with the characteristics of weight updates and network architecture. For more importantly fine-tuning, we integrate weight rollback with the optimizer, incorporating a rollback term into each weight update step. This ensures consistency in the weight distribution between upstream and downstream models, effectively mitigating knowledge forgetting and improving fine-tuning performance. Additionally, we extend the layer-wise weight decay to dynamically adjust the rollback levels across layers, adapting to the varying demands of different downstream tasks. Extensive experiments across diverse tasks, including image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation, and instance segmentation, demonstrate the broad applicability and state-of-the-art performance of DualOpt. Code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/qklee-lz/OLOR-AAAI-2024</uri>.

title: Implementation of Machine Learning Models to Predict First-Year Student Achievement Based on Ethnicity and Entrance Test Scores
authors: Evander Banjarnahor, Budi Wibawanta, Ronald Belferik, Tia Aulia Putri Sadeva, Neneng Andriani
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: This study examines the influence of cognitive and sociocultural factors on first-year students’ academic achievement, emphasizing the role of ethnicity and ethnicity-related cultural values as predictive variables within a machine learning framework. The dataset includes 730 students from four cohorts (2021–2024) representing diverse ethnic backgrounds across Indonesia. Variables analyzed comprise ethnicity, ethnic score (encompassing Ethnic Social Connectedness, Perception of Social Acceptance, Cultural Connectedness, Cultural–Academic Value Conflict, and Cross-Ethnic Community Support), entrance test scores (Mathematics, Academic Potential Test, English, and Indonesian), and first-year Grade Point Average (GPA). Descriptive statistics and predictive modeling were conducted using Linear Regression and Ridge Regression across five data split ratios (70:30, 75:25, 80:20, 85:15, and 90:10). Results reveal disparities in entrance test scores among ethnic groups, but the GPA range remains narrow (2.93–3.33), suggesting convergence in academic outcomes and evidence of academic resilience in groups such as Dayak and Minahasa. The best-performing model (70:30) achieved stable performance, with a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.19 GPA points, a Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of 5.76%, and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values ranging from 0.137 to 0.263, indicating no signs of overfitting. Regression coefficients show that cognitive factors positively predict GPA, while sociocultural factors exert mixed effects social acceptance, cultural connectedness, and cross-ethnic support positively influence GPA, whereas ethnic exclusivity and cultural–academic value conflict have negative effects. Practically, this study contributes to Educational Data Mining and machine learning fairness by offering interpretable, equity-oriented insights for predicting academic achievement in multicultural higher education contexts.
