title: Semantic Search-Enhanced Policy Review Models Using Azure Cognitive Services and Power Apps
authors: Nataraja Kumar Koduri, Sarat Piridi, Satyanarayana Asundi, Srinivas Kamineni
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Traditional keyword-based compliance searches often fail due to the ambiguity and complexity of regulatory language, leading to inefficiencies and compliance risks. This research investigates the integration of semantic search and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) with Azure Cognitive Services and Microsoft Power Apps to modernize the policy review process. Using a qualitative multi-phase design, we analyzed diverse policy documents, conducted focus groups with compliance officers, and developed standardized indices to measure interpretability, transparency, efficiency, and stakeholder adoption. The findings show that semantic models improved interpretability by 124%, transparency by 53 points, and review efficiency by 42-68% compared to conventional keyword search approaches. These results demonstrate the potential of semantic AI, when combined with low-code platforms, to significantly reduce compliance burdens while enhancing usability, trust, and adoption across stakeholder groups.

title: Artificial Intelligence for Sustainable Distance Learning: Insights on Scalability, Ethics, and Cultural Adaptation
authors: Jovita Ridhani, Kadek Dwi Hendratma Gunawan, I Nyoman Tri Anindia Putra, Budi Tri Cahyono, I Putu Oktap Indrawan
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: The integration of artificial intelligence (AI) into higher education has accelerated since the COVID-19 pandemic. AI applications such as adaptive tutoring, generative feedback, predictive analytics, and chatbots have transformed distance learning environments. This review uniquely integrates scalability, ethics, and culture as interdependent pillars of sustainable AI in distance learning. This narrative review synthesizes peer-reviewed studies (2018–2025) from major databases, examining how AI advances sustainability in distance learning through these three interrelated dimensions. Evidence shows that AI can enhance personalization and institutional scalability when guided by established learning theories and pedagogical frameworks. However, scalability without ethical safeguards may exacerbate inequities, while culturally "neutral" systems risk marginalizing local languages and practices. The review identifies five cross-cutting lessons: the importance of theory-grounded design, global core–local control architectures, governance as instructional technology, learner agency as the basis of sustainability, and interoperability as a condition for equity. By foregrounding sustainability, ethics, and cultural responsiveness, this review reframes AI in distance learning as an ecosystemic challenge and offers a framework for fostering resilient, equitable, and context-sensitive educational futures. The findings provide design and policy guidance for developing equitable, scalable, and culturally adaptive AI systems in higher education.

title: High Rise Building Inspection Using Aerial Images from UAV and Photogrammetry
authors: Martinus Edwin Tjahjadi, Fransisca Dwi Agustina, Bernika Natasya Ifada, Putu Harry Gunawan, Hardianto, Ester Priskasari
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Demands for non-contact and remote dimensionality inspection of high-rise building structures have increased recently since it can meet safety concerns in construction sites. UAVs (Unmanned Aerial Vehicles) gain wider acceptance for this task. Aerial images captured by the camera mounted on the flying platform suffer geometrical instabilities due to lens elements defects. A perturbing lens during the flight mission deviates its axis from its original position, the center of the camera’s sensor plane. This situation is unfavorable when a 3D high precision measurement is demanding. This paper evaluates a photogrammetric method called self-calibrating bundle adjustment to assess its feasibility in performing precise 3D measurement for such camera usage. Using a vertically convergent flying path, captured aerial images are processed to calibrate the camera. Twelve physical calibration parameters are obtained and categorized based upon four groups of deflection sources namely variations in principal distance and principal point, radial distortion, tangential distortion, and affinity distortion. An established collinearity-based intersection adjustment is performed repeatedly incorporating these parameters to generate 3D coordinates of the inspected structures. It is confirmed that features of the building can be measured within sub centimeters of accuracy. A high-rise building inspection using UAV gives significant prospect in reliability of 3D measurements in the future.

title: Review on AI-enabled iOS Mobile Apps for Skin Disease Management
authors: Shahira Padinharepattel Mohamed, Fathima Farha, Tanvir Alam
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: The global burden of dermatological conditions and the increased reliance on mobile health technologies go hand in hand. The emergence of AI-powered dermatology applications on the mHealth platform has revolutionized skin health management. This review evaluates systematically selected 20 iOS dermatology applications’ functionalities, validation, and effectiveness. Key features assessed include the underlying AI models (if mentioned), the scope of different diseases covered, user experience, patient education, and privacy measures. Despite all the improvements in diagnostic efficiency for skin diseases brought by AI, only half of the apps had clinical validation, and just 30% had model validation on all skin types. This must be considered in relation to their applicability in diverse populations. Furthermore, 55% of the apps did not specify their data sources, and 45% did not specify the AI models used, which showed a lack of transparency on data usage. We observed privacy concerns of the apps as 10% of the studied apps use user financial information, 30% apps use usage data without linking to users. Overall, we believe this review emphasizes the urgent need for improved validation, transparency measures and robust regulatory frameworks to use AI-based mHealth dermatology tools safely. Our results offer valuable insights to developers, researchers, and policymakers regarding the reliability and inclusivity of AI-powered dermatology applications for skin health management. The complete data extraction table can be found in https://github.com/tanviralambd/SkinDiseaseIOS.

title: Bot Detection on Twitter Using Neural Network
authors: Muhammad Affiq Fikri, Fitriyani, Abdullah Hanifan
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Bots on social media platforms like X (Twitter) are often used to propagate fake news and manipulate public opinion, creating a significant threat to information integrity. This study seeks to address the problem of detecting and classifying bots from human accounts accurately. The presence of bots on social media has negative implications for society, particularly through the spread of misinformation and disinformation. Although machine learning techniques have been used for bot detection, advanced bots that imitate human behavior continue to pose challenges, especially when dealing with imbalanced datasets. This paper examined three deep learning architectures: Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), Recurrent Neural Network (RNN), and a Hybrid CNN-RNN model, using the twibot-22 dataset by analyzing user bio text and numerical features. The methodology implemented various text preprocessing steps, feature scaling, GloVe embedding, and a class weighting strategy for handling data imbalance. Based on the evaluation results, no model outperformed all others in every aspect. However, the hybrid CNN-RNN model achieved the best overall accuracy at 77.63% and bot precision of 31.71%. On the other hand, the standalone CNN model yielded better results in terms of bot recall at 59.72%, indicating its superior capability in identifying bots. This reinforced the idea that there are always trade-offs between models when dealing with extremely imbalanced classes.

title:
Selective-injection GaN Heterojunction Bipolar Transistors with 275 kA/cm
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    Current Density
authors: Zhanbo Xia, Chandan Joishi, Shahadat H. Sohel, Andy Xie, Md Saklain Morshed, Binzhi Liu, Jinwoo Hwang, Edward Beam, Yu Cao, Siddharth Rajan
abstract: We design and demonstrate selective injection GaN heterojunction bipolar transistors that utilize a patterned base for selective injection of electrons from the emitter. The design maneuvers minority carrier injection through a thin p-GaN base region, while the majority carrier holes for base current are injected from thick p-GaN regions adjacent to the thin p-GaN base. The design is realized using a regrowth emitter approach with SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> as a spacer between the emitter layer and the thick p-GaN base contact regions. The fabricated device demonstrated state-of-art output current density (I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</sub>, max) ~275 kA/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> with a current gain (β) of 9, and 17 for the planar HBT design (I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</sub>, max =150 kA/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>). The reported results highlight the potential of the selective injection design to overcome the persistent GaN HBT design tradeoff between base resistance and current gain, paving the way for next-generation radio frequency and mm-Wave applications.

title: Multi-feature Fusion of Non-contact Laser Generated Ultrasound and Electrochemical Data for In-situ Battery State Estimation
authors: Meiqi Shao, Gaolong Lv, Wei Feng, Xinyu Wu, Zhi-Xin Yang, Shifeng Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Accurate estimation of state of charge (SOC) and state of health (SOH) is critical for reliable battery operation monitoring. However, conventional voltage/current-based estimation methods face fundamental limitations due to their intrinsic voltage plateau and pronounced voltage hysteresis during fast operation (>3C). Non-invasive ultrasound sensing provides a promising solution for precise lithium-ion tracking, whereas conventional contact-based ultrasound techniques are limited by their reliance on coupling agents. This work therefore proposes a multi-feature fusion method that integrates non-contact laser generated ultrasound and electrochemical data, combined with machine learning algorithms, for in situ monitoring of battery SOC and SOH during high-rate cycles. A novel physics-informed, multi-kernel fusion hierarchical network for battery state estimation is proposed, which integrates electrochemical mechanism with Spearman correlation to construct a robust feature set from ultrasonic and electrochemical data. A dedicated multi-kernel architecture within a least squares support vector machine framework, where physically distinct features are processed through tailored kernels before fusion. The crested porcupine optimizer-enhanced hierarchical structure facilitates coherent, layered estimation of SOC and SOH, achieving RMSEs of 4.33% and 0.24%. To verify the robust generalization and stability of the proposed approach, extensive validation across diverse testing scenarios, including slow-charging (1C), cross-cell testing, and comparisons with other algorithms (random forest, long short-term memory, enhanced interpretable generalized additive model and extended Kalman filter) achieved superior accuracy in both SOC and SOH estimation. This multi-feature fusion strategy effectively circumvents the information limitations of single sensing modalities while establishing a novel paradigm for operando battery diagnostics. The in situ, non-contact nature and computational accuracy further suggest strong potential for industrial deployment in smart battery management systems.

title: A Dual-Polarized Leaky-Wave Antenna with Polarization Reconfigurability and Electronically Beam Scanning for Millimeter-Wave Applications
authors: Sheng Li, Zheng Li, Yi Yan, Junhong Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper presents a novel dual-polarized leaky-wave antenna (LWA) that integrates dual-channel operation, polarization reconfigurability, and electronically controlled beam scanning into a simple structure. The architecture consists of a radiating superstrate, a 90° hybrid coupler, and gap waveguides (GWs). Two rows of patch elements on the superstrate are excited through orthogonal slots etched on a shared ground. PIN diodes and their electronically controlled network are placed beneath the slots to tune the radiation state of each element. The 90° hybrid coupler, implemented via a metal pin array, is integrated into the GWs to form the feed structure. A binary hologram control method based on the holographic principle is proposed, allowing real-time switching of polarization states and beam directions through the manipulation of PIN diodes. As a result, the antenna supports simultaneous dual-channel operation with four pairs of reconfigurable orthogonal polarizations—right-hand/left-hand circular polarization (RHCP/LHCP), LHCP/RHCP, horizontal/vertical polarization (HP/VP), and VP/HP—with independent beam scanning over a 100° range in each polarization state. A prototype is fabricated and measured, validating polarization switching among all four states and beam scanning from –50° to 50°. Owing to its dual-channel operation, polarization reconfigurability, and beam-scanning capability, the proposed antenna is a promising candidate for millimeter-wave applications.

title: Quality Prediction of Embedded and Overlaid Text in User-Generated Visual Content
authors: Maniratnam Mandal, Neil Birkbeck, Balu Adsumilli, Alan C. Bovik
abstract: User-generated visual content (UGC) now occupies a significant fraction of internet traffic, and billions of UGC videos and pictures are uploaded daily. Among these, short-form video content now accounts for most of the videos consumed by online users. Given the popularity of short-form UGC content, being able to control the perceptual quality of UGC videos has emerged as an important problem. Visual UGC is subject to myriad types, severity, and combinations of distortions. While UGC video quality has been closely studied, the quality and legibility of text that is overlaid or embedded in short-form UGC videos has received relatively low attention. However, being able to accurately predict text quality in images is important, since it both impacts the overall perception of the content it is embedded in, as well as the messages being conveyed. It is also beneficial for applications involving image or video text recognition which can affect visual search and content identification. Analyzing the quality of text embedded in pictures or videos is a hard problem, since perception of it is commingled with the surrounding visual content. Our work, which greatly extends our early report on text legibility prediction, contributes to both the psychophysics of embedded text quality as well as to computational models of its perception. We have created two subjective datasets – designated as the LIVE-COCO Text Legibility (LIVE-COCO-TL) Database (a modification of COCO-Text), and the LIVE-YouTube Text-in-Video Quality (LIVE-YT-TVQ) Database. LIVE-COCO-TL contains 74,440 text patches with legibility annotations, while LIVE-YT-TVQ contains ∼ 19K subjective quality ratings on 405 videos and 641 text patches extracted from them.We build models that predict embedded or overlaid text legibility and text quality, as well as a multi-task model that simultaneously predicts the overall quality of videos with embedded or overlaid and local text quality. We are making the databases and all models freely available at https://live.ece.utexas.edu/research/LIVE YouTube_Text Quality Assessment/index.html.

title: Energy-Efficient Hybrid Data Computation via Coordinated AirComp and Edge Offloading
authors: Yudan Jiang, Xiao Tang, Jinxin Liu, Qinghe Du, Dusit Niyato, Zhu Han
abstract: The development of 6G networks brings an increasing variety of data services, which motivates the hybrid computation paradigm that coordinates the over-the-air computation (AirComp) and edge computing for diverse and effective data processing. In this paper, we address this emerging issue of hybrid data computation from an energy-efficiency perspective, where the coexistence of both types induces resource competition and interference, and thus complicates the network management. Accordingly, we formulate the problem to minimize the overall energy consumption including the data transmission and computation, subject to the offloading capacity and aggregation accuracy. We then propose a block coordinate descent framework that decomposes and solves the subproblems including the user scheduling, power control, and transceiver scaling, which are then iterated towards a coordinated hybrid computation solution. Simulation results confirm that our coordinated approach achieves significant energy savings compared to baseline strategies, demonstrating its effectiveness in creating a well-coordinated and sustainable hybrid computing environment.

title: Metaheuristic Approaches for the Tourist Trip Design Problem Using Real-World Data from Yogyakarta
authors: Saskia Putri Ananda, Adiwijaya Adiwijaya, Z.K.A. Baizal, Agung Toto Wibowo
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: The Tourist Trip Design Problem (TTDP) is a complex combinatorial optimization problem that focuses on generating personalized multi-day route (itineraries) for tourists while considering multiple constraints such as time windows, point of interest (POI) opening hours, travel durations, rating and budget limitations. This study presents a comprehensive comparative analysis of ten metaheuristic algorithms applied to solve the TTDP using real-world data from Yogyakarta, Indonesia. The evaluated algorithms include classical methods such as Genetic Algorithm (GA), Simulated Annealing (SA), and Firefly Algorithm (FA); swarm intelligence approaches such as Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), Artificial Bee Colony (ABC), Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO), and Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA); as well as evolutionary and recently developed nature-inspired algorithms such as Differential Evolution (DE), Battlefield Optimization Algorithm (BfOA), and Komodo Mlipir Algorithm (KMA). A dataset comprising 99 POIs and 50 hotels was collected from Google Maps and utilized to evaluate solution quality, computational efficiency, and convergence behavior of each algorithm in depth. The experimental results reveal distinct strengths and weaknesses among the algorithms, where some achieve higher route quality while others converge faster with lower computational costs, offering valuable insights into the performance, robustness, scalability, and real-world applicability of metaheuristic algorithms for tourist route recommender systems.

title: Designing Smart Offerings under Privacy Costs: A Platform-Based Product Strategy
authors: Yi Xia, Yifan Wang, Jiajun Chen, Liqun Wei
abstract: Internet of Things (IoT) platforms enhance suppliers' basic product by integrating smart features through efficient data collection and analysis. Although smart technology improves the consumer experience, it raises privacy concerns. Information asymmetry between suppliers and platforms regarding consumer privacy concerns affects decision-making of both parties. The paper proposes an analytical model in which the supplier sells the basic product directly to consumers while also providing it to the IoT platform at a wholesale price. The platform then enhances the product into smart version for resale to consumers. Two decision sequences were considered: platform-leading and supplier-leading. We find that decision timing and information structures interact significantly. Under the symmetric information about consumers' privacy concerns, both the platform and supplier are better-off for making decisions first. Interestingly, under asymmetric information, supply chain members can reach a consensus on the decision sequence if the smartness level is low and the probability of high privacy concern is moderate, or the smartness level is high and the probability of high privacy concern is low. Moreover, in platform-leading scenario, asymmetric information always benefits the supplier but harms the platform. However, in supplier-leading scenario, asymmetric information harms the supplier, while also harms the platform when the consumers' privacy concern is high level. Therefore, mastering both first-mover and information advantages simultaneously can hurt the platform.

title: Fiber HGNN: Heterogeneous Graph Neural Network for Fiber Tract Segmentation
authors: Cheng Wang, Wuyang Li, Xinyu Liu, Zhibin He, Yifan Liu, Jian Cheng, Yixuan Yuan
abstract: Fiber tract segmentation is crucial for clinical applications such as brain function interpretation and surgical planning. Existing methods typically adopt either a cortical-parcellation-based or fiber clustering approach, but fail to simultaneously integrate heterogeneous information (e.g., streamline shape, point position, anatomical priors). In this work, we propose Fiber HGNN, a novel heterogeneous graph neural network that explicitly models and integrates heterogeneous information of fibers for accurate fiber tract segmentation. We construct a heterogeneous graph comprising three types of nodes: streamline, fiber keypoint and anatomical region. Specifically, fiber keypoints are representative points sampled along each streamline to characterize local geometric features, while anatomical regions provide contextual priors derived from brain atlas. This design enables the network to jointly capture the complementary information of streamline shape, local geometry, and anatomical priors, thus facilitating the learning of more discriminative feature representations. To further leverage implicit anatomical connectivity, we design a Metapath-guided Heterogeneous Information Aggregation (MHIA) network. By analyzing the spatial relationships between streamline keypoints and anatomical regions, the heterogeneous graph is decomposed into anatomical subgraphs for each streamline. In each subgraph, heterogeneous information from metapath-linked nodes is aggregated to obtain the final fiber representation. We evaluate the effectiveness of our framework on the HCP105 and TractoInferno datasets. The experimental results demonstrate that our method significantly outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods. The source code is available at https://github.com/CUHK-AIM-Group/Fiber-HGNN.

title: Joint Optimization of Decoupled User Association, Trajectory and Phase Shift Design for RIS-Enabled UAV-Assisted ISAC Systems
authors: Chen Dai, Tianyu Wu, Guozi Sun, Yiping Zuo, Changyan Yi, Haotong Cao, Kun Zhu, Shahid Mumtaz
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)-assisted integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) system is envisioned as a promising design to improve the original ISAC performance. Nevertheless, due to UAV's inherent constraints, such as limited energy and radio resources, the expected ISAC performance assisted by the UAV may be deteriorated. In addition, system performance can degrade as the link between user equipment (UE) and its associated UAV changes. Confronted with these, we attempt to apply Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS) to enable the UAV-assisted ISAC system, by which each RIS's phase can be shifted to reflect the sense and communicate signals, thus improving the UAV's sensing and communication performance. Besides, downlink (DL)-uplink (UL) decoupling (DUDe) can be introduced to allow each UE to establish separate associations with distinct UAVs for DL and UL, further enhancing the transmission quality. Therefore, we investigate the joint DUDe association, trajectory and phase shift design, and beamforming for RIS-enabled UAV-assisted ISAC systems. A joint optimization problem is formulated to maximize the sum rate for both the sensing and communication. Then the joint problem is alternately decomposed into two subproblems: the beamforming design, and the optimization of DUDe association, trajectory, and phase shift design. Specifically, we adopt the successive convex approximation to achieve an optimal solution for the first subproblem. The latter is transformed into a partially observable Markov decision process (POMDP), with the strategy being determined through a distributed multi-agent deep reinforcement learning (MADRL) methodology. Additionally, a robust Random Environment Distribution (RED)-based proximal policy optimization (PPO) algorithm is developed to train the actor and critic networks within the MADRL framework. Simulation results show the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed approach by comparisons.

title: A High-Precision Reference-Free Relaxation Oscillator With Supply-Tracking Switching Threshold
authors: Chuanhang Shao, Min Song, Zhen Zhang, Jichao Sui, Ming Zhang, Yin Zhang, Zhangqing He, Meilin Wan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: Conventional relaxation oscillators typically rely on reference circuits to generate switching threshold voltages or charging currents, resulting in increased chip area and power consumption. This article presents a high-precision relaxation oscillator that eliminates the need for reference circuits. A holding capacitor is charged through a timing resistor, and the switching threshold voltage is directly derived from the power supply using a resistor divider. Since both the charging speed and the switching threshold voltage scale with <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V_{\!D\!D}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, their dependencies cancel each other out, resulting in an oscillation period determined solely by the time constant of the holding capacitor and timing resistor. Moreover, the tracking behavior of the switching threshold with respect to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V_{\!D\!D}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> further reduces the sensitivity of the comparator delay to supply voltage variations. A dual-path charge–discharge scheme is also employed to eliminate frequency deviations caused by digital logic delays and capacitor discharge time. Fabricated in a 110-nm CMOS process, the oscillator operates at 24.5 MHz with an active area of 0.026 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. Measurement results indicate a power consumption of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$160~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>W and a maximum frequency error of 1.88% across a temperature range from −55 °C to 125 °C, corresponding to a temperature coefficient of 140 ppm/°C. When <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V_{\!D\!D}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> increases from 3.0 to 3.6 V, the output frequency variation is 0.4%.

title: Emphasizing Domain Differences through Interactive-Augmented Prompts in Continual Audio-Visual Speech Recognition
authors: Dongjie Fu, Xize Cheng, Jingyuan Chen, Tao Jin, Zhongfei Zhang
abstract: Audio-Visual Speech Recognition (AVSR) has been studied for a long time in the literature. By leveraging the complementary information from both acoustic and visual modalities, this approach offers a promising solution for robust speech transcription. While recent AVSR models have achieved impressive performance on large-scale, uniformly distributed datasets, they often overlook the challenges posed by real-world scenarios—where data is collected across multiple sessions and environments, leading to significant domain shifts and heterogeneous distributions. Such heterogeneity can result in catastrophic forgetting and hinder the generalization ability of the conventional models. To bridge this gap, we introduce the Continual Audio-Visual Speech Recognition (CL-AVSR) problem, which formulates AVSR as a continual learning task. We establish a dedicated benchmark for CL-AVSR by designing three experimental scenarios that reflect real-world challenges: introducing varying background noise for the audio stream, degrading video quality for the visual stream, and dividing tasks by speaker characteristics to jointly affect both modalities. These scenarios systematically evaluate the model’s ability to adapt and retain knowledge across dynamic and non-stationary data streams. To address the unique challenges of CL-AVSR, we propose the Interaction-enhanced Multimodal Prompt learning (IMP) framework. IMP builds upon a pre-trained AV-HuBERT backbone and integrates task-relevant soft prompts with cross-modal and cross-task interactions, enabling efficient knowledge transfer from high-quality source domains to typical low-quality target domains with minimal parameter overhead. The interactive prompts facilitate fine-grained alignment and adaptation between modalities and tasks, while contrastive regularization further mitigates catastrophic forgetting. Furthermore, we devise a multi-modal prompt selection strategy that leverages clustering-based feature analysis, empowering the model to dynamically select optimal prompts for unseen data distributions during inference. Extensive experiments on the LRS2 dataset demonstrate that IMP achieves substantial improvements over strong baselines, setting new state-of-the-art performance in all CL-AVSR scenarios. Our results highlight the effectiveness of IMP in enhancing continual learning capabilities for AVSR, paving the way for more robust and adaptable multi-modal speech recognition systems in real-world applications.

title: Satellite-Driven Surveillance of Urban Water Infrastructure: Using SAR Imagery to Monitor and Detect Potential Pipeline Leakages
authors: Aysha Yusuf Almahmeed, Fatima Alasbool
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Water leakage in urban distribution networks poses a significant challenge worldwide, with losses reaching 30– 50% in some regions. In arid countries like the Kingdom of Bahrain, where water is primarily sourced through energy-intensive desalination, reducing non-revenue water is a national priority. Conventional ground-based surveys for detecting pipeline failures are costly, time-consuming, and spatially limited. This study investigates the feasibility of using Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery, specifically Sentinel-1 data, to detect and monitor potential pipeline leakages in Bahrain. Two temporal images, acquired before and after reported incidents, were processed in the Sentinel Application Platform (SNAP) through calibration, terrain correction, co-registration, and RGB composite generation. Validation was conducted using Bahrain’s District Metered Areas (DMA) and historical leakage incident records provided by the Electricity and Water Authority (EWA). The findings demonstrate that SAR imagery can effectively highlight anomalous zones associated with water leaks, with good spatial agreement to EWA’s records. While small-scale leaks remain difficult to capture due to resolution constraints, the study establishes a baseline methodology for integrating open-source SAR data with national infrastructure datasets. Recommendations are provided for future enhancements, including AI-driven anomaly detection, higher-resolution SAR missions, and the development of a national monitoring platform. Overall, the research highlights the potential of satellite-driven surveillance as a cost-effective and scalable approach for strengthening water security in Bahrain and similar arid environments.

title: Modeling AI-Enhanced e-WOM Effects on Consumer Loyalty in Post-Boycott Contexts: A SmartPLS Approach
authors: Rajiv Dharma Mangruwa, Han-Foon Neo, Dandi Yunidar, Zoyabelle Ratu Arbian
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Product boycotts driven by socio-religious issues have become a significant force in shaping consumer decision-making, particularly in Indonesia. The Semarang City was awarded as the top three of the national index for tolerance city in Indonesia. The study aims to develop an AI-based e-WOM and consumer loyalty model within the context of product boycotts driven by socio-religious issues in Indonesia, particularly in Semarang City, which ranks among the top three most tolerant cities nationally. Using a quantitative research approach, this study employs primary data collected from 400 respondents through online and offline Likert-scale questionnaires using purposive non-probability sampling. Data were analyzed using variance-based Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) with SmartPLS 4. The results indicate that consumer belief, perceived product quality, and religiosity significantly influence consumer attitudes, which in turn affect repurchase intention and consumer loyalty. Repurchase intention serves as a key mediator between attitudes and loyalty, while the moderating effect of AI-based electronic word-of-mouth (AI e-WOM) was not supported. This may stem from AI’s evolving role in shaping trustworthiness and the authenticity of online reviews. This study contributes to the boycott behavior literature by integrating socio-religious, marketing, and technological perspectives. It highlights the strategic importance for brands to align with consumer values, maintain credibility, and adapt to AI-driven digital ecosystems to sustain long-term loyalty amid socio-religious consumption shifts.

title: Hybrid of Spatial Ising Model and MSTBurr Distribution for Cardiac Ultrasound Imaging Segmentation
authors: Ulfa Siti Nuraini, Nur Iriawan, Kartika Fithriasari, Irhamah, I Ketut Alit Utamayasa
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Due to increased noise, cardiac ultrasound imaging is challenging. Their variable quality, characterized by speckle noise, low spatial resolution, and acoustic dropouts, makes ultrasound segmentation a complex and demanding task. To reduce noise in ultrasound imaging, this research proposed a segmentation approach that enhances model-based clustering. The combination of a non-symmetrical data distribution (MSTBurr) with spatial statistical-mechanical effects (the Ising model) is the primary contribution of this article. The model was developed to handle the laxity of the Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM), which used symmetric distributions and ignored pixel neighbors. Hybrid of Variational Inference and Gibbs Sampling in the MCMC framework is used to estimate the model using the Bayesian technique. Using the G-Mean and IoU with GMM and Bayesian Gaussian Mixture, MSTBurr-Ising performance was assessed, exceeding 70%. This article also used simulated data with varying noise percentages compared to other methods. The segmentation results show that segmentation using the MSTBurr-Ising model reduces noise better than Gaussian-based models. When it comes to cardiac ultrasound image data and simulation image data with different types of noise, MSTBurr-Ising performs better than GMM and Bayesian Gaussian Mixture. The results of this modeling hopefully can help paramedics treat patients more accurately by improving their interpretation of cardiac ultrasound images.

title: Mission Planning and Scheduling of Dual Pod Aerial Refueling for Heterogeneous Mixed Multi Receiver Formations
authors: ZIKANG SU, CHENYV LI, ZHUOLIN XING, JIALIANG FAN, XIANYONG JING, CHANGHUI JIANG
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Addressing the challenges of complex scheduling and trajectory coordination in dual-pod aerial refueling missions for heterogeneous unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) formations, this paper proposes a hierarchical refueling joint scheduling and collision-aware trajectory planning method based on Multi-Agent Proximal Policy Optimization (MAPPO). A scheduling model is first developed that incorporates task constraints across fighter aircraft, wingmen, and UAVs. The MAPPO framework integrates refueling time windows, fuel states, and platform types into the state representation to output refueling sequences and refueling point assignments, enhanced by priority weighting and time penalty mechanisms. At the trajectory level, Dubins curves and trajectory homotopy are combined to enforce temporal synchronization and spatial deconfliction, ensuring safe and punctual rendezvous. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms baseline algorithms, including IQL, QMIX, and VDN. Specifically, the average estimated convergence episodes are reduced by over 65%, representing a substantial enhancement in the operational efficiency of dual-pod aerial refueling systems.

title: Local Dimension Enhancement Representation Learning for Skeleton-Based Action Segmentation
authors: Shaofan Sun, Lilang Lin, Jiahang Zhang, Ling-Yu Duan, Jiaying Liu
abstract: Most existing self-supervised learning methods for skeleton-based temporal action segmentation (TAS) fail to capture the short-term motion semantics essential for dense framelevel prediction, as they typically learn representations that are either too coarse or motion-insensitive. This issue is reflected in local dimension collapse, which highlights the limitations of current approaches and suggests directions for improvement. Specifically, to address the issue of local dimension collapse for self-supervised learning in TAS, we propose the Local Dimension Enhancement (LoDE) framework, which introduces the local effective rank (LER) as a metric to measure and a learning objective to reduce this collapse. A new fine-grained representation scale, termed a motion unit, is defined as a temporal clip of consecutive skeleton frames to model skeleton data. Centered on this representation scale, we analyze existing methods (sequence-scale and frame-scale learning) with the tool of LER and theoretically demonstrate that introducing motion unit-scale learning is essential to alleviate local dimension collapse. Inspired by our theoretical insights, we design a multi-scale semantics module that integrates frame-, sequence-, and motion unit-scale learning, with LER-based regularization to enrich local representation diversity. These designs effectively alleviate local dimension collapse and lead to significant improvements in TAS, as evidenced by LoDE’s superior performance over state-of-the-art methods on three large-scale untrimmed datasets: PKUMMD, TSU, and BABEL. Our project website is available at https://carefreesun.github.io/LoDE TIP 2026/.

title: Real-Time Proximity Sensing for Autonomous Systems: Custom AFE and FPGA Acceleration for FMCW Architecture
authors: Yehyeon An, Jonghyun Kim, Seungju Lee, Jinwook Burm
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: In frequency-modulated continuous-wave (FMCW) light detection and ranging (LiDAR) systems, frequency modulation (FM) linearity and continuous-wave (CW) demodulation are the key factors determining overall performance. While most prior works focused on CPU-based linearization and decoding, this study presents a real-time FMCW architecture optimized for autonomous systems. While the system utilizes a generally robust distributed feedback (DFB) laser-based transmitter, it can be susceptible to temperature-induced frequency drift in dynamic autonomous environments. To address these fluctuations, an existing kernel-based nonlinearity tracking method was adopted and further extended into a coarse-to-fine framework for wide-range operation. The main design focus lies in the receiver, which includes a custom analog front-end (AFE) optimized for analog-to-digital converter (ADC) interfacing and multiply-accumulate (MAC) operations, along with a hardware-description-language (HDL)-based real-time decoding process. Experimental results in free space achieved a 0.15% error rate and 0.48-mm STD at 2 m, demonstrating the feasibility of the proposed architecture. In addition, an intensity measurement was conducted to verify the AFE performance.

title: ViT-UWA: Vision Transformer Underwater-Adapter for Dense Predictions Beneath the Water Surface
authors: Yuheng Jia, Qirui Lin, Hua Li, Yutong Li, Sam Kwong, Runmin Cong
abstract: Vision Transformer (ViT) and its variants have witnessed a significant success in computer vision. However, their performance may degrade in underwater dense prediction tasks due to challenges like complex underwater environments, quality degradation, and light scattering in underwater images. To solve this problem, we propose the Vision Transformer Underwater-Adapter (ViT-UWA), the first detail-focused and adapted ViT backbone for underwater dense prediction tasks, without requiring task-specific pretraining. In ViT-UWA, we first introduce High-frequency Components Prior (HFCP) to add high-frequency information of underwater images to the plain ViT, which can help recover and capture lost high-frequency information of underwater images. Then, we propose a Detail Aware Module (DAM) to obtain a detail-focused multi-scale convolutional feature pyramid, which can be used in kinds of dense prediction tasks. Through the ViT-DAM Cross Fusion (VDCF), we achieve bidirectional feature cross fusion between ViT and DAM. We evaluate ViT-UWA on multiple underwater dense prediction tasks, including semantic segmentation, instance segmentation, and object detection. With only ImageNet-22K pretraining, our ViT-UWA-B yields state-of-the-art 46.4 box AP and 44.2 mask AP on USIS10K dataset, which demonstrates the superiority of our method.

title: Probing Effective and Efficient Category-Level Articulated Object Pose Perception
authors: Li Zhang, Xianhui Meng, Liu Liu, Haonan Jiang, Jianan Wang, Rujing Wang, Cewu Lu, Jun Liu, Hong Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Category-level articulated object pose perception-encompassing both static pose estimation and dynamic pose tracking-is critical for embodied AI systems interacting with complex environments. Due to the inherent complexity and diverse motion structures of articulated objects, existing methods often exhibit limitations in adequately modeling kinematic constraints, handling self-occlusions, and meeting optimization requirements. Building upon EfficientCAPER [1], this work introduces CAPER++, a unified framework addressing these limitations through three key innovations: first, a joint-centric hierarchical model decomposes objects into a root part and constrained parts linked by joints, explicitly embedding kinematic constraints for geometrically consistent pose recovery. Second, an SE(3) manifold formulation leverages Lie algebra in the tangent space for singularity-free rotation representation and stable optimization, replacing error-prone direct regression. Third, for tracking, a proxy canonicalization strategy reformulates pose updates as SE(3) increment predictions relative to keyframes, enhanced by a dynamic keyframe mechanism to suppress drift. Extensive experiments on synthetic (ArtImage, PM-Videos), semi-synthetic (ReArtMix, ReArt-Videos), and real-world (RobotArm, RobotArm-Videos) benchmarks demonstrate state-of-the-art accuracy and robustness. CAPER++ achieves real-time inference (50 FPS) without post-processing, significantly advancing category-level articulated perception for real-world applications. Codes and datasets are available at project website: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://sites.google.com/view/caperplusplus</uri> <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://sites.google.com/view/caperplusplus</uri>.

title: Simulation and Experimental Validation of Nanoscale Air-Channel Transistors Using Ion Implantation
authors: Junxiang Yang, Ruihan Huang, Yuting Zhou, Dawei An, Yang Liu, Hao Jiang, Fan Yang, Feiliang Chen, Jiang Zhang, Mo Li
abstract: Nanoscale air-channel transistors (NACTs) are gaining significant attention for their potential in high-speed, high-frequency, and radiation resistance applications. However, challenges, such as a lack of saturation characteristics, as well as high gate leakage, have limited their applications in amplification circuits. Previous studies have demonstrated that ion implantation can achieve substantial saturation in NACTs by controlling electron concentration at the source. The ion-implantation parameters play a crucial role in this process, making a systematic investigation of their impact essential. In this work, we simulate and optimize both the ion-implantation conditions and the gate-dielectric thickness of the reported NACT, and propose an improved process flow. The optimized device delivers a drain current in the milliampere range and a cutoff frequency of 69.1GHz. Furthermore, we fabricated NACTs and compared the experimental data with the simulation results, confirming the reliability of the simulation model. This work provides practical guidelines for fabricating NACTs by ion implantation and lays a valuable foundation for their deployment in high-frequency amplification.

title: Digital Curation of Cultural Heritage Knowledge Through Crowdsourcing: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: I Made Wardana, Cokorda Rai Adi Pramartha
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Galleries, Libraries, Archives, and Museums (GLAM) are digitizing collections at unprecedented scale, making digital curation critical yet capacity-constrained. Crowdsourcing has emerged as a proven paradigm to mobilize the public for large-scale tasks—transcription, annotation, OCR correction, and georeferencing—while fostering participatory engagement. This systematic literature review synthesizes peer-reviewed studies from 2015–2023 across multiple databases (ACM Digital Library, Google Scholar), following PRISMA 2020 with a multi-stage screening and predefined inclusion/exclusion criteria. We analyze crowd tasks, quality-control strategies, volunteer motivation, and the expanding role of artificial intelligence (AI), including large language models (LLMs). The evidence indicates that crowd curation is mature and effective when layered with redundancy and expert review. We distill current practice, identify gaps, and outline a research agenda, proposing an AI-in-the-loop pipeline that balances efficiency with trust, provenance, and cultural sensitivity. Future success hinges on systems that deliver high-quality data and provide meaningful, sustained volunteer experiences while ethically navigating human–AI collaboration.

title: Do golden board secretaries enhance customer relationship stability? - Evidence from Chinese A-Share Firms_supp1-3683187.docx
authors: Xiuqi Jiang
abstract: Information asymmetry between firms is a significant factor influencing customer relationships stability (CRS) within the supply chain. As the legal information hub of the capital market, the board secretary plays a key role in shaping the enterprise's information environment. This paper explores the impact of golden board secretaries (GBS) on CRS using Chinese A-share listed companies as a research sample from 2007 to 2022. It finds that GBS significantly improved CRS, but its performance is highly dependent on information infrastructure. When the information disclosure system is weak, the GBS, as a “soft information infrastructure” driven by human capital, plays a substitute governance role; conversely, when the information transmission infrastructure is more developed (reflected in higher levels of digitization, geographic proximity, and marketization), the governance effect of the GBS is significantly amplified. Heterogeneity tests indicate that the GBS–CRS link is primarily observed in firms where board secretaries possess higher education, government experience, and stronger pay incentives. The expansion test confirms that this governance path ultimately increases enterprise value. This study breaks with the traditional paradigm of executive characteristics, revealing how governance effectiveness is shaped by enterprises' existing information infrastructure and offering a dual-governance-system perspective for understanding supply chain security.

title: The Shannon Upper Bound for the Error Exponent
authors: Sergey Tridenski, Anelia Somekh-Baruch
abstract: For the discrete-time additive white generalized Gaussian noise channel with a generalized input power constraint, with the respective shape and power parameters ≥ 1, we derive an upper bound on the optimal block error exponent. Explicit asymptotic upper bounds in the limit of a large block length n are given for three special cases: the Laplace noise channel and the Gaussian noise channel with the average absolute value constraint, and for the Laplace noise channel with the average squared value constraint. The derivation uses the method of types with finite alphabets of sizes depending on the block length n and with the number of types sub-exponential in n1.

title: An All-Digital Outphasing Transmitter IC for Ka-Band Bit-to-RF Concurrent Multibeam Digital Beamforming Array
authors: Jiazheng Zhou, Dong Wang, Hui Xu, Ningyuan Zhang, Xiaolei Su, Zhengkun Shen, Haoyun Jiang, Fan Yang, Yixiao Wang, Junhua Liu, Huailin Liao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This work presents the first fully integrated Ka-band all-digital outphasing transmitter-based IC for bit-to-RF multibeam digital beamforming (DBF) arrays. To enhance the design flexibility of digital systems operating at millimeter-wave (mmWave) frequencies, this work employs an outphasing architecture that facilitates signal digitization in the phase domain as opposed to the amplitude domain. The <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$LC$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-based injection-locked oscillator (LC-ILO) digital phase modulator (DPM) and the quadrupler are adopted to provide the constant envelope modulated signals. The proposed quadrupler lowers the operating frequency and reduces the phase shifting range to 90°, which allows the DPM to work in a better linear range. For high intrinsic linearity, a piecewise varactor array (PVA) linearization technique is used in the DPM of the DTX, and an rms phase error of 0.38°–0.75° is achieved in the 90° phase range under 6–8GHz. Furthermore, an all-digital phase-locked loop (ADPLL) is integrated to reduce the clock distribution complexity by operating from a high radio frequency (RF) to a reference frequency. With these techniques, a low-cost 24.5–30-GHz DTX is fabricated in 40-nm CMOS and occupies a compact core area of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.2\times 1.35$</tex-math> </inline-formula>mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. Abit-to-RF concurrent multibeamforming for three independent beams is demonstrated with a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1\times 8$</tex-math> </inline-formula> uniform linear array (ULA) demo. It achieves state-of-the-art normalized energy and area efficiency, consuming only 53mW per beam per element and 0.54mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> per beam per element.

title: Back-EMF Optimization of Hybrid Less Rare-earth Synchronous Reluctance Motor Using PSO Algorithm
authors: Ziyu Wang, Xiaolin Wang, Qixing Gao, Fanghua Zhang
abstract: Hybrid less rare-earth synchronous reluctance motors (HLR-SRMs) feature complex magnetic circuits that render back-electromotive force (back-EMF) optimization challenging, leading to torque ripple and reduced voltage utilization. This paper introduces an analytical model-based particle swarm optimization (PSO) scheme to minimize back-EMF harmonics by employing flux barrier angles as optimization variables. Analysis demonstrates that suppressing targeted orders of no-load slotless air-gap flux density harmonics is essential for enhancing back EMF performance. A simplified analytical model for the HLR SRM is developed to enable rapid calculation of the air-gap flux density. Leveraging the multi-segment distribution characteristics of the model's air-gap flux density, optimization targets the modulation of flux barrier angles to attenuate specific harmonics By minimizing dependence on finite element analysis (FEA), the proposed model-based PSO achieves efficient multi-step iterations while maintaining accuracy, thereby avoiding trapped in local optima and significantly reduces computational resource consumption. FEA and experimental validation on a prototype motor confirm the efficacy of the proposed algorithm in reducing the total harmonic distortion (THD) of the back-EMF.

title: Improved Deadbeat Predictive Current Control With Model-Aid Extended State Observer for CSI Fed PMSM
authors: Shaopeng Wu, Jiaqiang Yu, Bochao Du, Pinjia Zhang
abstract: The conventional deadbeat predictive current control (DPCC) for current-source inverter-fed permanent magnet synchronous motor (CSI-PMSM) deteriorates control performance when subjected to disturbances such as model parameter mismatch. Therefore, the model-aided extended state observer (MAESO) CSI-DPCC is proposed. First, a high precision discrete time CSI-PMSM prediction model is established, integrated with an ESO to combine model predictive capabilities with lumped disturbance estimation, thereby enhancing state variable prediction accuracy. The MAESO effectively enhances parameter robustness. Meanwhile, predicting known states through the prior model reduces the observer computational burden and increases the system control bandwidth. To eliminate the tracking error caused by disturbances, the system disturbance estimated by MAESO is fed back to the deadbeat model controller, which can estimate and compensate for internal and external disturbances in real time without requiring an additional integrator to eliminate steady-state errors. In addition, the parameter design method of MAESO-CSI-DPCC is proposed based on the Jury stability criterion for discrete systems. Finally, comparative simulations and experiments between the CSI-DPCC and the proposed MAESO-CSI-DPCC verify the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed strategy.

title: Automatic Labeling Using Generative AI in Educational Question Dataset
authors: Orvalamarva, Oktariani Nurul Pratiwi, Hanif Fakhrurroja
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: The increased use of artificial intelligence (AI) in education presents new opportunities for automating the cognitive-level labeling of test items. This study examines the capability of the Gemini 2.5 Pro generative model to automatically label junior high school science questions according to the Revised Bloom's Taxonomy. A total of 505 questions were collected from four online education platforms and processed through an iteratively optimized prompt design to minimize linguistic bias and ensure consistent cognitive mapping. The labeling results were evaluated using two approaches, namely manual validation of a 10% random sample and an inter-model consistency test between Gemini 2.5 Pro and Gemini 2.5 Flash. The results showed a high level of agreement, with an agreement rate of 88% in manual validation and 94% in inter-model consistency testing, indicating the model's reliability in interpreting the cognitive dimensions of educational questions. However, minor discrepancies were found in the transitions between levels C1 (Remembering), C2 (Understanding), and C4 (Analyzing) due to semantic ambiguity. This system demonstrates strong potential as a pre-annotation tool in educational dataset processing, making significant contributions to the integration of Gemini inter-model consistency analysis and the prompt-driven labeling approach, which has not been widely explored in the educational domain. Nevertheless, this study is still limited by the size of the dataset and the number of single validators; further research is recommended to expand the question corpus, involve multiple raters, and measure Inter-Rater Reliability (IRR) to strengthen the validation of results.

title: A Lightweight Neural Network for Pipeline Flow Rate Measurement Using Distributed Acoustic Sensing
authors: Ruiqing Tang, Yi Shi, Fei Liu, Xin Huang, Isaack Kamanga, Huayang Lv, Tong Zhou, Guozhen Tan, Guo Zhu, Hao Zeng, Yuanyuan Li, Xian Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Distributed acoustic sensing (DAS) has proven to be a revolutionary technology for the applications in the oil and gas industry, including leak detection, pattern recognition and pipeline flow rate measurement. However, existing DAS recognition methods prioritize accuracy through computationally intensive algorithms, overlooking stringent hardware constraints in field-deployable systems. To address this gap, we propose LAMNet—a lightweight neural network for efficient flow rate measurement. This novel approach combines the Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) with Depthwise Separable Convolution (DSC) to enhance feature extraction capability from DAS data while preserving a lightweight model architecture. Ablation study validates the optimal architecture, and comparative experiments with classical networks confirm state-of-the-art performance.With a compact size of 1.568 MB, LAMNet achieves real-time processing at 5.471 ms per sample on a commercial GPU (NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060), enabling efficient edge deployment. In pipeline flow rate measurement, the model achieves a relative prediction error of 0.110, a coefficient of determination of 0.864, and an average relative uncertainty of 0.442%. Furthermore, comparative experiments with multiple existing pipeline flow rate measurement methods demonstrate that our approach achieves the state-of-the-art overall performance. The LAMNet-enabled smart DAS for pipeline flow rate measurement represents a significant breakthrough, providing fast and accurate pipeline flow rate measurement. This advancement is expected to have a substantial impact on DAS applications. Code will be available at https://github.com/OpticalLab/LAMNet.

title: Comprehensive Analysis of an Isolated Boost Converter Based on the Converter PWM Switch (CPS) Concept
authors: Chen Chen, Cheolhwan Kim, Mafu Zhang, Alex Q. Huang
abstract: A new pulse width modulation (PWM) switch concept, referred to as the converter PWM switch (CPS), has recently been introduced in the literature. Unlike traditional semiconductor switches, the CPS employs a power converter as a controllable PWM switch while maintaining the power-transfer capability. By replacing one of the semiconductor switches in a traditional boost converter with an isolated CPS, a new CPS-based isolated boost converter (CPS-IBC) is created, which can achieve zero voltage switching (ZVS), a wide voltage conversion range and high efficiency. This article presents the first comprehensive analysis of the ZVS conditions for the proposed CPS-IBC and introduces a rms current estimation method, both of which enhance the understanding and practical implementation of CPS-based converters. Building upon these analyses, an improved modulation scheme is proposed to reduce conduction losses and further enhance the overall efficiency of the CPS-IBC. A 10 kW CPS-IBC prototype is developed to validate the topology and demonstrate the benefits of the proposed modulation. The experimental results highlight the strong potential of the CPS-IBC for high-performance isolated power conversion applications.

title: Towards Generalizable Lithographic Hotspot Detection—An Asynchronous Meta-Learning Approach Using One Layout Sample for Adaptation
authors: Cong Jiang, Yujia Wang, Dan Feng, Haoyu Yang, Kang Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: As circuit feature sizes continue to scale down in advanced technology nodes, layout printability has become increasingly challenging, making lithographic hotspot detection an ever more difficult task in computer-aided design (CAD) flows. In recent years, deep learning has emerged as a key enabler in identifying lithographic hotspots, with numerous studies reporting encouraging results. However, neural networks can become prone to bias and overfitting, especially when trained on limited data—a common crux in the CAD domain. A generalizable deep learning (DL)-based hotspot detector should learn the genuine lithography principle and ensure consistent accuracy across layouts from various designs at the same technology node, regardless of their varying design styles. However, we find that existing convolutional neural network (CNN)-based hotspot detectors fail to generalize to different circuit layouts other than the design for which they were trained. To this end, we propose a few-shot learning-based framework for generalizable CNN-based hotspot detection. We propose a meta-learning scheme that asynchronously updates the CNN feature extraction and classification components to obtain a meta-initialized model that can quickly adapt to new designs using as few as one training layout clip. Specifically, following a label-free layout feature-based clustering, we develop an accuracy estimation method that selects the most accurate meta-initial model for new designs. We additionally introduce a geometry-based sampling strategy that selects representative layout clips for few-shot adaptation, thereby enhancing generalization stability. Experimental results on the ICCAD 2012 and 2019 datasets demonstrate that our framework offers superior generalization capabilities compared to prior art on unseen new designs. This paper extends our previous work [1].

title: Scenario-Based Evaluation of Hybrid MINLP–Metaheuristic Model for Adaptive Logistics Routing in E-Retail Networks
authors: Kristian Telaumbanua, Syahril Efendi, Poltak Sihombing, Maya Silvi Lydia
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: This study presents a hybrid optimization framework that integrates Mixed-Integer Nonlinear Programming (MINLP) with Feasible Neighborhood Search (FNS) to solve the Location-Inventory-Routing Problem (LIRP) in multi-echelon e-retail logistics networks. The proposed model simultaneously determines facility activation, inventory distribution, customer assignment, and vehicle routing by decomposing decision spaces into continuous and discrete variables. Generalized Reduced Gradient (GRG) optimization is applied to manage continuous flows and inventory levels, while FNS dynamically explores feasible solutions for binary and routing decisions through local search. The framework is evaluated using a scenario-based approach under five different operational disruptions, including demand surges, fleet reduction, and tighter delivery windows. Results demonstrate that the model consistently achieves high service level fulfillment (above 90%) and fleet utilization (up to 99.8%) while maintaining computational efficiency with convergence times below four minutes, even under compound stress scenarios. The integration of GRG and FNS enables rapid adaptation to dynamic logistics conditions, making the model suitable for near-real-time applications. Additionally, the proposed approach supports proactive logistics planning by offering quantifiable trade-offs between service performance and operational cost. This research contributes both theoretically and practically by bridging adaptive metaheuristic optimization and operational decision-making under uncertainty, providing a scalable solution for e-retail logistics systems operating in increasingly volatile environments.

title: Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis of blu Application Reviews Using IndoBERT
authors: Robby Kishan Pardamean, Bunyamin Bunyamin, Hasmawati Hasmawati
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: The growth of digital banking in Indonesia produces extensive user feedback that provides valuable insights for enhancing service quality and improving overall customer experience. However, most sentiment analysis research focuses only on overall polarity, offering limited understanding of user opinions toward specific application features and performance aspects. This study applies Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis (ABSA) to reviews of blu, a mobile banking application, using IndoBERT, a Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) based model pre-trained on large-scale Indonesian corpora. A total of 10,159 user reviews were collected, carefully inspected, and manually labeled for both aspect and sentiment categories, then processed for classification across three major aspects: Feature, App Interface, and User Experience. Two configurations of IndoBERT were evaluated, where in the non–fine-tuned model only the classification head was trained while transformer layers remained frozen, and in the fine-tuned model all parameters were updated during training. Experimental results show that the fine-tuned IndoBERT achieved up to 99% accuracy in aspect classification and F1-scores of 84%, 96%, and 94% for sentiment classification, indicating effective learning and strong generalization capability. These findings confirm the effectiveness, robustness, and adaptability of IndoBERT for ABSA in Indonesian-language mobile banking reviews, demonstrating its potential for deeper interpretation of user perceptions and for supporting data-driven improvements in digital financial applications.

title: From Language to Prototype: An End-to-End Inverse Design Framework for Diplexers in Mobile Communication Using Multifidelity Learning
authors: Kaiyuan Jin, Siyuan He, Jingwei Zhang, Mingkai Wang, Yiheng Wang, Ting Zhang, Enze Zhu, Hao Xie, Zhun Wei, Wen-Yan Yin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: The inverse design of practical diplexers poses significant challenges due to strong electromagnetic (EM) nonlinearity, computationally expensive full-wave validation, and the intrinsic one-to-many mapping from performance specifications to manufacturable circuits. This work presents a unified, end-to-end framework that integrates a multifidelity forward–inverse surrogate modeling strategy, a specification-to-curve generation module, and a large language model (LLM) to translate natural-language requirements directly into manufacturable diplexers for mobile communication. A multifidelity training approach transfers physical trends learned from rapid circuit-level simulations to high-accuracy full-wave behaviors using limited high-fidelity samples. The forward surrogate model is employed to rapidly evaluate and filter inverse-generated candidates by predicting EM responses, ensuring consistency with target specifications prior to verification. This workflow reduces design turnaround from hours of manual optimization to minutes of automated inference and supports rapid batch design via component replacement without layout modification. The proposed framework is further examined under different topologies, operating frequency bands, substrate configuration, and enlarged parameter space, demonstrating its broader applicability beyond a single diplexer setting. Validated through full-wave simulation and experimental characterization of multiple fabricated diplexer cases, the designs demonstrate close agreement between simulated and measured results. Beyond accuracy, this framework enhances automation and human–machine interaction, offering a scalable pathway toward specification-driven RF component design.

title: 6G Cross-Band Wireless Channel Measurements and Similarity Metrics in Six Domains
authors: Zihao Zhou, Cheng-Xiang Wang, Jie Huang, Lijian Xin, El-Hadi M. Aggoune
abstract: Cross-band collaborations are poised to play a pivotal role in the sixth generation (6G) wireless communications, offering a promising approach to enhance spectrum efficiency and support heterogeneous networks integration. The centimeter wave (cmWave) and millimeter wave (mmWave) bands are characterized by their superior propagation characteristics and abundant spectrum resources, with their collaborative integration demonstrated to effectively extend coverage and improve system capacity. However, the performance of such collaborative frameworks critically depends on cross-band channel similarity, which necessitates an in-depth investigation. This paper conducts comprehensive cross-band channel measurements and similarity analysis across 7, 10, 13, 26, 28, and 39 GHz bands. Multiple similarity metrics are proposed to evaluate cross-band channel similarity in spatial/angular, frequency/delay, and temporal/Doppler domains. These metrics include the F-eigen ratio, correlation coefficient of angular/delay/Doppler power spectral densities (PSDs), angular/delay/Doppler power ratio, and ratio of coherence bandwidth/stationary time. Results demonstrate that significant cross-band channel similarity can be observed when the frequency separation is below 10 GHz. Furthermore, a comprehensive similarity metric is developed by integrating all individual metrics, offering a unified perspective on cross-band channel similarity. This index provides valuable insights into determining the frequency separation threshold that enables practical cross-band collaborations.

title: Intelligent UAV Surveillance Networks With Edge-Assisted Execution of Computer Vision Tasks
authors: N. Varshney, C. Puligheddu, C. Casetti, S. De, C. F. Chiasserini
abstract: Developing an edge-assisted UAV surveillance system that ensures low-latency, high-accuracy execution of computer vision tasks while optimizing energy consumption and network resources remains a complex challenge. In this paper, we address the limitations of existing research by leveraging image semantics, image ensemble processing, and mmWave UAV-edge channel statistics. We do so by focusing on joint optimization of UAV speed, camera images per second (IPS) rate, offloading policy, and transmission rates with the aim to minimize the UAV's energy consumption. Given the NP-hardness of the problem, we propose an algorithmic solution, named Intelligent UAV Network (IntUNe), which is based on an innovative constrained reinforcement learning strategy that dynamically and effectively adjusts to real-time conditions. Our results demonstrate that, in delay-constrained UAV surveillance networks, IntUNe closely matches the optimum in small-scale scenarios, and it increases inference accuracy by significantly reducing violations of the accuracy constraint by up to 96.19% compared to state-of-the-art alternatives. Also, it reduces UAV propulsion energy consumption by 34.67% and total UAV energy consumption by up to 37.3%.

title:
12-inch Bottom-Pinned VCMA-MRAM Devices with 0.6 ns Switching Speed, 30 fJ/bit Write Energy, and 10
                    <sup>14</sup>
                    Endurance
authors: Puyang Huang, Qingyuan Shu, Zheng Zhu, Shan Yao, Albert Lee, Chin-Chung Chen, Di Wu, Xufeng Kou, Kang L. Wang
abstract: This letter reports high-performance VCMA-MRAM devices fabricated on 12-inch wafer. By leveraging a bottom-pinned MTJ stack and synthetic antiferromagnet engineering, the optimized structure reduces stray field to 1.5 mT, enabling highly symmetric switching with a VCMA coefficient of 71.2 fJ/V·m. Meanwhile, a thicker MgO barrier enhances device robustness and endurance exceeding 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">14</sup> cycles. Moreover, the devices sustain fast voltage-driven switching down to 0.6 ns while maintaining a low write energy of 30 fJ/bit, surpassing prior state-of-the-art MRAM counterparts. Our results establish bottom-pinned VCMA-MRAM as a promising candidate for ultra-fast, energy-efficient, and high-endurance embedded non-volatile memory.
