title: A Comparative Study of Deep Learning Models for Breast Cancer Classification on Ultrasound Image
authors: M Tsani Faishal Azhar, Surya Michrandi Nasution, Inung Wijayanto, Saichand Raghupatrini
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Breast cancer remains one of the most critical global health challenges, established as a leading cause of death among women, which profoundly emphasizes why early detection is crucial not only for enhancing survival rates but also for ensuring significantly better overall patient outcomes. The pursuit of enhanced diagnostic efficiency has led to extensive research into Computer-Aided Diagnosis (CAD) systems, frequently leveraging deep learning methodologies to classify its conditions from medical images. However, despite the considerable development of CAD systems, current methods often suffer from critical limitations, including suboptimal classification accuracy, a lack of interpretability that can erode clinical trust, and the use of ineffective feature extraction methods. To empirically observe and address these limitations, this study conducted a rigorous comparative analysis of four Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architectures: DenseNet121, ResNet50, EfficientNetB0, and InceptionV3 to determine the most optimal model for breas cancer classification using ultrasound images. It was evaluated for classification using a dataset of ultrasound images obtained from Baheya Hospital, with performance measured based on validation accuracy and validation loss. The results demonstrated a clear superiority, as DenseNet121 significantly outperformed other models, achieving an excellent validation accuracy of 96.40% and a notably low loss value of 0.1992. This performance is particularly striking when compared to InceptionV3, which has an accuracy of 90.97% and a validation loss (0.3782) which nearly twice that of DenseNet121, highlighting its superior predictive stability. Conversely, ResNet50 yielded the weakest result, obtaining an accuracy of only 52.17%. These compelling findings robustly confirm that DenseNet121 is the most optimal and robust model for accurate breast cancer classification using ultrasound images.

title: Comparative Study of BiLSTM and XGBoost for Twitter Bot Detection
authors: Jordy Rafif Putra Alwi, Fitriyani Fitriyani
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Large numbers of automated accounts, or social bots, on Twitter undermine the reliability of conversations, distort the flow of information, and may influence public opinion. Identifying these accounts is difficult because bot developers continually refine them to look and behave more like real human users, both in their activity patterns and in the language they use. This work examines Twitter bot identification by evaluating and contrasting two contemporary classifiers: Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks and the Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) algorithm. Both models are trained on an account-level representation constructed from the Cresci-2017 benchmark dataset, which contains thousands of genuine users and social spambots labeled at the account level. A unified preprocessing and feature-extraction pipeline is applied so that the comparison focuses on model architecture and feature representation. Many prior works assess deep neural networks and boosted-tree models using separate preprocessing pipelines and experimental setups, which makes their relative advantages hard to compare fairly. In this study, the BiLSTM relies only on tweet text represented as sequences of tokens, whereas XGBoost is trained on a combined feature space that joins TF–IDF vectors of aggregated tweets with profile and behavioral attributes at the account level. Experimental results on a held-out test set show that both models achieve strong performance, with XGBoost reaching 98.69% accuracy, 98.87% F1-score, and 99.92% ROC-AUC, and BiLSTM achieving 97.91% accuracy, 98.20% F1-score, and 99.67% ROC-AUC. Confusion-matrix analysis confirms that both models maintain a very low false-positive rate, while XGBoost exhibits slightly fewer false negatives. These findings indicate that deep sequence models effectively capture linguistic cues, but tree-based ensembles exploiting structured metadata can provide a modest yet consistent advantage for Twitter bot detection on Cresci-2017.

title: From Pixels to Portions: Deep Learning-Based Segmentation and Depth Estimation for Measuring Food Leftovers Percentage in Hospital Meals
authors: Baghas Rizaluddin, Yuita Arum Sari, Sigit Adinugroho
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Food leftovers in hospitals are a critical issue, directly impacting the quality of patient nutrition abd the efficiency of the hospital operating budget. This study proposes an automated framework to estimate the percentage of food leftovers from patient meals using deep learning-based image analysis. The proposed method utilizes the combination of Mask R-CNN model for object pixel-wise segmentation and Depth Anything V2 for monocular depth estimation, with the calculation of leftovers based on before and after object volumes. The dataset used in this study, referred to as Le-Food dataset, consists of manually captured food images in a controlled environment to maintain uniform conditions. Experimental results show that deeper backbones improve accuracy but require a higher amount of computational process, ResNet-50 + Depth Anything V2 VIT-L combination achieved the lowest Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 10.516% with an inference time of 6.275s per pair. However, a lighter model, ResNet-50 + Depth Anything V2 VIT-S, offered a more practical compromise, producing a competitive MAE of 10.987% at 4.878s, the fastest combinations among all. These findings demonstrate that an objective, automated approach to monitoring food waste is viable. The proposed framework contributes to the improvement of hospital nutritional services and supports a data-driven solution in dietary planning for hospital patients. In addition, this approach can be generalized to other healthcare or food services where volume estimation is essential.

title: Eye Tracking-Based Autism Detection: Machine Learning Approaches and Clinical Translation Potential
authors: Muhammad Firly Feisal Abdigusna, Ridwan Wicaksono, Sunu Wibirama
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a neurodevel-opmental condition that requires early detection for optimal intervention outcomes. Eye tracking technology combined with machine learning offers a non-invasive and objective approach to ASD detection. However, the optimal combination of eye tracking features, stimuli types, and machine learning methods remains unclear. To address this scientific gap, this systematic literature review analyzed 20 peer-reviewed studies (from 270 initial records screened) published between 2020-2024. Our analysis revealed that image-based stimuli achieved the highest accuracy (93.45%), scanpath features outperformed other eye tracking metrics (90.46%), and hybrid machine learning approaches demonstrated superior performance (95.71% mean accuracy) compared to traditional methods (80.40%). Notably, 50% of the studies achieved clinical-grade performance (≥ 90% accuracy), with top-performing reaching 98% accuracy. However, clinical translation requires external validation on independent datasets, standardized protocols for cross-site reproducibility, and prospective trials in real-world settings. Furthermore, integrating interpretable models with multimodal biomarkers is essential for clinical acceptance and regulatory compliance. This study provides evidence-based recommendations for optimal feature-stimuli-method combinations and identifies critical research gaps that must be addressed for successful clinical implementation.

title: Block Tomlinson–Harashima Precoding and BER Analysis for MIMO-OTFS Systems
authors: Heng Liu, Mingjun Zhang, Yi Zhou, Yue Xiao, Zheng Ma, Pingzhi Fan, Erdal Panayirci
abstract: This article proposes a novel block-level Tomlinson-Harashima precoding (THP) scheme for spatial interference management in multiple-input multiple-output orthogonal time frequency space (MIMO-OTFS) systems. By constructing a causal equivalent channel representation, our approach enables the pre-cancellation of the interference at transmitter side to achieve inter-symbol interference-free transmission, thereby simplifying the architecture at the receiver to a linear equalizer with significantly reduced complexity. Next, the closed-form of bit error rate (BER) is investigated based on the statistical distributions of the noise enhancement factor. Simulation results verify the accuracy of derived BER expressions via Monte Carlo and demonstrate that the proposed scheme achieves superior performance at moderate-to-high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) regimes when compared to the benchmark schemes.

title: Transient Thermal Analyses of the MPEX Upstream Dump for Loss-of-Coolant Events
authors: Vimal Ramanuj, Adam Aaron, Aftab Hussain
abstract: The Materials Plasma Exposure eXperiment (MPEX) is a facility enabling the study of the effects of fusion reactor-like plasma conditions on materials. The system experiences high heat flux primarily by radiation from the plasma, which makes the design of plasma-facing components (PFC) critical. Upstream dump is a heat exchanger intercepting the plasma, which is designed considering its thermo-mechanical stability under steady-state operation. In this work, we focus on the thermal response of the component in a loss-of-coolant event. Thermal analyses have been performed considering two major scenarios: 1) plug on the downstream side of the cooling system leading to loss of flow and 2) break in the inlet pipe resulting in pressure loss. The key objective of the analyses is to estimate the equilibrium state of the upstream dump and the response times under both these loss-of-coolant events. Multiphase computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulations are performed to elucidate the coolant characteristics namely, its temperature and phase-change evolution to identify the limiting timescales in each event.

title: Analysis of Power-Domain NOMA for Uplink UAV-HAP Communications in Sub-6 GHz Band
authors: M. Comisso, F. Vatta, G. Buttazzoni, F. Babich
abstract: This paper presents a theoretical model to estimate the coverage probability and a semi-analytical model to compute the throughput of a power-domain non-orthogonal multiple access (PD-NOMA) scheme applied to an aerial network operating in the sub-6 GHz band. The presented framework investigates an uplink scenario in which multiple unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) contend for channel access on a PD-NOMA basis to deliver information towards a common destination, represented by a high-altitude platform (HAP). The analysis is developed in a realistic propagation environment by accounting for path-loss attenuation, small-scale fading, airframe shadowing, random waypoint mobility, multi-lobe antenna patterns, polarization losses, and pointing errors. Closed-form formulas are derived for the cumulative distribution function (cdf) of the power received by the HAP from the single UAV, and simple integral expressions are obtained for the corresponding coverage probability by exploiting the properties of order statistics. The theoretically evaluated cdfs are then integrated into a numerical framework to explore the impact of the channel parameters, of the modulation order, and of the residual interference on the throughput of a PD-NOMA UAV-HAP network. The accuracy of the model and the impact of the introduced approximations are assessed through extensive Monte Carlo simulations.

title: A Data-Driven Trading Strategy for Wind-Solar-Battery Microgrids in the Spot Electricity Market
authors: Jiwen Rao, Weilong Liu, Debin Fang, Qiao Peng
abstract: The integration of renewable energy sources, such as wind and solar power, into microgrids presents challenges owing to their intermittent output and the volatility of spot markets. This paper proposes a multi-energy complementary operation mode for wind-solar-battery microgrids, enabling engagement in day-ahead and ancillary service markets to optimize trading amid output uncertainties. We develop a machine learning-based optimization framework: Gaussian Mixture Models (GMM) with K-means clustering produce representative seasonal scenarios for wind and solar generation to capture stochastic variations; Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks forecast electricity prices to model temporal dynamics. These inputs inform a Mixed Integer Linear Programming (MILP) model that maximizes microgrid profitability while ensuring a series of constraints on microgrid operations and electricity market transactions. An empirical evaluation is conducted using real operational data, meteorological records, and market prices from the Guangdong spot electricity market in China. We employ a comprehensive validation framework including out-of-sample evaluation of the LSTM price forecaster, clustering-based scenario generation preserving real-world renewable variability, and systematic sensitivity analyses across prediction errors among 5% to 10% and imbalance price coefficients among 1.05 to 1.35. The main findings reveal: (1) The data-driven operation mode provides feasible and reliable supply by coordinating energy storage and ancillary market procurement, supported by the LSTM model that achieves high prediction accuracy with a mean absolute error of 0.0093 on a 20% holdout test set; (2) The microgrid exhibits pronounced seasonal characteristics, requiring differ entiated trading strategies across seasons; (3) The imbalance price in the ancillary service market critically shapes microgrid profitability, with profits peaking around an imbalance price coefficient of 1.2 and displaying an inverted U-shaped response.

title: MF-DETR: A Feature Fusion Network for SAR Ship Detection Based on Electromagnetic Scattering Features and Deep Features
authors: Wenqiang Hua, Ruizhe Wang, Chen Ding, Yizhuo Dong, Zhe Meng, Hu Cui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Convolutional neural network (CNN)-based methods have been widely employed in recent years for ship target detection in synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images. However, most existing studies adopt optical image-oriented network architectures without fully considering the inherent characteristics of SAR targets, leading to limited detection accuracy and stability. Furthermore, the coexistence of complex background clutter interference in inshore scenarios and the foreground-background class imbalance in offshore scenarios poses significant challenges for SAR ship detection. To address these issues, we propose a multifeature fusion DETR network (MF-DETR) for SAR ship detection that synergistically integrates electromagnetic scattering characteristics with deep learning frameworks. The proposed method utilizes a dual-backbone architecture consisting of: (1) a multi-scale electromagnetic scattering feature extraction network (MEFE-Net) based on attribute scattering center modeling, and (2) a deep convolutional network for hierarchical feature learning. An adaptive feature fusion module (AFFM) dynamically combines these complementary features, which are subsequently processed by a dual-scenario feature augmentation network (DeFAN) to suppress background interference in coastal areas, while the class imbalance issue is alleviated by increasing positive samples during training. Experimental results on the SAR ship detection dataset (SSDD) and high-resolution SAR images dataset (HRSID) demonstrate that the MF-DETR is effective, achieving superior performance and more accurate detection results under complex conditions.

title: A Systematic Literature Review on the Application of BERT-Based Topic Modeling and Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis in Tourism Review Texts
authors: Olivia Olita Olga, Aain Eka Karyawati
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Tourism constitutes a strategic sector with substantial contributions to economic development, cultural sustainability, and the formation of public perception, while the proliferation of digital technologies and the ubiquity of online reviews have transformed the ways in which tourist experiences are expressed and assessed. Online review data increasingly function as critical sources for monitoring tourist satisfaction, identifying emerging issues, and tracing dynamic trends in tourism demand. In response, recent scholarly work has increasingly employed Natural Language Processing (NLP) methodologies, with particular emphasis on BERT-based architectures and BERTopic for topic modeling and aspect-based sentiment analysis (ABSA). A systematic review of seventeen indexed international journal articles published between 2021 and 2025 indicates a pronounced research trend toward the utilization of contextual embeddings and dynamic topic modeling techniques to extract nuanced insights from large-scale review corpora. Despite these methodological advances, however, relatively few studies explicitly interrogate the comparative dimensions of cultural and modern tourism preferences, especially in the Indonesian context, where heritage-based attractions and contemporary tourism offerings intersect as critical elements of the industry. This review underscores the existence of this research gap and advances the proposition that future studies may benefit from integrating IndoBERT and BERTopic to model locally grounded tourist preferences, thereby supporting data-driven strategies for sustainable tourism development.

title: Safety-Aware UAV Traffic Management for Low-Altitude Urban Airspace
authors: Yian Zhu, Ziye Jia, Yao Wu, Yuanhao Cui, Qiuming Zhu, Qihui Wu
abstract: This work presents a safety-aware uncrewed aerial vehicle (UAV) traffic management framework that jointly optimizing the airspace capacity and surveillance communication quality during the path pathing. In contrast to conventional schemes that decouple strategic planning from tactical collision avoidance or assume full prior knowledge of all flight requests, our approach explicitly formulates airspace congestion and communication reliability as hard constraints for online trajectory generation under dynamic task arrivals. Specifically, we construct a predictive occupancy map to forecast spatiotemporal resource availability. On this basis, we develop a multi-objective spatiotemporal A* planner that navigates the trade-offs between flight efficiency, safety margins, and expected signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR). Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves a 24.34% improvement in average SINR and reduces the average airspace load by 40.91% compared to standard baselines, while sustaining a 88.39% task completion rate under high-density dynamic traffic conditions.

title: Line-Start Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors Stator Fault Diagnostics Immune to Unbalanced Supply Voltages
authors: D. S. B. Fonseca, Hugo R. P. Antunes, Antonio J. Marques Cardoso
abstract: This paper presents an inter-turn short-circuit fault diagnostic technique for three-phase line-start permanent magnet synchronous motors immune to the supply conditions. More traditional fault diagnostic methodologies are unable to fully address the diagnosis of stator inter-turn short-circuit faults in three-phase motors under unbalanced supply voltage conditions or high resistive connections between the supply and the motor, since the most traditional indicators are similarly impacted. The proposed technique relies on the spectral analysis of specific harmonics (related to the intermodulation phenomena between rotor slotting and stator time harmonics), presented in the alpha-beta plane modulus of the motor current, explored in a new way. The severity factor is determined resorting to the Parseval's theorem (calculation of the total energy of the analyzed harmonics). Experimental results, considering different load torques, unbalanced supply voltage conditions, high resistive connections, and fault severity levels, show that inter-turn short-circuit fault detection can be fully achieved under unbalanced supply conditions.

title: Multi-Level GAA FeFET by Multi-Ferroelectric/Dielectric Gate Stacks
authors: Wei-Jen Chen, Tao Chou, Ching-Wang Yao, Bo-Wei Huang, Min-Kuan Lin, Yun-Wen, Chen, Yu-Rui Chen, Jhih-Yuan Liang, C. W. Liu
abstract: The 4-level threshold voltage (V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</sub>) and a large memory window of 3V are demonstrated by the Si nanosheet (NS) gate-all-around (GAA) FeFET using multi-ferroelectric (FE)/dielectric (DE) stacks with minimal Si/HZO interfacial layer (IL) and 400oC thermal budget. The three HZO layers are separated by two AlO layers with native oxide removal on Si to favor o-phase HZO growth and to minimize IL for reducing program voltage. The AlO layers trap electrons and pin polarization during program operation. The 4-level V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</sub> operation is achieved through layer-by-layer electron detrapping from AlO and polarization depinning at erase operation, confirmed by TCAD simulation. The experimental fast write time of 20 ns, and high endurance >1x10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">8</sup> cycles are demonstrated with negligible read disturbance for 4-level V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</sub>.

title: RISC-V Proton Reliability Assessment using Redundancy and Optimization Techniques on Bare Metal and Linux
authors: S. Cuenca-Asensi, E. Abma-Romero, M. IJzelendoorn, P. Martínez-Olgado, A. Serrano-Cases, A. Martínez-Álvarez
abstract: This paper presents a comparative evaluation of traditional software-based fault tolerance methods, Triple Modular Redundancy (TMR) and Duplication with Re-execution (DWR), on a commercial RISC-V System-on-Chip under proton irradiation. Their effectiveness is assessed across bare-metal and lightweight Linux environments using multiple versions of a matrix multiplication benchmark to explore the interaction between cache optimization, software redundancy, and operating system complexity. Proton beam experiments conducted on the Allwinner D1-H SoC reveal three key findings. First, cache-aware programming achieves SDC reductions of 1.25–1.39× without redundancy, through a combination of execution acceleration and cache data replacement. Second, when combined with software redundancy, cache-aware implementations reduce SDC cross-sections by exceeding two orders of magnitude, while also yielding the best-performing configurations in terms of useful work accomplished between failures (MWTF). Third, lightweight Linux exhibits configuration-dependent reliability with respect to critical SEFIs, with the maximum degradation remaining 3–4× lower than the penalties reported for full-featured Linux distributions. Overall, the results establish that both cache-aware programming and lightweight operating system configurations provide measurable reliability benefits that persist even when software redundancy techniques are deployed.

title: Challenges of Implementing AI Technologies in Traditional Educational Settings
authors: Yohanes Adi Saputra Abraham, Evander Banjarnahor, Sintika Agustina Sembiring, Deborah Angelina Geovani Sihotang, Tia Aulia Putri Sadeva
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: This study investigates the challenges of integrating Artificial Intelligence (AI) into traditional educational environments by systematically reviewing 31 studies published between 2020 and 2025. The analysis identifies four primary challenge categories: pedagogical, technical, ethical, and infrastructural. Pedagogical challenges were the most frequently reported (65%), with issues related to student engagement and the learning process being central concerns. Technical challenges (61%), including difficulties with system integration, compatibility with existing platforms, and AI tool performance, also emerged as significant barriers to AI adoption in education. Ethical challenges, including data privacy and algorithmic bias, appeared in 39% of studies, while infrastructural challenges (32%) raised equity concerns, particularly in under-resourced institutions. The findings reveal the interconnected nature of these challenges. Technical issues often undermine pedagogical adoption, while infrastructure gaps exacerbate ethical and equity concerns. To address these multifaceted challenges, a holistic approach is required. This approach should include investing in reliable AI technologies, providing comprehensive teacher training, and establishing clear ethical guidelines to mitigate bias. Furthermore, collaborations with external stakeholders such as government entities and technology companies are essential to address resource gaps in educational settings. Educators must be well-equipped to integrate AI into traditional teaching practices. Proactively addressing these challenges can improve educational experiences and learning outcomes, benefiting both students and educators and promoting equity in AI access across institutions.

title: Visual Image Analysis for Predicting Hospital Food Leftovers Percentage Using Random Forest Regressor
authors: Ahmad Jawahirul Islami, Yuita Arum Sari, Muh. Arif Rahman
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Monitoring hospital food consumption is an important component of nutritional care and food service management. Standard methods such as direct observation and weighed records remain the reference standard but they take a lot of work, subjective, and difficult to apply consistently in large scale monitoring. The proposed approach used a Random Forest Regressor trained on visual features that combine color, texture, and area information extracted from paired before and after meal images. The experiment was conducted using the LeFood-Set dataset, which contains 524 pairs of hospital meal images along with corresponding weight annotations. Two experimental scenarios are implemented: raw images and segmented images, where segmentation isolates the food region to enhance the relevance of extracted features. Experimental results show that the model trained on segmented images achieved better performance, with MAE = 11.35, MSE = 239.87, and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.834, outperforming the raw-image model (MAE = 17.43, MSE = 439.13, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.697). Feature importance analysis further shows that area and texture based features contribute most to prediction accuracy. This shows that segmentation not only improves precision but also increases model interpretability. These results confirm that integrating computer vision and machine learning provides a reliable framework for objective nutrition monitoring and future real-time food waste assessment in hospital settings.

title: Failure Detection in Image Segmentation under Conditions of Semantic and Covariate Shifts
authors: Yijun Liu, Jinghua Wang, Zhuotao Tian, Hang Zhao, Zipeng Zhu, Siqi Luo, Jingyong Su
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Whether deep neural networks can provide reliable confidence is of great significance, especially in risk-sensitive scenarios. This work explores the impact of covariate and semantic shifts on segmentation tasks, an area which has not been extensively studied. Covariate shift refers to changes in the data distribution without alterations in the label space, while semantic shift involves changes in both data distribution and label space. We find that model-unknown distributional shifts in test data can transform an overconfidence problem into a situation of making random predictions with arbitrary confidence. The paper proposes a novel approach for effective failure detection that combines holistic image-level analysis and detailed pixel-level information. This approach involves the use of a Gray Level Co-occurrence Matrix (GLCM) to analyze the prediction randomness between adjacent pixels and a Magnitude-Direction Confidence Score Function (MD-CSF) for determining pixel acceptance or rejection. Furthermore, we introduce a new benchmark dataset, the Robot Inspection dataset for Semantic and Covariate shift in Segmentation (RISKS, the homophone of RISCS), to fill the need for datasets capable of evaluating the simultaneous impact of semantic and covariate shifts. Experimental results demonstrate that our method successfully detects image-level failures in segmentation, with MD-CSF outperforming other pluggable CSFs. The code and RISKS dataset will be available at https://github.com/liuyijungoon/MD-CSF.

title: CDIR: LoRA-Inspired Attention for Efficient Composite Degradation Image Restoration
authors: Yuning Cui, Wenqi Ren, Boxin Shi, Jianhou Gan, Alois Knoll
abstract: Specialized image restoration methods have been extensively explored, each targeting a specific type of degradation. However, real-world images often suffer from composite degradations, prompting growing interest in unified restoration approaches. While recent unified models have shown promising results, many are hindered by high computational complexity, limiting their deployment in resource-constrained settings. Motivated by the parameter-efficient design of Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), we propose an efficient attention module specifically designed for composite degradation image restoration. The proposed method adopts a dual-branch architecture, where one branch processes features at full resolution, and the other operates with reduced spatial and channel dimensions to improve efficiency. To better adapt to diverse degradation patterns, the latter branch is further divided into two sub-branches, each incorporating dynamic operations guided by local and contextual priors. These context priors are iteratively updated within each module, drawing inspiration from feedback mechanisms in reinforcement learning, thereby enabling the model to effectively perceive and handle multiple degradation types within a unified structure. Additionally, we introduce a multi-scale feed-forward network to further enhance both performance and computational efficiency. Extensive experiments on two composite degradation benchmarks demonstrate that our proposed network, CDIR, achieves state-of-the-art performance with significantly reduced complexity and fast inference speed. In addition, CDIR shows strong adaptability to various task-specific image restoration scenarios, such as dehazing, desnowing, and deraining. It also performs robustly on domain-specific applications such as ultra-high-definition (UHD), remote sensing, and medical image restoration, highlighting its versatility and practical applicability.

title: The role of Artificial Intelligence in supply chain risk management: a literature review and a conceptual framework
authors: Fu Jia, Shoufeng Hu, Qin He, Lujie Chen, Xingjie Xing
abstract: The integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology in Supply Chain Risk Management (SCRM) has markedly strengthened supply chain resilience, particularly in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic. As AI applications in SCRM continue to expand, there is a need to conduct a bibliometric analysis that yields rigorous and objective insights into its current research landscape. This study adopts a bibliometric systematic literature review (B-SLR) approach, utilizing the Citespace tool to systematically analyze 303 articles related to AI in SCRM, complemented by an in-depth content analysis of 98 carefully selected studies. This research offers a comprehensive overview of the current status and evolving trends of AI adoption within SCRM, identifying three principal themes emerging from the literature. Additionally, based on identified research hotspots and content analysis, the study proposes four promising directions for future research. By systematically reviewing the literature on the application of AI in SCRM, we clarify the research trajectory of AI applications in SCRM and develop an integrated conceptual framework, thereby making a significant contribution to the engineering management literature.

title: ISO/IEC/IEEE International Standard - Systems and software engineering -- Software life cycle processes
authors: N/A
abstract: This document establishes a common process framework for describing the full life cycle of software systems from conception through retirement. It applies to the acquisition or development of software products and services whether performed internally or externally to an organization. Those aspects of software system definition needed to provide the context for software products and services are included. The document also supports the definition, control, assessment, and improvement of these processes. These processes can be applied concurrently, iteratively, and recursively to a software system and its elements throughout the life cycle of a software system. Users of the document can apply the processes and terminology to construct a suitable life cycle model, composed of stages that use selected processes. The process purposes and outcomes in ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 (systems life cycle processes) and this document are fully aligned so that one may select appropriate activities from either document for use in systems with substantial software content.

title: Dual-Domain Deep Learning-Assisted NOMA-CSK Systems for Secure and Efficient Vehicular Communications
authors: Tingting Huang, Jundong Chen, Huanqiang Zeng, Guofa Cai, Georges Kaddoum
abstract: Ensuring secure and efficient multi-user (MU) transmission is critical for vehicular communication systems. Chaos-based modulation schemes have garnered considerable interest owing to their inherent advantages in physical layer security. However, most existing MU chaotic communication systems, particularly those based on non-coherent detection, suffer from low spectral efficiency due to reference signal overhead and limited user connectivity under orthogonal multiple access (OMA). Although non-orthogonal schemes such as sparse code multiple access (SCMA)-based differential chaos shift keying (DCSK) have been explored, they incur high computational complexity and exhibit inflexible scalability owing to fixed codebook designs. This paper proposes a deep learning-assisted power-domain non-orthogonal multiple access chaos shift keying (DL-NOMA-CSK) system for vehicular communications. A deep neural network (DNN)-based demodulator is designed to learn the intrinsic characteristics of chaotic signals during offline training, thereby eliminating the need for chaotic synchronization or reference signal transmission. The demodulator employs a dual-domain feature extraction architecture that jointly processes time-domain and frequency-domain information of the received chaotic signals, enhancing feature learning under dynamic channel conditions. The DNN is integrated into a successive interference cancellation (SIC) framework to mitigate error propagation. Theoretical analysis and extensive simulations demonstrate that the proposed system achieves superior performance in terms of spectral efficiency (SE), energy efficiency (EE), bit error rate (BER), security, and robustness compared to conventional MU-DCSK and existing deep learning-aided schemes. These advantages confirm the practical viability of the proposed system for secure vehicular communications.

title: Integration of Waste Management and Decommissioning in European DEMO Design
authors: Sandrine Rosanvallon, Joëlle Elbez-Uzan, Priti Kanth, Vesa Laitinen, Tomi Pakarinen, Christian Zambellini
abstract: The objective of European DEMO is to demonstrate the production of net electricity, the feasibility of operation with a closed tritium fuel cycle and the introduction of maintenance systems capable of achieving adequate plant availability, while demonstrating the safety and environmental advantages of fusion power over fission designs. In this perspective, a coherent strategy for the management of the waste generated by the reactor must be part of the design justification for European DEMO. This strategy aims to minimize the amount and the severity of the radioactive waste produced (i.e., minimizing the long-term geological disposal of waste). Although the European DEMO site has not yet been selected and local regulations and waste acceptance criteria (WAC) cannot yet be considered, these aspects are being incorporated into the design from the earliest stages. Based on the guidelines from the waste management and decommissioning exercises, preconceptual design studies of the hot cell facility have started.

title: A 1.25 pJ/Bit 73 Gb/s/ch Wireless Thru-Silicon Interface for 3-D Integration Using Terahertz Near-Field Coupling
authors: Chen Jiang, Xiaohan Shen, Xiaodi Feng, Chixiao Chen, Qi Liu, Ming Liu, Ningsheng Xu
abstract: The escalating demands of high-performance computing (HPC) and artificial intelligence (AI) drive the adoption of chiplet technologies, positioning 3-D integration as a key enabler for performance scaling. However, conventional 3-D integration schemes based on through-silicon vias (TSVs) face significant challenges, including high fabrication complexity, yield degradation, and thermal reliability issues. This article presents a novel high-speed terahertz (THz) through-silicon interface for cost-effective 3-D integration. By eliminating TSVs, the proposed architecture substantially simplifies fabrication, reduces manufacturing costs, and improves system flexibility. Leveraging near-field THz coupling, the interface achieves high bandwidth and a compact form factor while maintaining competitive energy efficiency and interconnect density. A prototype with two independent data links was implemented in a standard 28-nm CMOS process. The QPSK link demonstrates a data rate of 73 Gb/s with an energy efficiency of 1.25 pJ/bit, while the PAM4 link achieves 71.4 Gb/s at 1.22 pJ/bit.

title: A 0.4-V 988-nW Tiny Footprint Time-Domain Audio Feature Extraction ASIC for Keyword Spotting Using Injection-Locked Oscillators
authors: Ali Mostafa, Emmanuel Hardy, Franck Badets
abstract: This work presents an injection-locked oscillator (ILO)-based feature extraction (FEx) system. It combines voltage- and time-domain signal processing to implement a power-efficient programmable gain amplifier (PGA) and a small-footprint, high-selectivity ILO-based voltage-to-time converter bandpass filter (VTC-BPF) bank and rectifier. The VTC-BPF enables direct analog-to-time conversion, eliminating the need for a separate VTC stage, and allows compact full-wave rectification via an <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">xor</small> gate. Fabricated in 65-nm CMOS, the prototype occupies 0.15mm, 3.5 smaller than recent designs, while consuming 988nW, making it 9 more power-efficient than existing fully time-domain solution. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first silicon-verified analog FEx operating at 0.4V, achieving 86.3% accuracy on a 12-class keyword spotting (KWS) task.

title: Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis of Indonesian YouTube Comments on Electric and Hybrid Vehicles Using IndoBERT and IndoRoBERTa
authors: Gisela Aveline Canavara, Herry Irawan
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: The global shift toward sustainable mobility has increased the adoption of Electric Vehicles (EVs) and Hybrid Vehicles (HVs) as alternatives to conventional fuel-based cars. In Indonesia, the government promotes this transition through tax incentives, purchase subsidies, and the expansion of charging infrastructure. Yet, public adoption remains limited due to concerns about cost, battery durability, and charging accessibility. This study applies Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis (ABSA) using IndoBERT and IndoRoBERTa to analyze 37,407 Indonesian YouTube comments discussing EVs and HVs. After preprocessing and annotation, comments were categorized into predefined aspects—Performance and Driving, Cost and Economic Value, Features and Design, Battery and Charging System for EVs, and Hybrid System Component for HVs-and assigned a sentiment polarity label. Both models were fine-tuned and assessed using macro-averaged Precision, Recall, and F1-Score. IndoRoBERTa achieved the best performance, obtaining an F1-Score of 0.3319 on the HV dataset, indicating a stronger ability to interpret informal Indonesian expressions. Overall, comments about HVs reflected a higher proportion of positive sentiment (73.28%) than EVs (66.69%). Toyota and Honda were the most frequently mentioned hybrid brands, while Wuling and Hyundai dominated EV discussions. These results suggest that Indonesian users perceive hybrid vehicles as more practical and economically accessible. This study advances Indonesian-language ABSA research and provides insights to support policymakers and the automotive sector in strengthening sustainable mobility strategies.

title: Systematic Literatur Review - Classification of Urgency Levels of Public Complaints Using Semantic Analysis and Name Entity Recognition (NER) Approaches
authors: I Kadek Aldy Oka Ardita, AAIN Eka Karyawati
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: Increase in complaint volume the public in the digital era demands system classification automatic For identify report emergency in a way efficient . Research This aim for map landscape methodology classification urgency complaint public through Review Literature Systematic, with analyze relevant studies for identify approaches, challenges, and gaps research. Review process systematic This filter and analyze various research that focuses on the application of Natural Language Processing (NLP) to classification text complaint . The result show shift clear paradigm from method statistics traditional to Transformer-based deep learning models, such as BERT, which have been proven more superior in understand context semantics. Challenge main identified in a way consistent throughout literature is handling of unauthorized data balanced, complexity linguistics, and informality text from social media. Contribution main from review This is identification gap significant research, including need urge for development definition greater urgency dynamic and adaptive as well as importance integration temporal analysis for track escalation incident. Implications practical from findings This is give guide for institution public in select and apply the most appropriate technology for triage complaints, so that can increase time response to situation emergency in a way more effective.

title: Evaluation of Data Governance Frameworks for Trustworthy AI in Healthcare
authors: Chitiz Tayal
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Geoinformatics (ICDSG)
abstract: The evolution of Artificial intelligence (AI) has opened a new window for the public institutions to adopt automated work-process. Healthcare sectors have been facing consequences of digitalization due to their inclination over modern technologies for efficient handling of vast amounts of data that are being generated from healthcare systems comprising images, texts and administration reports. AI-driven solutions are beneficial for health systems in prediction of probable consequences and automation of work-process but this comes along with challenges in patient data security and trustworthiness of the automation models. The study provides an evaluation on the implementation of statistical analysis to determine how data governance practices are carried out in healthcare AI. The dataset used for performing the statistical analysis has been developed by collecting information across hospitals in the UK signifying realistic patient data. However, the name of the hospitals has been kept anonymous due to ethical and privacy concerns. Due to the fact that AI has occupied a prioritised position in the field of healthcare, it becomes necessary to develop concern on transparency and trust. The statistical analysis has been carried out using MS Excel to undergo analytics of datasets collected from Electronic Health Records (EHRs) and clinical data repositories . These analytical processes include the use of descriptive statistics to gain insights into samples and their impact on the data. Furthermore, t-tests have been carried out using ANOVA and Pivot tables and carts such that visualisation of the data can be done in terms of their quality testing. Findings from the study indicates that the use of data governance helps in protecting privacy and confidentiality of patient records stored across institutions offering services through AI.

title: EG-CMFNet: An Edge-Guided Cross-Modal Fusion Network for Remote Sensing Semantic Segmentation
authors: Jinqi Zhao, Zhonghuai Zhou, Liansong Zhang, Linxin Wang, Zhong Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Multimodal remote sensing data often exhibit complementary information about ground objects and are widely utilized for semantic segmentation tasks. However, spatial heterogeneity and misalignment from different modalities hinder the utilization of multimodal information. To address these challenges, an edge-guided cross-modal fusion network (EG-CMFNet) is proposed for multimodal remote sensing imagery. We introduce a multimodal adaptive feature interaction (MAFI) module to adaptively interact with modal information through dual-stage spatial–channel interactions. Furthermore, during the feature fusion stage, a lightweight feature fusion (LFF) module suppresses unimodal noise to achieve efficient fusion of multimodal complementary information. Additionally, an edge auxiliary (EA) module is designed to extract boundary features precisely, mitigating confusion between semantic and edge information. Comparative experiments demonstrate that EG-CMFNet achieves state-of-the-art accuracy and robustness across three datasets of varying scales, including WHU-OPT-SAR, Dongying, and Pohang datasets. Specifically, EG-CMFNet achieves the highest mean intersection over union of 56.05%, 94.68%, and 97.51%, as well as overall accuracy (OA) of 84.47%, 94.97%, and 97.72%, respectively. The source code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/masurq/EG-CMFNet</uri>
